Logic_Trace: Class 41 includes "entertainment; sporting and cultural activities." Dance hall services are a clear form of entertainment and cultural activity, directly aligning with this class.

Purpose: Providing financing for hire-purchase agreements
Expanded_Name: Financial services relating to hire-purchase
Logic_Trace: Class 36 covers "Financial, monetary and banking services." Hire-purchase financing is a specific type of financial service, thus fitting directly into this class.

Original_Term: Bags specially adapted for sports equipment
Purpose: Being specially adapted for carrying sports equipment
Expanded_Name: Carrying bags specially adapted for sports equipment
Logic_Trace: Class 28 includes "gymnastic and sporting articles." Bags specially adapted for sports equipment are considered accessories for sporting articles, which are classified in this class.

Purpose: Being preserved by freezing for consumption
Expanded_Name: Edible meat products that are frozen
Logic_Trace: Class 29 explicitly lists "Meat, fish, poultry and game" and mentions "preserved, frozen, dried and cooked fruits and vegetables." Frozen meat directly aligns with the 'meat' category and the concept of 'frozen' food within this class.

Original_Term: Rinsing aids for use when washing clothes
Purpose: Assisting the rinsing process when washing clothes
Expanded_Name: Chemical preparations in the nature of rinsing aids for use when washing clothes
Logic_Trace: Class 3 includes "bleaching preparations and other substances for laundry use; cleaning, polishing, scouring and abrasive preparations." Rinsing aids are substances for laundry use, fitting well within this class.

Purpose: Being consumed as a drink
Expanded_Name: Non-alcoholic beverages, namely, waters
Logic_Trace: Class 32 explicitly lists "mineral and aerated waters" and "non-alcoholic beverages." 'Waters' in this context refers to beverages and fits perfectly within this class.

Purpose: Being used as poles for skiing
Expanded_Name: Sporting articles, namely, ski sticks
Logic_Trace: Class 28 includes "gymnastic and sporting articles." Ski sticks are a common and essential type of sporting article for skiing, fitting perfectly within this class.

Purpose: Covering windows or openings and made of plastic
Expanded_Name: Household articles, namely, curtains made of plastic
Logic_Trace: Class 24 explicitly states "curtains of textile or plastic." The term directly matches the classification description for this class.

Purpose: Correcting vision or for cosmetic use by being worn on the eye
Expanded_Name: Optical apparatus, namely, contact lenses
Logic_Trace: Class 9 includes "optical apparatus and instruments." Contact lenses are optical devices used for vision correction or cosmetic purposes, fitting squarely within this category.

Purpose: Holding and organizing keys, made of leather
Expanded_Name: Small carrying cases made of leather for holding keys
Logic_Trace: Class 18 covers "Leather and imitations of leather; luggage and carrying bags." A key case made of leather is a type of small carrying case, consistent with this class.

Purpose: Being used as pet litter, having an aromatic property
Expanded_Name: Aromatic granular material for use as pet litter
Logic_Trace: Class 31 includes "foodstuffs and beverages for animals." While not food, pet litter is an essential animal care product commonly classified within this class, similar to other non-food preparations for animals.

Purpose: Forming a flexible membrane within a pump
Expanded_Name: Mechanical components, namely, diaphragms for pumps
Logic_Trace: Class 7 covers "Machines, machine tools, power-operated tools... machine coupling and transmission components." Pump diaphragms are components of pumps, which are machines, thus aligning with this class.

Purpose: Serving and drinking wine
Expanded_Name: Articles of glassware, namely, wine glasses
Logic_Trace: Class 21 explicitly lists "glassware, porcelain and earthenware" and "tableware." Wine glasses are a direct example of glassware and tableware, fitting perfectly within this class.

Purpose: Being used as a raw material or in various metallic forms
Expanded_Name: Aluminium in raw or semi-worked form
Logic_Trace: Class 6 begins with "Common metals and their alloys, ores." Aluminium is a common metal, fitting directly into this category.

Purpose: Functioning as rings within the structure of an umbrella
Expanded_Name: Component parts for umbrellas, namely, umbrella rings
Logic_Trace: Class 18 includes "umbrellas and parasols." Umbrella rings are component parts of umbrellas and are therefore classified with the main item in this class.

Nature: Structural components for umbrellas and parasols
Purpose: Supporting the canopy of umbrellas and parasols
Expanded_Name: Structural components for umbrellas and parasols, namely, ribs
Logic_Trace: Class 18 includes "umbrellas and parasols." Umbrella or parasol ribs are essential structural components of these items and are classified accordingly within this class.

Purpose: Destroying harmful microorganisms and sanitizing surfaces
Expanded_Name: Chemical preparations in the nature of disinfectants
Logic_Trace: Class 5 explicitly lists "disinfectants." The term is a direct match for the class description.

Purpose: Being fresh, unprocessed nuts for consumption
Expanded_Name: Raw agricultural produce, namely, fresh nuts
Logic_Trace: Class 31 includes "Raw and unprocessed agricultural... products" and "fresh fruits and vegetables, fresh herbs." Fresh nuts are unprocessed agricultural products, fitting perfectly within this class.

Purpose: Holding small items for personal carrying
Expanded_Name: Small carrying bags, namely, pouches
Logic_Trace: Class 18 includes "luggage and carrying bags." Pouches are a type of small carrying bag or container, consistent with this class.

Purpose: Holding and storing pins and needles
Expanded_Name: Haberdashery items, namely, pin cushions
Logic_Trace: Class 26 includes "pins and needles" and "haberdashery ribbons and bows." Pin cushions are common haberdashery items used for holding pins and needles, fitting within this class.

Purpose: Covering tables for decorative or protective purposes
Expanded_Name: Household linen, namely, table covers
Logic_Trace: Class 24 includes "Textiles and substitutes for textiles; household linen." Table covers are a quintessential item of household linen, fitting perfectly within this class.

Purpose: Being worn as a decorative or functional front part of a shirt
Expanded_Name: Clothing accessories, namely, shirt fronts
Logic_Trace: Class 25 covers "Clothing, footwear, headwear." Shirt fronts are considered articles of clothing or clothing accessories, fitting within this class.

Purpose: Providing shape and padding to the shoulders of clothing
Expanded_Name: Haberdashery articles, namely, shoulder pads for clothing
Logic_Trace: Class 26 includes "haberdashery ribbons and bows; buttons, hooks and eyes, pins and needles." Shoulder pads are commonly classified as haberdashery or sewing notions, used in the tailoring and construction of clothing, aligning with this class.

Purpose: Distributing electrical power to multiple devices or circuits
Expanded_Name: Electrical apparatus for distributing power
Logic_Trace: Class 9 explicitly mentions "apparatus and instruments for conducting, switching, transforming, accumulating, regulating or controlling the distribution or use of electricity." A power distributor directly matches "apparatus... for... controlling the distribution or use of electricity" and is consistent with this class.

Purpose: Generating heat and steam for power production
Expanded_Name: Apparatus for generating heat and steam, namely, nuclear reactors
Logic_Trace: The class description mentions "Apparatus and installations for lighting, heating, cooling, steam generating...". Nuclear reactors are primarily used for generating heat, which in turn produces steam for electricity. This aligns perfectly with the 'heating' and 'steam generating' aspects of Class 11. The other brainstormed meanings (research, fictional weapon) are not directly covered by Class 11's scope of utility apparatus.

Purpose: Providing nourishment and being used in food preparation
Expanded_Name: Oils and fats for human consumption
Logic_Trace: The class description explicitly lists "oils and fats for food". This directly confirms the meaning as substances suitable for human consumption and as cooking ingredients. Dietary supplements might fall into Class 5.

Purpose: Forming parts of harness and saddlery for animals
Expanded_Name: Leather straps for saddlery
Logic_Trace: The term explicitly states "[saddlery]", and the class description includes "whips, harness and saddlery". This unequivocally points to leather straps used in equestrian equipment. Decorative fashion accessories would be Class 25, and industrial strapping might be Class 22 or other classes depending on material/purpose.

Purpose: For human consumption as a drink
Expanded_Name: Alcoholic beer
Logic_Trace: The class description explicitly lists "Beers". This directly confirms the meaning as an alcoholic beverage for consumption. Ingredients would typically be Class 1 or, if processed for beverages, sometimes Class 32 (like syrups). Informal terms are not relevant to IP classification.

Purpose: Being mixed with water or other liquids to create non-alcoholic beverages
Expanded_Name: Non-alcoholic concentrates for beverage preparation
Logic_Trace: The class description explicitly mentions "syrups and other preparations for making non-alcoholic beverages". This directly matches the first brainstormed meaning of concentrated mixtures for drink preparation. Flavoring extracts could be Class 30 (food flavorings) or 32 if specifically packaged for non-alcoholic beverage making. Raw fruit pulp would typically be Class 29 or 31. The description is broad enough to cover concentrates for beverages.

Purpose: Preventing backflow in fluid systems within machines
Expanded_Name: Mechanical clack valves being parts of machines
Logic_Trace: The term explicitly states "[parts of machines]", and the class description for Class 7 is for "Machines, machine tools... motors and engines... machine coupling and transmission components". This confirms that these are mechanical valves functioning as components within machinery, designed to prevent backflow. Valves for plumbing systems would typically be Class 11. Figurative use is irrelevant.

Purpose: Transporting and storing materials in bulk
Expanded_Name: Sacks for bulk transport and storage of materials
Logic_Trace: The class description explicitly mentions "sacks for the transport and storage of materials in bulk". This directly matches the first brainstormed meaning. Small pouches for personal items would typically be Class 18 (luggage/carrying bags) or Class 25 (if part of clothing). Figurative use is irrelevant.

Purpose: Initiating the operation of motors and engines
Expanded_Name: Mechanical and electrical starters for motors and engines
Logic_Trace: The class description includes "motors and engines" and general "machines". Starters are crucial components for initiating the operation of these. Food starters are Class 29 or 30. Introductory courses are services, typically Class 41.

Purpose: Facilitating the buying and selling of stocks and bonds for clients
Expanded_Name: Financial brokerage services for stocks and bonds
Logic_Trace: The class description is "Financial, monetary and banking services". Brokerage, by its nature, is a financial service that directly involves facilitating transactions of stocks and bonds. Software would be Class 9. Educational services would be Class 41.

Purpose: Forming the main external and internal structure of vehicles
Expanded_Name: Structural bodies for land, air, or water vehicles
Logic_Trace: The class description is "Vehicles; apparatus for locomotion by land, air or water". Vehicle bodies are essential structural parts of vehicles. Human bodies are not goods for classification. Figurative meaning is irrelevant.

Purpose: Cleaning dishes in automatic dishwashers
Expanded_Name: Chemical cleaning powder for dishwashers
Logic_Trace: The class description includes "cleaning, polishing, scouring and abrasive preparations". Dishwasher powder clearly falls under cleaning preparations. While it is for automatic dishwashers, the general cleaning aspect fits. Powder for hand-washing dishes would also be Class 3. An ingredient would typically be Class 1.

Purpose: Eliminating or neutralizing unpleasant odors in the air for sanitary or health purposes
Expanded_Name: Chemical preparations for deodorizing the air, for sanitary purposes
Logic_Trace: The class description includes "sanitary preparations for medical purposes" and "disinfectants". While typical air fresheners are often Class 3, the context of Class 5 suggests preparations with a more functional, possibly sanitizing or health-related, effect beyond just masking odors. This aligns with eliminating odors for sanitary or health reasons.

Original_Term: Sighting mirrors for guns and rifles
Purpose: Assisting in aiming guns and rifles
Expanded_Name: Optical sighting mirrors for firearms, specifically guns and rifles
Logic_Trace: The class description is "Firearms; ammunition and projectiles; explosives; fireworks". Accessories specifically designed for firearms and used for their essential function (sighting/aiming) fall directly into this class, as they are integral to the use of firearms. Decorative mirrors would be Class 20. Inspection mirrors might be Class 9.

Purpose: For human consumption as food
Expanded_Name: Non-live crustaceans for food
Logic_Trace: The class description includes "Meat, fish, poultry and game" and "preserved, frozen, dried and cooked fruits and vegetables". The inclusion of "not live" directly implies they are processed for food. Live crustaceans would be Class 31. Biological specimens (Class 1) or decorative items (Class 20) are not food items for this class.

Purpose: For human consumption
Expanded_Name: Eggs for food
Logic_Trace: The class description explicitly lists "eggs" within a list of food products. This clearly refers to eggs for consumption. Eggs for hatching would be Class 31 (agricultural products). Decorative eggs could be Class 20.

Purpose: For human consumption as a staple food or ingredient
Expanded_Name: Starchy food product, namely sago
Logic_Trace: The class description explicitly lists "tapioca and sago" among other food items like rice, pasta, and flour. This directly confirms it as a food product. The tree would be Class 31 (raw agricultural product). A brand name is not a product classification.

Purpose: Providing controlled conditions for hatching eggs
Expanded_Name: Agricultural incubators for hatching eggs
Logic_Trace: The class description explicitly lists "incubators for eggs". This precisely matches the function of hatching eggs within the context of agricultural machinery. Medical incubators for infants would be Class 10. Laboratory incubators for cell culture could also be Class 9.

Purpose: Assessing the performance and function of diesel fuel injectors
Expanded_Name: Testing apparatus for diesel fuel injectors
Logic_Trace: The class description includes "testing, inspecting... apparatus and instruments". A diesel injector tester is clearly an instrument for testing and inspecting the performance of these engine components. Diagnostic software would be Class 9 (software), but the term is for physical 'testers'. Installation tools would likely be Class 8 (hand tools) or Class 7 (machine parts).

Original_Term: Sparking plugs for internal combustion engines
Purpose: Igniting fuel-air mixtures in internal combustion engines
Expanded_Name: Electrical sparking plugs for internal combustion engines
Logic_Trace: The class description includes "motors and engines". Sparking plugs are essential electrical components for the operation of internal combustion engines, which fall under the scope of Class 7. Decorative items are Class 20. Collectibles are not a classification of the item itself.

Purpose: Aiding in rehabilitation, therapeutic exercises, and physical recovery
Expanded_Name: Medical and therapeutic equipment for physical therapy
Logic_Trace: The class description includes "therapeutic and assistive devices adapted for persons with disabilities" and "massage apparatus". Physical therapy equipment clearly fits within the scope of therapeutic devices intended for medical or rehabilitative purposes. General fitness equipment might be Class 28. Consulting services would be Class 44.

Purpose: Cutting glass when integrated into machines
Expanded_Name: Diamond-tipped cutting tools being parts of machines for glaziers
Logic_Trace: The term explicitly states "[parts of machines]", and Class 7 is for "Machines, machine tools, power-operated tools". This confirms these are components for glass-cutting machinery. Hand tools for glaziers would be Class 8. Jewelry would be Class 14.

Purpose: Sealing fuel tanks in vehicles
Expanded_Name: Caps for fuel tanks of vehicles
Logic_Trace: The class description is "Vehicles; apparatus for locomotion by land, air or water". Caps for vehicle fuel tanks are specific parts of vehicles, essential for their function (preventing fuel evaporation and spills). Decorative caps could be Class 20 or 25 depending on material and purpose. Caps for fuel cans might be Class 20.

Purpose: Being used in industrial manufacturing, processing, or scientific applications
Expanded_Name: Chemical substances for industrial purposes
Logic_Trace: The class description explicitly states "Chemicals for use in industry, science and photography". This directly matches the primary meaning. Household cleaning chemicals are Class 3. Medical chemicals are Class 5.

Purpose: Acting as an intermediary in the arrangement of insurance policies for clients
Expanded_Name: Financial brokerage services for insurance
Logic_Trace: The class description explicitly states "insurance services". Insurance brokerage is a core insurance service, facilitating the connection between clients and insurance providers. Software would be Class 9. Educational services would be Class 41.

Purpose: Protecting wood from deterioration, decay, and pests
Expanded_Name: Chemical preparations for preserving wood
Logic_Trace: The class description explicitly lists "preservatives against rust and against deterioration of wood". This directly matches the primary function. Natural oils/waxes for finishing could also be Class 2. Tools for application would be Class 8.

Purpose: Purifying, conditioning, and decontaminating water
Expanded_Name: Services for the treatment of water
Logic_Trace: The class description explicitly states "treatment of water" under "Treatment of materials". This clearly refers to services for altering the properties of water. Chemicals for water treatment would be Class 1 or 5. Equipment for water treatment would be Class 11.

Purpose: Holding and styling hair
Expanded_Name: Non-medicated cosmetic hair spray
Logic_Trace: The class description includes "Non-medicated cosmetics and toiletry preparations". Hair spray is a quintessential cosmetic product for hair styling. Temporary hair coloring sprays might also fit Class 3, but the primary meaning is styling. Pesticide spray would be Class 5.

Purpose: Performing mathematical operations and calculations
Expanded_Name: Electronic calculating devices
Logic_Trace: The class description explicitly includes "calculating devices" and "computers and computer peripheral devices". This clearly refers to electronic calculators, whether standalone or software. Manual tools like an abacus could be Class 28 or other depending on material. Human calculators are not a product.

Purpose: Propelling watercraft by harnessing wind power
Expanded_Name: Flexible fabric sails for watercraft
Logic_Trace: The class description explicitly lists "sails" within a class containing ropes, nets, tents, and tarpaulins. This strongly implies sails for marine vessels, as these are typically made from textile or synthetic materials and are associated with ropes and nets. Parts of wind turbines would likely be Class 7. Decorative panels might be Class 24.

Purpose: Providing expert advice, analysis, and solutions regarding information technology systems and strategies
Expanded_Name: Professional consulting services in the field of information technology
Logic_Trace: The class description includes "Scientific and technological services and research and design relating thereto" and "design and development of computer hardware and software". IT consulting is a professional service providing expertise and solutions in technology, fitting well within "scientific and technological services" and often involving design and development aspects. Software would be a product (Class 9). Training courses are Class 41.

Purpose: Reading and processing bar code data
Expanded_Name: Electronic bar code scanning and processing devices
Logic_Trace: Class 9 explicitly covers "apparatus and instruments for recording, transmitting, reproducing or processing sound, images or data" and "computer peripheral devices". The interpretation as an electronic scanning device for processing data fits perfectly. Software is a possibility, but "readers" implies a physical device. A person is not a product.

Nature: Receptacles made of leather, imitation leather, or other materials
Purpose: Carrying items, luggage
Expanded_Name: Carrying bags and luggage
Logic_Trace: Class 18 explicitly lists "luggage and carrying bags". This directly confirms the first meaning. Plastic bags for packaging belong to Class 16, and medical/cosmetic meanings are irrelevant.

Purpose: Creating artworks
Expanded_Name: Drawing and artistic creation materials
Logic_Trace: Class 16 specifically mentions "drawing materials and materials for artists" and "paintbrushes". This directly supports the interpretation of supplies used for creating art. Reference books are a subset of printed matter but less direct, and raw manufacturing materials are too broad for this term in this context.

Purpose: Providing insulation and warmth as underclothing
Expanded_Name: Insulating undergarments for warmth
Logic_Trace: Class 25 is dedicated to "Clothing, footwear, headwear". Thermal underwear fits perfectly within the 'Clothing' category as garments designed for warmth. Insulating materials are raw goods, and novelty types are still garments.

Purpose: Regulating flow in infant feeding bottles
Expanded_Name: Valves for infant feeding bottles
Logic_Trace: Class 10 includes "apparatus, devices and articles for nursing infants". This directly matches components for infant feeding bottles. Industrial valves are for different classes, and while medical feeding tubes are Class 10, the term specifies 'feeding bottle' which points to infants.

Purpose: Mounting and allowing rotational movement of heavy weapons
Expanded_Name: Pivoting supports for mounting heavy weapons
Logic_Trace: Class 13 is for "Firearms; ammunition and projectiles; explosives; fireworks". The term explicitly states 'for heavy weapons', which are firearms. Therefore, pivoting supports for such weapons are directly relevant to this class. General industrial parts or medical implants are irrelevant.

Purpose: Providing air-conditioning apparatus for temporary use to facilitate air treatment
Expanded_Name: Rental services for air-conditioning apparatus for air treatment purposes
Logic_Trace: Class 40 covers "Treatment of materials; recycling of waste and trash; air purification and treatment of water". While air conditioning primarily controls temperature and humidity, it often includes filtration and thus contributes to 'air purification and treatment'. Therefore, the rental service providing the apparatus for this treatment fits within Class 40, distinguishing it from general installation (Class 37) or simply the apparatus (Class 11).

Purpose: Providing ready-to-eat or easily heated meat-based meals
Expanded_Name: Pre-cooked or ready-to-eat meat-based dishes
Logic_Trace: Class 29 includes "Meat, fish, poultry and game; meat extracts; preserved, frozen, dried and cooked fruits and vegetables". 'Prepared meat dishes' directly falls under the category of prepared food products, fitting the scope of this class. Ingredients are less specific for 'dishes', and recipes are Class 16.

Purpose: Providing ready-to-eat mixed food preparations
Expanded_Name: Pre-mixed and ready-to-eat salads
Logic_Trace: Class 29 includes "preserved, frozen, dried and cooked fruits and vegetables". 'Prepared salads' consisting of these ingredients fit perfectly as ready-to-eat food products within this class. Ingredients are too general, and salad dressings are typically Class 30.

Purpose: Providing vegetables treated for extended shelf life
Expanded_Name: Vegetables preserved by canning, freezing, drying or other methods for consumption
Logic_Trace: Class 29 explicitly lists "preserved, frozen, dried and cooked fruits and vegetables". This directly matches the product definition. Methods of preservation are not products, and containers are in other classes (e.g., Class 21 or 20).

Purpose: Making literary works available to the public
Expanded_Name: Publication services for books
Logic_Trace: Class 41 covers "Education; providing of training; entertainment; sporting and cultural activities". The publication of books is a service that disseminates cultural and educational content, fitting squarely into 'cultural activities' and 'entertainment'. The physical printing is typically Class 40, and the books themselves are Class 16.

Purpose: Securing or holding up clothing sleeves
Expanded_Name: Elastic expanding bands for holding up clothing sleeves
Logic_Trace: Class 26 covers "Lace, braid and embroidery, and haberdashery ribbons and bows; buttons, hooks and eyes, pins and needles". Expanding bands for holding sleeves are an accessory for clothing, falling under the broad category of haberdashery. Industrial or medical bands are for different classes.

Purpose: Identification, decoration, or as part of a uniform
Expanded_Name: Decorative or identification armbands
Logic_Trace: Class 26 focuses on decorative and functional haberdashery items like "ribbons and bows" and "hair decorations". Armbands used for identification or as decorative accessories fit this description. Armor (Class 9 or 13) and medical slings (Class 10) are incorrect.

Purpose: Providing the support structure for umbrellas and parasols
Expanded_Name: Structural frames for umbrellas and parasols
Logic_Trace: Class 18 explicitly lists "umbrellas and parasols". Therefore, their structural components, such as frames, belong in the same class. Picture frames are Class 20, and decorative stands would be in other classes.

Purpose: Securing hair in place or as a decorative item
Expanded_Name: Mesh coverings for securing or decorating hair
Logic_Trace: Class 26 includes "hair decorations" and "false hair". Hair nets, used for securing or decorating hair, fit well within this category. Fishing nets (Class 22) or drain stoppers (Class 21) are irrelevant.

Purpose: Enabling communication over distance via electronic means
Expanded_Name: Services for transmitting and receiving information via telecommunication networks
Logic_Trace: Class 38 is precisely defined as "Telecommunications services". The term directly matches the class description. Equipment belongs to Class 9, and installation to Class 37.

Purpose: Creating holes as parts of drilling machines or power-operated tools
Expanded_Name: Interchangeable cutting bits for drilling machines and power-operated tools
Logic_Trace: Class 7 includes "Machines, machine tools, power-operated tools". The term explicitly states '[parts of machines]'. Drilling bits are essential, replaceable cutting parts of drilling machines or power-operated tools. Digital bits (Class 9) or bridle bits (Class 18) are incorrect.

Purpose: Facilitating financial transactions without electronic data storage
Expanded_Name: Non-magnetic debit cards for financial transactions (as printed matter/stationery items)
Logic_Trace: Class 16 includes "stationery and office requisites". Debit cards 'without magnetic coding' implies they are not data carriers (Class 9) but rather physical items, like printed cards, used in conjunction with a financial service (Class 36). As non-electronic, tangible cards, they can be classified similarly to other plastic or paper stationery items. Game cards are Class 28.

Original_Term: Padlocks of metal, other than electronic
Purpose: Securing property as a portable metal lock
Expanded_Name: Mechanical metal padlocks for security purposes
Logic_Trace: Class 6 includes "small items of metal hardware" and "safes". Padlocks fit perfectly as small mechanical metal hardware for security. The exclusion 'other than electronic' confirms its placement here rather than Class 9. Safes are distinct, and ornaments are irrelevant.

Original_Term: Adhesive fabric for application by heat
Purpose: Adhering to surfaces using heat
Expanded_Name: Heat-activated adhesive fabric for textile applications
Logic_Trace: Class 24 is for "Textiles and substitutes for textiles; household linen; curtains of textile or plastic". 'Adhesive fabric' is a type of textile material, simply modified with an adhesive backing. Adhesives are Class 1, and heating apparatus are Class 7 or 11.

Purpose: Reproducing data, images, or text onto physical media
Expanded_Name: Electronic computer peripheral printers
Logic_Trace: Class 9 includes "computers and computer peripheral devices" and "apparatus and instruments for recording, transmitting, reproducing or processing sound, images or data". Printers as computer peripherals for reproducing data fit perfectly. Industrial printing machines are Class 7, and people are not products.

Purpose: Embellishing clothing with shimmering accents
Expanded_Name: Decorative spangles for clothing ornamentation
Logic_Trace: Class 26 covers "Lace, braid and embroidery, and haberdashery ribbons and bows; buttons, hooks and eyes, pins and needles". Spangles, as small decorative elements for clothing, fit well within haberdashery or decorative articles for textiles. Confetti (Class 28) or paint (Class 2) are incorrect.

Purpose: Enhancing grip and protecting hands during golf
Expanded_Name: Sporting gloves for playing golf
Logic_Trace: Class 28 includes "gymnastic and sporting articles". Golf gloves are clearly a specific type of sporting article. General purpose or fashion gloves are typically Class 25 due to the specific 'Golf' qualifier.

Purpose: Providing financial backing or support for events, organizations, or individuals
Expanded_Name: Financial support and funding services through sponsorship
Logic_Trace: Class 36 is for "Financial, monetary and banking services". Financial sponsorship, involving the provision of funds or resources in exchange for recognition, is a financial service. Advertising services are Class 35.

Purpose: Used as a base material for crafting jewelry, watches, or other precious articles
Expanded_Name: Unwrought or semi-wrought alloys containing precious metals
Logic_Trace: Class 14 explicitly lists "Precious metals and their alloys". This directly matches the definition of such mixtures as raw materials. Finished jewelry items are products made from these alloys, and industrial alloys for general manufacturing are typically Class 6.

Purpose: Consumed as an alcoholic drink
Expanded_Name: Alcoholic distilled spirit known as vodka
Logic_Trace: Class 33 is for "Alcoholic beverages, except beers; alcoholic preparations for making beverages". Vodka is a clear example of an alcoholic beverage and fits perfectly. Cocktail mixes might also be Class 33, but the term 'Vodka' is more specific. A brand name is not the product category.

Purpose: Flavoring food and culinary use
Expanded_Name: Edible salt for culinary purposes
Logic_Trace: Class 30 explicitly lists "salt, seasonings, spices, preserved herbs". This refers to salt used as a food seasoning. Road salt (Class 1) or Epsom salts (Class 5) are incorrect for this class.

Purpose: Consumed as a condiment or side dish
Expanded_Name: Vegetables preserved in vinegar or brine for consumption
Logic_Trace: Class 29 includes "preserved, frozen, dried and cooked fruits and vegetables". Pickles are a form of preserved vegetables, fitting perfectly into this category as a food product. The process of pickling is not a product, and pickleball equipment is Class 28.

Purpose: Creating and distributing instruments or digital units representing monetary worth or credit
Expanded_Name: Financial services for the issuance of tokens of monetary value
Logic_Trace: Class 36 is for "Financial, monetary and banking services". The 'Issuance of tokens of value' is a financial service, pertaining to the creation and distribution of instruments that represent monetary worth or credit. Physical tokens as products might be Class 16 if non-electronic, but the 'issuance' itself is a service. Game tokens are Class 28.

Purpose: Diagnosis, treatment, monitoring, or aid in medical, surgical, dental, or veterinary applications
Expanded_Name: Apparatus and instruments for surgical, medical, dental, and veterinary purposes
Logic_Trace: Class 10 is broadly defined as "Surgical, medical, dental and veterinary apparatus and instruments". The term directly matches this primary description of the class. Software is Class 9, and publications are Class 16.

Nature: Stationary component of a rotary machine
Purpose: Providing a static magnetic field or acting as the stationary fluid-dynamic component within a machine
Expanded_Name: Stators, being stationary components of machines
Logic_Trace: Class 7 covers 'Motors and engines' and 'Machine coupling and transmission components'. The term 'Stators [parts of machines]' directly aligns with the meaning of a stationary component within a machine, such as an engine or motor.

Purpose: Supporting workpieces or equipment during the operation of machines
Expanded_Name: Supporting tables for machines
Logic_Trace: Class 7 is for 'Machines, machine tools, power-operated tools'. 'Tables for machines' clearly refers to components designed to support workpieces or tools in conjunction with machines. This meaning is perfectly consistent with Class 7, while general furniture (Class 20) or data structures are irrelevant.

Purpose: Removing dirt, grease, and other contaminants from surfaces or fabrics
Expanded_Name: Chemical cleaning preparations, namely detergents
Logic_Trace: Class 3 specifically includes 'cleaning, polishing, scouring and abrasive preparations' and 'substances for laundry use'. All brainstormed meanings of detergents as cleaning agents align perfectly with this description.

Purpose: Designing and assembling decorative wreaths from natural or artificial materials
Expanded_Name: Horticultural services, namely wreath making
Logic_Trace: Class 44 covers 'horticulture and forestry services'. 'Wreath making' in this class refers to the service or craft of arranging natural or artificial materials into wreaths, which is a form of floristry or horticultural service. It does not refer to a physical kit or a general event outside of a service context.

Purpose: Acting as a gelling and thickening agent in food preparations, especially for jams and jellies
Expanded_Name: Pectin for culinary purposes, being a food additive used as a gelling agent
Logic_Trace: Class 29 is for food items, explicitly mentioning 'jellies, jams, compotes'. Pectin is a common ingredient used as a gelling agent for these culinary products. The term 'for culinary purposes' confirms its food-related nature.

Purpose: Providing a foodstuff for human consumption, preserved by freezing
Expanded_Name: Deep-frozen poultry for human consumption
Logic_Trace: Class 29 covers 'Meat, fish, poultry and game' and 'preserved, frozen, dried and cooked fruits and vegetables'. 'Deep-frozen poultry' clearly falls under processed meat products intended for human consumption, consistent with the class description.

Purpose: Storing and dispensing gunpowder for use with firearms
Expanded_Name: Powder horns for storing gunpowder for firearms
Logic_Trace: Class 13 is for 'Firearms; ammunition and projectiles; explosives'. Powder horns are traditional containers specifically used to hold and dispense gunpowder, which is an explosive component for firearms. This aligns directly with the class.

Purpose: Providing support, compression, or stabilization for the body during recovery or for therapeutic reasons after surgery
Expanded_Name: Corsetry for surgical purposes, being medical support garments
Logic_Trace: Class 10 includes 'orthopaedic articles' and 'surgical, medical...apparatus and instruments'. The explicit mention 'for surgical purposes' distinguishes this corsetry from fashion items (Class 25) and confirms its nature as a medical or orthopedic support garment within Class 10.

Purpose: Providing a foodstuff derived from marine life for human consumption
Expanded_Name: Seafood, not live, for human consumption
Logic_Trace: Class 29 includes 'Meat, fish, poultry and game'. The descriptor '[not live]' clarifies that this term refers to seafood prepared for consumption, fitting perfectly into this food class, unlike live animals which would be Class 31.

Purpose: Providing amusement, entertainment, and a means of recreation
Expanded_Name: Games, being recreational playthings
Logic_Trace: Class 28 explicitly lists 'Games, toys and playthings; video game apparatus'. This confirms that 'Games' in this context refers to recreational articles and apparatus for playing, including both physical and video games.

Purpose: Removing wax and finishes from floors by chemical action
Expanded_Name: Chemical scouring preparations for removing floor wax
Logic_Trace: Class 3 covers 'cleaning, polishing, scouring and abrasive preparations'. The term 'Floor wax removers [scouring preparations]' directly indicates a chemical preparation used for cleaning and scouring, which aligns precisely with the class description.

Purpose: Providing a concentrated milk product for human consumption or as an ingredient in food preparation
Expanded_Name: Condensed milk, being a dairy product
Logic_Trace: Class 29 specifically lists 'milk, cheese, butter, yogurt and other milk products'. Condensed milk is a dairy product, a processed form of milk, and therefore fits directly into this food category.

Purpose: Equipping and furnishing indoor or outdoor spaces for various functions like seating, storage, and display
Expanded_Name: Movable articles of furniture
Logic_Trace: Class 20 explicitly lists 'Furniture'. This is a direct and unambiguous match, indicating the general category of movable articles used to equip a space.

Purpose: Supplying materials, workpieces, or power to a machine or processing system
Expanded_Name: Feeders, being parts of machines for supplying materials
Logic_Trace: Class 7 covers 'Machines, machine tools, power-operated tools'. The term explicitly states '[parts of machines]', indicating that 'feeders' are components within machinery, typically for delivering materials or power. Animal feeders (Class 21) are clearly excluded by the '[parts of machines]' qualifier.

Purpose: Used for decoration, tying, trimming, or as haberdashery
Expanded_Name: Ribbons, being narrow woven fabric strips for haberdashery and decorative purposes
Logic_Trace: Class 26 explicitly mentions 'haberdashery ribbons and bows'. This directly confirms the meaning of ribbons as narrow strips of fabric used for decorative or haberdashery purposes, distinguishing them from ink ribbons (Class 2/9) or food items (Class 30).

Original_Term: Business information services provided online from a global computer network or the internet
Purpose: Delivering data, insights, and intelligence to businesses to aid in management, organization, and decision-making via global computer networks
Expanded_Name: Online business information services, provided via global computer networks or the internet
Logic_Trace: Class 35 covers 'business management, organization and administration; office functions'. Providing information vital for business operations, especially when delivered online, falls squarely within business administration and management services.

Purpose: Arranging or making available residential properties for long-term dwelling
Expanded_Name: Real estate services for the provision of permanent housing accommodation
Logic_Trace: Class 36 includes 'real estate services'. The 'Provision of permanent housing accommodation' directly relates to services involved in making residential properties available for long-term dwelling, such as real estate leasing or sales, which are core real estate services.

Purpose: Used as a binder, mortar, or additive in construction and building applications
Expanded_Name: Lime, being a non-metallic building material
Logic_Trace: Class 19 covers 'Materials, not of metal, for building and construction'. Lime (calcium oxide/hydroxide) is a widely used mineral material in construction. The citrus fruit meaning (Class 31) is clearly not applicable here.

Purpose: Providing an ignition source for cigars within an automobile
Expanded_Name: Cigar lighters specifically adapted for use in automobiles
Logic_Trace: Class 12 is for 'Vehicles; apparatus for locomotion by land, air or water'. A 'cigar lighter for automobiles' is an accessory specifically designed to be integrated into and used within a vehicle, thus functioning as a vehicle part or apparatus.

Purpose: Providing vegetables preserved through dehydration for human consumption
Expanded_Name: Dried vegetables for human consumption
Logic_Trace: Class 29 directly lists 'preserved, frozen, dried and cooked fruits and vegetables'. 'Vegetables, dried' fits this description precisely as a preserved foodstuff.

Purpose: Providing administrative and operational management for hotel properties on behalf of owners
Expanded_Name: Business management services for hotels, provided for others
Logic_Trace: Class 35 covers 'business management, organization and administration'. 'Hotel management for others' refers to the service of operating and administering a hotel business on behalf of an owner, which is a business management service. Actual hotel accommodation is Class 43.

Original_Term: Padding materials, not of rubber, plastics, paper or cardboard
Purpose: Providing stuffing, cushioning, or protective padding, excluding those made of rubber, plastics, paper, or cardboard
Expanded_Name: Padding, cushioning and stuffing materials, not of rubber, plastics, paper or cardboard
Logic_Trace: Class 22 explicitly states 'padding, cushioning and stuffing materials, except of paper, cardboard, rubber or plastics'. This definition directly matches the term and its specific exclusions, identifying fibrous materials like textiles or natural fibers.

Purpose: Used as a base material in industrial manufacturing processes to create various plastic products
Expanded_Name: Unprocessed plastics, being raw chemical materials for industrial use
Logic_Trace: Class 1 explicitly includes 'unprocessed artificial resins, unprocessed plastics'. This is a direct match, indicating the raw, primary form of plastics used in industrial processes, not finished products or semi-processed materials.

Purpose: Reducing the intensity of sound reaching the ears to prevent hearing damage or improve comfort in noisy environments
Expanded_Name: Hearing protection devices for personal safety and therapeutic purposes
Logic_Trace: Class 10 covers 'therapeutic and assistive devices adapted for persons with disabilities' and broadly medical/surgical apparatus. Hearing protection devices (e.g., earplugs, earmuffs) are considered health and safety devices to prevent hearing loss, fitting the assistive or therapeutic nature of articles in Class 10.

Purpose: Separating components of liquid mixtures through fractional distillation processes in industrial settings
Expanded_Name: Refining towers, being industrial apparatus for distillation processes
Logic_Trace: Class 11 includes 'Apparatus and installations for lighting, heating, cooling, steam generating, cooking, drying, ventilating, water supply and sanitary purposes'. Distillation involves heating and cooling processes to separate substances, making refining towers for this purpose an industrial apparatus related to these thermal and fluid processes within Class 11.

Purpose: Serving as a decorative article of jewellery worn on the head, often signifying rank or for ceremonial purposes
Expanded_Name: Diadems, being ornamental headwear and jewellery
Logic_Trace: Class 14 is for 'jewellery, precious and semi-precious stones'. Diadems are typically ornamental headwear, often made with precious metals and stones, placing them firmly within the category of jewellery.

Purpose: Used as sporting apparatus for target practice or hunting in a sporting context
Expanded_Name: Crossbows, being sporting apparatus for archery or hunting
Logic_Trace: Class 28 includes 'gymnastic and sporting articles'. The explicit qualifier '[sporting apparatus]' clarifies that crossbows in this context are for recreational or competitive sports, not for military weaponry (Class 13), which fits the class.

Purpose: Providing land locomotion, typically characterized by high performance and dynamic handling
Expanded_Name: Sports cars, being motor vehicles for land locomotion
Logic_Trace: Class 12 is for 'Vehicles; apparatus for locomotion by land, air or water'. Sports cars are a specific type of automobile designed for land locomotion, fitting directly into this vehicle class. Toy cars are Class 28.

Purpose: Sealing the combustion chamber and transferring heat from the piston to the cylinder wall within an internal combustion engine
Expanded_Name: Piston rings, being internal combustion engine parts
Logic_Trace: Class 7 covers 'motors and engines, except for land vehicles'. The term explicitly states '[internal combustion engine parts]', which directly aligns with components of motors and engines described in this class.

Purpose: Processing waste materials to recover usable substances or components for reuse
Expanded_Name: Material treatment services, namely reclamation of material from waste
Logic_Trace: Class 40 includes 'Treatment of materials; recycling of waste and trash'. 'Reclamation of material from waste' is a direct description of a service involving the processing and recycling of waste materials to recover usable substances, which is central to this class.

Purpose: Preserving fish for human consumption by freezing
Expanded_Name: Edible fish preserved by freezing
Logic_Trace: Class 29 explicitly includes "frozen fish" and covers edible animal products. This confirms the term refers to fish preserved by freezing for consumption. Meanings 2 and 3 are outside the scope of food products in this class.

Original_Term: Advisory services relating to the use of energy
Purpose: Providing expert guidance and recommendations regarding energy consumption, efficiency, and related technologies
Expanded_Name: Professional advisory services concerning the efficient and optimal use of energy
Logic_Trace: Class 42 covers "Scientific and technological services and research and design relating thereto; industrial analysis, industrial research". Advisory services concerning energy use, especially efficiency and technology, fit within this scientific and technological scope. Legal or financial advice (meanings 2 and 3) belong to other classes.

Purpose: Polishing and providing a non-slip surface for floors
Expanded_Name: Chemical preparation in the nature of non-slipping wax for polishing floors
Logic_Trace: Class 3 includes "cleaning, polishing, scouring and abrasive preparations". A non-slipping wax for floors is a preparation for polishing and protecting floors, fitting this class. Meaning 3 refers to construction materials (Class 19) and meaning 2 is too general.

Purpose: Providing support and fastening for corsets
Expanded_Name: Rigid structural components, namely busks, for corsets
Logic_Trace: Class 26 covers "Lace, braid and embroidery, and haberdashery ribbons and bows; buttons, hooks and eyes, pins and needles". Corset busks are components of corsets, fitting into the general category of haberdashery or fasteners for clothing. Meaning 3 is incorrect.

Purpose: Lifting heavy objects using a rack and pinion mechanism
Expanded_Name: Mechanical lifting apparatus, namely rack and pinion jacks
Logic_Trace: Class 7 covers "Machines, machine tools, power-operated tools". Rack and pinion jacks are mechanical lifting machines. Meaning 2 refers to land vehicles, which are excluded from motors and engines in this class, and meaning 3 is a component, not a complete tool/machine.

Purpose: Grinding grain as part of a milling machine
Expanded_Name: Heavy circular grinding components for milling machines, namely millstones
Logic_Trace: Class 7 is for "Machines, machine tools". Millstones are essential components of milling machines used for grinding grain. They are parts of machines. Meaning 2 is a metaphor, and meaning 3 is outside the scope of machines.

Purpose: Protecting the head and face from cold or wind
Expanded_Name: Close-fitting headwear, namely balaclavas
Logic_Trace: Class 25 specifically lists "Clothing, footwear, headwear". Balaclavas are a form of headwear. Meanings 2 and 3 are too specific or incorrect for the general clothing class.

Purpose: Guiding or controlling animals, particularly in equestrian activities
Expanded_Name: Flexible striking device, namely whips, for equestrian use
Logic_Trace: Class 18 explicitly lists "whips, harness and saddlery". This directly matches the primary meaning of whips as used with animals or for equestrian activities. Meanings 2 and 3 are clearly outside this class.

Purpose: Safekeeping and warehousing of furniture for others
Expanded_Name: Commercial storage services for furniture
Logic_Trace: Class 39 covers "packaging and storage of goods". "Storage of furniture" perfectly aligns with this description, indicating a commercial service. Meaning 2 describes a place, not a service. Meaning 3 is a domestic activity, not a commercial service.

Purpose: Cutting materials when integrated into sawing machines
Expanded_Name: Cutting components for sawing machines, namely saw blades
Logic_Trace: Class 7 includes "Machines, machine tools, power-operated tools" and implies components thereof. The qualifier "[parts of machines]" is key, indicating these are components of industrial or power tools, directly fitting this class. Hand saw blades (meaning 2) would be Class 8.

Purpose: Protecting, coloring, or preserving surfaces
Expanded_Name: Chemical preparations in the nature of protective and decorative coatings
Logic_Trace: Class 2 primarily deals with "Paints, varnishes, lacquers; preservatives against rust and against deterioration of wood; colorants, dyes". Coatings, in this context, refer to substances applied to surfaces for protection, decoration, or preservation, aligning with paints and varnishes. Meanings 2 and 3 refer to different classes (24 for textiles, 25 for clothing).

Purpose: Facilitating various mechanical processes such as rotation, mixing, or conveying within machines
Expanded_Name: Cylindrical machine components, namely drums, for industrial machinery
Logic_Trace: Class 7 covers "Machines, machine tools". The qualifier "[parts of machines]" explicitly states the context. Drums as machine parts (e.g., for rotating, mixing, conveying) fit this class. Musical instruments (meaning 2) are Class 15. Containers (meaning 3) are usually Class 20 or 21.

Purpose: Providing and distributing water for various uses
Expanded_Name: Apparatus and integrated systems for water supply
Logic_Trace: Class 11 explicitly includes "apparatus and installations for... water supply and sanitary purposes". This directly matches the term, referring to the physical apparatus and systems. Meaning 2 is a service (Class 39), and meaning 3 is too broad and may include elements outside of Class 11.

Purpose: Sweetening food and beverages
Expanded_Name: Crystalline foodstuff used for sweetening, namely sugar
Logic_Trace: Class 30 explicitly lists "sugar, honey, treacle" within the context of food items. This clearly refers to the edible food substance. Meanings 2 and 3 are either too generic or refer to non-food applications.

Purpose: Consuming as a confection or ingredient
Expanded_Name: Edible food product made from cacao, namely chocolate
Logic_Trace: Class 30 explicitly lists "chocolate" among food items like "bread, pastries and confectionery". This confirms it refers to the solid food product. Meaning 2 is a color, meaning 3 is a specific preparation, but the core term refers to the food item itself.

Original_Term: Retail or wholesale services for pharmaceutical, veterinary and sanitary preparations and medical supplies
Purpose: Facilitating the sale and distribution of pharmaceutical, veterinary, sanitary preparations, and medical supplies to consumers or businesses
Expanded_Name: Retail and wholesale services featuring pharmaceutical, veterinary, and sanitary preparations, and medical supplies
Logic_Trace: Class 35 covers "Advertising; business management, organization and administration; office functions", which includes retail and wholesale services. The term explicitly states "Retail or wholesale services". This clearly matches the class. Meaning 2 (the goods) would be Class 5. Meaning 3 (logistics) would be Class 39.

Purpose: Cleaning, polishing, and preserving shoes
Expanded_Name: Chemical preparation for cleaning, polishing, and preserving shoes
Logic_Trace: Class 3 includes "cleaning, polishing, scouring and abrasive preparations". Shoe polish is a preparation specifically for polishing shoes, falling directly into this category. Meaning 2 is a kit that might contain other items. Meaning 3 is a service (Class 37).

Purpose: Simulating tobacco smoking by vaporizing liquid for inhalation
Expanded_Name: Battery-powered vaping devices, namely electronic cigarettes, for smokers
Logic_Trace: Class 34 explicitly lists "electronic cigarettes and oral vaporizers for smokers". This is a direct match for the device itself. Meaning 2 is too specific (components) and meaning 3 is a brand.

Purpose: Promoting products, services, or brands to target audiences
Expanded_Name: Professional business services in the field of marketing and promotion
Logic_Trace: Class 35 covers "Advertising; business management, organization and administration". Marketing is a core business service involving promotion, advertising, and strategic business functions, fitting well within this class. Meaning 2 refers to an organizational unit, meaning 3 to an abstract concept.

Purpose: Detecting, measuring, or converting light into an electronic signal
Expanded_Name: Electronic detecting and measuring apparatus, namely optical sensors
Logic_Trace: Class 9 includes "optical, weighing, measuring, signalling, detecting, testing, inspecting apparatus and instruments". Optical sensors are detecting/measuring instruments using light, which fits perfectly. Meaning 3 is too specific and likely not the primary meaning in this class.

Purpose: Assembling and covering books
Expanded_Name: Materials and components for the process of bookbinding
Logic_Trace: Class 16 explicitly lists "bookbinding material". This covers the raw materials and components used in the physical process of binding books. Meaning 2 is a service (Class 40). Meaning 3 refers to finished printed matter, not the raw material.

Purpose: Providing abdominal and back support to pregnant women
Expanded_Name: Orthopaedic support garments, namely maternity belts, for pregnant women
Logic_Trace: Class 10 includes "orthopaedic articles" and "therapeutic and assistive devices". Maternity belts provide orthopedic support and assistance during pregnancy, fitting well within these categories. Meaning 2 is clothing (Class 25). Meaning 3 (baby carriers) would be different.

Purpose: Bonding bricks or stones and filling gaps in masonry construction
Expanded_Name: Non-metallic construction material, namely mortar, for masonry
Logic_Trace: Class 19 covers "Materials, not of metal, for building and construction". Mortar (the building material) is a key component in masonry construction, fitting this class perfectly. Meaning 2 (weapon) is Class 13. Meaning 3 (tool) is Class 8 or 21 depending on its nature.

Purpose: Covering and adorning the human body
Expanded_Name: Garments and articles of dress for covering the human body
Logic_Trace: Class 25 explicitly lists "Clothing, footwear, headwear". This is a direct match. Meaning 2 refers to raw textiles (Class 24). Meaning 3 is an abstract concept.

Purpose: Displaying visual information from electronic devices
Expanded_Name: Electronic display devices, namely screens, for visual information
Logic_Trace: Class 9 includes "apparatus and instruments for recording, transmitting, reproducing or processing sound, images or data; computers and computer peripheral devices". This clearly aligns with screens as display devices for electronic information. Physical barriers (meaning 2) could be Class 20, sifting devices (meaning 3) could be Class 7 or 8.

Purpose: Manipulating, enhancing, or analyzing digital images
Expanded_Name: Electronic apparatus and software for processing digital images
Logic_Trace: Class 9 includes "apparatus and instruments for recording, transmitting, reproducing or processing sound, images or data; computers and computer peripheral devices". Image processors, whether hardware or software, fall directly into the category of processing images or data. Meaning 2 is outdated for general "image processors". Meaning 3 refers to a person/service, not an apparatus or software.

Purpose: Consuming as a sweet treat
Expanded_Name: Edible confectionery, namely sweetmeats in the nature of candy
Logic_Trace: Class 30 includes "bread, pastries and confectionery; chocolate". "Sweetmeats [candy]" directly refers to confectionery, fitting this class. Meaning 2 is too broad. Meaning 3 is a specific type of food within confectionery.

Original_Term: Articles of jewellery coated with precious metals
Purpose: Adorning the body as jewellery, featuring a coating of precious metal
Expanded_Name: Decorative articles of jewellery coated with precious metals
Logic_Trace: Class 14 explicitly lists "jewellery, precious and semi-precious stones". Jewellery coated with precious metals is still fundamentally an article of jewellery, fitting perfectly within this class. Meaning 2 refers to the raw material, not an article of jewellery. Meaning 3 would be for the coating material (Class 2).

Purpose: Consuming as a cooked food item
Expanded_Name: Edible vegetable foodstuffs prepared by cooking
Logic_Trace: Class 29 explicitly lists "preserved, frozen, dried and cooked fruits and vegetables". This directly matches the term, referring to the food product itself. Meaning 2 is a service (Class 43). Meaning 3 is a specific context for the food.

Purpose: Writing, drawing, and general office use
Expanded_Name: Paper-based and writing materials, namely stationery
Logic_Trace: Class 16 explicitly lists "stationery and office requisites, except furniture". This is a direct match for the general category of writing and office supplies. Meaning 2 is too specific. Meaning 3 is a service (Class 35 for retail).

Purpose: Generating power or other forms of energy
Expanded_Name: Services for generating energy
Logic_Trace: Class 40 describes services related to 'Treatment of materials'. Energy production, in this context, refers to the industrial process or service of generating energy rather than the energy itself or the physical plant. This aligns with the material treatment aspect of transforming resources into energy.

Purpose: Wrapping and packaging goods
Expanded_Name: Paper sheets and rolls for wrapping and packaging
Logic_Trace: Class 16 specifically mentions 'Paper and cardboard' and 'plastic sheets, films and bags for wrapping and packaging'. This confirms that 'Paper for wrapping and packaging' refers to the raw material itself, intended for these functions.

Purpose: Soothing discomfort during infant teething
Expanded_Name: Rings for soothing infants' teething discomfort
Logic_Trace: Class 10 includes 'apparatus, devices and articles for nursing infants'. Teething rings are articles designed to aid infants during the teething process, fitting this description as an infant care item.

Purpose: Regulating milk flow from feeding bottles for infants
Expanded_Name: Teats for infant feeding bottles
Logic_Trace: Class 10 covers 'apparatus, devices and articles for nursing infants'. Feeding bottle teats are essential components of infant feeding bottles, directly aligning with articles for nursing infants.

Purpose: Safekeeping valuable items or assets
Expanded_Name: Services for the safekeeping of valuables
Logic_Trace: Class 36 specifies 'Financial, monetary and banking services'. The term 'Deposits of valuables' refers to a service of safekeeping, typically offered by financial or security institutions, which is a banking or financial service.

Purpose: Cleaning, conditioning, and polishing leather
Expanded_Name: Non-medicated creams for cleaning and conditioning leather
Logic_Trace: Class 3 includes 'cleaning, polishing, scouring and abrasive preparations'. Creams specifically formulated for leather are used for cleaning, conditioning, and polishing leather goods, fitting within this category of preparations.

Purpose: Assessing and accepting risks for accident insurance policies
Expanded_Name: Underwriting services for accident insurance
Logic_Trace: Class 36 explicitly covers 'insurance services'. Underwriting is the core service component of assessing and accepting risks for accident insurance policies, making it a direct fit.

Purpose: Transmitting and receiving voice communications over a distance
Expanded_Name: Electronic apparatus for telephone communication
Logic_Trace: Class 9 includes 'apparatus and instruments for recording, transmitting, reproducing or processing sound, images or data'. Telephone apparatus are electronic devices designed for transmitting and receiving sound (voice communication), which aligns with this description.

Purpose: Transporting and delivering furniture items to a specified location
Expanded_Name: Services for the delivery and transport of furniture
Logic_Trace: Class 39 specifies 'Transport; packaging and storage of goods'. The 'delivery of furniture' is a direct example of a transportation service for goods, fitting perfectly within this class.

Purpose: Flavoring and accompanying food dishes as a condiment
Expanded_Name: Edible sauces for use as condiments
Logic_Trace: Class 30 explicitly lists 'vinegar, sauces and other condiments'. The term 'Sauces [condiments]' is a direct match, specifying its nature as an edible food preparation used for flavoring.

Purpose: Facilitating the exchange and trading of different national currencies
Expanded_Name: Financial services for currency trading and exchange
Logic_Trace: Class 36 covers 'Financial, monetary and banking services'. Currency trading and exchange are fundamental monetary and financial services, aligning perfectly with the class description.

Purpose: Assessing and accepting risks for fire insurance policies
Expanded_Name: Underwriting services for fire insurance
Logic_Trace: Class 36 explicitly covers 'insurance services'. Underwriting is the core service of evaluating risks and accepting liabilities for fire insurance policies, making it a direct fit.

Purpose: Providing a source of non-live, edible seafood
Expanded_Name: Non-live shellfish prepared for consumption
Logic_Trace: Class 29 includes 'Meat, fish, poultry and game' and lists various prepared food products. 'Shellfish, not live' clearly refers to edible shellfish prepared for food consumption, fitting the general category of food products in this class.

Purpose: Producing light through combustion
Expanded_Name: Candles and wicks specifically for lighting purposes
Logic_Trace: Class 4 explicitly lists 'candles and wicks for lighting'. This term is a direct match, confirming its nature as an illuminant.

Purpose: Providing comfortable and slip-resistant covering for feet in bathroom environments
Expanded_Name: Slippers designed for use in bathing or wet environments
Logic_Trace: Class 25 is dedicated to 'Clothing, footwear, headwear'. Bath slippers are a specific type of footwear, designed for particular environments, fitting directly into this class.

Purpose: Providing financial, monetary, and credit-related services to individuals and businesses
Expanded_Name: Financial services related to banking operations
Logic_Trace: Class 36 explicitly covers 'Financial, monetary and banking services'. 'Banking' refers to the provision of these financial and monetary services.

Purpose: Providing management and advisory services regarding money, credit, and investments
Expanded_Name: General financial services for money and asset management
Logic_Trace: Class 36 explicitly covers 'Financial, monetary and banking services'. 'Finance services' is a broad term directly encompassing these listed financial activities.

Purpose: Providing fruit that has undergone preservation processes for extended shelf life and consumption
Expanded_Name: Preserved fruit for food consumption
Logic_Trace: Class 29 explicitly lists 'preserved, frozen, dried and cooked fruits and vegetables'. 'Fruit, preserved' is a direct match for this category, indicating fruit that has undergone a preservation process for consumption.

Original_Term: Ultraviolet ray lamps, not for medical purposes
Purpose: Emitting ultraviolet rays for non-medical applications, such as disinfection or curing
Expanded_Name: Lamps emitting ultraviolet rays, not for medical applications
Logic_Trace: Class 11 covers 'Apparatus and installations for lighting, heating, cooling...'. Ultraviolet ray lamps, when specified as 'not for medical purposes', function primarily as lighting apparatus or for related non-medical heating/drying applications, fitting this class.

Purpose: Marking, writing, and drawing on surfaces
Expanded_Name: Writing and drawing pencils
Logic_Trace: Class 16 includes 'stationery and office requisites' and 'drawing materials and materials for artists'. Pencils are fundamental items for both writing and drawing, fitting these categories directly.

Purpose: Regulating and controlling the distribution and use of electricity in systems
Expanded_Name: Electrical control panels for regulating and distributing electricity
Logic_Trace: Class 9 mentions 'apparatus and instruments for conducting, switching, transforming, accumulating, regulating or controlling the distribution or use of electricity'. Electric control panels are precisely designed to regulate and control electrical distribution and usage.

Purpose: Increasing the amplitude or power of an electrical signal, such as sound or radio waves
Expanded_Name: Electronic amplifiers for increasing signal strength
Logic_Trace: Class 9 covers 'apparatus and instruments for recording, transmitting, reproducing or processing sound, images or data'. Amplifiers are electronic apparatus that process (by increasing the strength of) electrical signals, including those for sound or data, aligning with this description.

Purpose: Winding or unwinding materials such as wire, cable, or film within a machine
Expanded_Name: Reels as parts of machines
Logic_Trace: Class 7 covers 'Machines, machine tools...; machine coupling and transmission components...'. The term 'Reels [parts of machines]' explicitly identifies them as components integral to the functioning of machines, fitting this class.

Purpose: Brewing a beverage for consumption
Expanded_Name: Dried tea leaves for making beverages
Logic_Trace: Class 30 explicitly lists 'Coffee, tea, cocoa and substitutes therefor'. 'Tea' is a direct match for one of the primary goods in this class.

Purpose: Assessing and accepting risks for marine insurance policies
Expanded_Name: Underwriting services for marine insurance
Logic_Trace: Class 36 explicitly covers 'insurance services'. Underwriting is the core service of evaluating risks and accepting liabilities for marine insurance policies, making it a direct fit.

Purpose: Containing recorded photographic or cinematographic images or data
Expanded_Name: Exposed photographic and cinematographic films
Logic_Trace: Class 9 covers 'photographic, cinematographic... apparatus and instruments' and 'apparatus and instruments for recording, transmitting, reproducing or processing sound, images or data'. Exposed films contain recorded photographic or cinematographic images/data, fitting this description as recorded media.

Purpose: Providing activities and facilities for wagering and games of chance
Expanded_Name: Entertainment services in the nature of gambling
Logic_Trace: Class 41 explicitly includes 'entertainment; sporting and cultural activities'. Gambling services are inherently a form of entertainment, aligning with this class.

Purpose: Managing and advising on matters relating to money and currency
Expanded_Name: Financial services concerning monetary affairs
Logic_Trace: Class 36 explicitly covers 'Financial, monetary and banking services'. 'Monetary affairs' refers to the broad range of services dealing with money, its management, and related financial transactions, fitting this class.

Purpose: Protecting eyes from sunlight and reducing glare
Expanded_Name: Optical apparatus in the nature of sunglasses for protection against glare
Logic_Trace: Class 9 includes 'optical... apparatus and instruments'. Sunglasses are optical instruments designed to filter light and protect the eyes, aligning with this category.

Purpose: Generating heat for various purposes such as space heating or industrial processes
Expanded_Name: Apparatus for generating heat
Logic_Trace: Class 11 explicitly lists 'Apparatus and installations for lighting, heating, cooling...'. 'Heating apparatus' is a direct and general term for devices designed to generate heat, fitting perfectly within this class.

Purpose: Being eaten as a meal or snack
Expanded_Name: Prepared food product in the nature of flat cakes made from batter
Logic_Trace: The NICE Class 30 description includes "flour and preparations made from cereals; bread, pastries and confectionery." This clearly matches 'flat cakes made from batter, cooked on a griddle'. 'Pancake makeup' would be Class 3, and a 'pancake landing' is not a product.

Purpose: Providing monetary funds for various transactions or investments
Expanded_Name: Financial services, namely, providing monetary funds
Logic_Trace: The Class 36 description explicitly lists "Financial, monetary and banking services." 'Financing services' directly pertains to providing or obtaining funds within this financial context.

Purpose: Assessing risk and determining coverage for health insurance policies
Expanded_Name: Insurance services, namely, underwriting health insurance policies
Logic_Trace: The Class 36 description explicitly includes "insurance services." 'Underwriting' is a core process within insurance, specifically for assessing risks and issuing policies, which fits this category.

Purpose: Being consumed as a foodstuff, typically with bread or toast
Expanded_Name: Preserved fruit spreads, namely, jams
Logic_Trace: The Class 29 description explicitly lists "jellies, jams, compotes." This directly confirms the meaning of fruit preserves. The other meanings are not physical products and do not fit this class.

Original_Term: Safety seats for children, for vehicles
Purpose: Securing children safely within vehicles during transport
Expanded_Name: Child restraint apparatus, namely, safety seats for children, for use in vehicles
Logic_Trace: The term explicitly states "for vehicles," and Class 12 covers "Vehicles; apparatus for locomotion by land, air or water." Child safety seats are apparatus for vehicle locomotion. Amusement park seats (Class 28) or training seats (Class 9) are explicitly excluded by the 'for vehicles' qualifier.

Purpose: Igniting gas for lighting, heating, or cooking purposes
Expanded_Name: Ignition apparatus, namely, gas lighters for lighting, heating, or cooking purposes
Logic_Trace: Class 11 covers "Apparatus and installations for lighting, heating, cooling, steam generating, cooking..." Gas lighters are used for igniting gas, often for cooking or heating purposes, fitting broadly within this description.

Purpose: Being used as a beverage or as a preparation for making other non-alcoholic beverages
Expanded_Name: Unfermented fruit juice, namely, must (grape must)
Logic_Trace: The Class 32 description lists "non-alcoholic beverages; fruit beverages and fruit juices; syrups and other preparations for making non-alcoholic beverages." Unfermented grape juice ('must') fits perfectly as a fruit beverage or preparation.

Purpose: Cutting internal screw threads in materials
Expanded_Name: Hand tools for cutting internal screw threads, namely, taps
Logic_Trace: The term explicitly specifies '[hand tools],' and Class 8 is for "Hand tools and implements, hand-operated." This clearly points to the meaning of a tool for cutting internal screw threads. Faucets would be Class 11.

Original_Term: Yarns and threads for textile use
Purpose: Being used in the production of textiles through weaving, knitting, or sewing
Expanded_Name: Textile materials, namely, yarns and threads for textile use
Logic_Trace: The term is a direct match with the Class 23 description: "Yarns and threads for textile use." The other meanings are clearly incorrect for this product class.

Purpose: Dispensing products in exchange for payment
Expanded_Name: Automatic machines for dispensing products, namely, vending machines
Logic_Trace: The Class 7 description explicitly includes "automatic vending machines." This is a direct and unambiguous match for the term.

Purpose: Booking and arranging transport for passengers or goods
Expanded_Name: Travel arrangement services, namely, transport reservation services
Logic_Trace: Class 39 covers "Transport; packaging and storage of goods; travel arrangement." 'Transport reservation' clearly falls under services for arranging travel or booking transport, aligning with 'travel arrangement'.

Purpose: Being worn as an accessory over shoes and ankles for protection or fashion
Expanded_Name: Articles of clothing, namely, spats worn over shoes and ankles
Logic_Trace: Class 25 is for "Clothing, footwear, headwear." Spats, as short gaiters worn over shoes and ankles, are articles of clothing/footwear. The other meanings are not physical goods.

Purpose: Facilitating transactions and management of real property
Expanded_Name: Services related to real property, namely, real estate services
Logic_Trace: The Class 36 description explicitly lists "real estate services." This is a direct and unambiguous match.

Purpose: Diagnosing, treating, and preventing human diseases and injuries
Expanded_Name: Professional healthcare services, namely, medical services
Logic_Trace: The Class 44 description explicitly lists "Medical services." This is a direct and unambiguous match. Equipment repair (Class 37) and research (Class 42) are distinct service categories.

Purpose: Forming the stiff projecting front of a cap
Expanded_Name: Component parts of headwear, namely, cap peaks
Logic_Trace: Class 25 covers "Clothing, footwear, headwear." A cap peak is a component part of a cap, which is a type of headwear. The other meanings are not physical goods in this context.

Purpose: Managing and handling various matters pertaining to real property
Expanded_Name: Services related to real property, namely, managing real estate affairs
Logic_Trace: Class 36 explicitly lists "real estate services." 'Real estate affairs' in this context refers to the professional handling and management of real property, which falls under real estate services.

Purpose: Being eaten as a snack or ingredient
Expanded_Name: Preserved food products, namely, dried fruit
Logic_Trace: The Class 29 description explicitly lists "preserved, frozen, dried and cooked fruits and vegetables." This directly matches the definition of fruit with water removed for consumption.

Purpose: Indicating or determining quantities such as pressure, temperature, or liquid levels
Expanded_Name: Measuring instruments, namely, gauges
Logic_Trace: The Class 9 description explicitly includes "measuring, signalling, detecting, testing, inspecting apparatus and instruments." 'Gauges' directly refer to measuring instruments within this category. The other brainstormed meanings are not physical products or are properties rather than instruments.

Purpose: Being eaten as a snack or ingredient, or for further processing
Expanded_Name: Preserved food products, namely, frozen fruits
Logic_Trace: The Class 29 description explicitly lists "preserved, frozen, dried and cooked fruits and vegetables." This directly matches fruits preserved by freezing for consumption. Fruit-flavored ice desserts would typically be 'edible ices' in Class 30.

Purpose: Being eaten as a ready-to-eat snack
Expanded_Name: Prepared food products, namely, potato snacks
Logic_Trace: Class 29 includes "preserved, frozen, dried and cooked fruits and vegetables" and other food products. Many common potato snacks (e.g., potato chips, crisps, processed potato products) fall under this broad category of prepared/processed vegetables for consumption. Raw potatoes are Class 31.

Purpose: Providing legal advice, representation, and assistance
Expanded_Name: Professional services, namely, legal services
Logic_Trace: The Class 45 description explicitly lists "Legal services." This is a direct and unambiguous match. Legal publishing is typically Class 41.

Purpose: Providing insulation or protection for pipes
Expanded_Name: Non-metallic insulating and protective coverings, namely, pipe muffs
Logic_Trace: Class 17 explicitly lists "packing, stopping and insulating materials; flexible pipes, tubes and hoses, not of metal." 'Pipe muffs, not of metal' perfectly fits as insulating or protective coverings for pipes made of non-metallic materials.

Purpose: Being eaten as a convenient and long-lasting foodstuff
Expanded_Name: Preserved food products, namely, tinned vegetables
Logic_Trace: The Class 29 description explicitly lists "preserved, frozen, dried and cooked fruits and vegetables." 'Tinned vegetables' are a form of preserved vegetables, fitting this category directly.

Purpose: Whitening and removing stains from laundry
Expanded_Name: Chemical preparations for laundry use, namely, laundry bleach
Logic_Trace: The Class 3 description explicitly lists "bleaching preparations and other substances for laundry use." This is a direct and unambiguous match for the term.

Purpose: Initiating the detonation of an explosive charge
Expanded_Name: Explosive device components, namely, detonating plugs
Logic_Trace: Class 13 explicitly lists "Explosives." Detonating plugs are integral components for initiating explosives, fitting directly into this class.

Purpose: Being used for writing by hand or with a writing instrument
Expanded_Name: Stationery material, namely, writing paper
Logic_Trace: Class 16 explicitly lists "Paper and cardboard; stationery and office requisites." Paper designed for writing on perfectly fits this category. Digital surfaces are Class 9.

Purpose: Performing calculations, storing data, and executing programs
