Expanded_Name: Electronic data processing apparatus, namely, computers
Logic_Trace: The Class 9 description explicitly lists "computers and computer peripheral devices." This is a direct and unambiguous match for the term, referring to the hardware.

Purpose: Mixing air and fuel for internal combustion engines
Expanded_Name: Engine components, namely, carburetters
Logic_Trace: Class 7 covers "Machines, machine tools, power-operated tools; motors and engines, except for land vehicles." Carburetters are essential components of internal combustion engines, which fall under motors and engines.

Purpose: Being consumed as a staple food item
Expanded_Name: Baked food product, namely, bread
Logic_Trace: The Class 30 description explicitly lists "bread, pastries and confectionery." This is a direct and unambiguous match for the term.

Original_Term: Chemical substances, chemical materials and chemical preparations, and natural elements
Purpose: Being used in industrial, scientific, agricultural, or manufacturing processes
Expanded_Name: Raw or processed chemical products, namely, chemical substances, chemical materials and chemical preparations, and natural elements for industrial purposes
Logic_Trace: The Class 1 description explicitly lists "Chemicals for use in industry, science and photography, as well as in agriculture, horticulture and forestry." The term precisely defines the broad category of raw or semi-processed chemicals and natural elements as industrial inputs for this class.

Original_Term: Putties, and fillers and pastes for use in industry
Nature: Chemical compounds in the form of putties, fillers, and pastes.
Purpose: Filling gaps, sealing, binding, or smoothing surfaces in industrial applications.
Expanded_Name: Chemical putties, fillers and pastes for industrial sealing, binding, and gap filling
Logic_Trace: The Class 1 description explicitly mentions "adhesives for use in industry; putties and other paste fillers." This confirms the industrial context and the nature of the products for filling, binding, and sealing. The craft material meaning is rejected.

Nature: Chemical compositions in liquid, gel, or spray form.
Purpose: Cleansing surfaces and imparting a pleasant scent.
Expanded_Name: Non-medicated chemical preparations for cleaning and fragrancing surfaces
Logic_Trace: The Class 3 description includes "cleaning, polishing, scouring and abrasive preparations" and "perfumery, essential oils." This confirms the term refers to non-medicated chemical preparations for both cleaning and imparting scent, aligning with household and personal care contexts.

Nature: Non-medicated chemical preparations in liquid, spray, or cream form.
Purpose: Cleaning, conditioning, and deodorizing the fur and skin of animals.
Expanded_Name: Non-medicated chemical preparations for animal grooming, cleaning, and conditioning
Logic_Trace: Class 3 covers non-medicated cosmetics and toiletry preparations. Therefore, 'Animal grooming preparations' refers to non-medicated products like shampoos, conditioners, and deodorizers for animals. Medicated treatments (Class 5) and grooming tools (Class 8) are excluded.

Nature: Volatile liquid compounds extracted from plants.
Purpose: Imparting scent for perfumery, cosmetics, or aromatherapy.
Expanded_Name: Natural essential oils and aromatic extracts for perfumery, cosmetics, and aromatherapy
Logic_Trace: The Class 3 description explicitly lists "essential oils" and "perfumery." This directly supports their use in perfumery, cosmetics, and aromatherapy. Flavoring agents for food (Class 30) and industrial solvents (Class 1) are rejected.

Nature: Chemical compositions in liquid, solid, or spray form.
Purpose: Imparting pleasant scents to the body or to environments.
Expanded_Name: Chemical preparations for perfumery and fragrances for personal or ambient use
Logic_Trace: Class 3 explicitly includes "perfumery, essential oils." This indicates that the term refers to products primarily for personal use or for scenting environments. Flavorings for food or drink are classified elsewhere (Class 30).

Original_Term: Body cleaning and beauty care preparations
Nature: Non-medicated chemical preparations in liquid, gel, cream, or solid form.
Purpose: Cleansing the human body and enhancing personal appearance.
Expanded_Name: Non-medicated chemical preparations for body cleansing and beauty care
Logic_Trace: The Class 3 description specifies "Non-medicated cosmetics and toiletry preparations." This confirms the products are non-medicated and intended for cleansing the body and enhancing personal appearance. Medicated treatments would fall under Class 5.

Nature: Wax-based preparations in solid or paste form.
Purpose: Polishing, waterproofing, conditioning, or marking materials for tailoring and cobbling.
Expanded_Name: Wax-based preparations for tailors' fabric treatment and cobblers' shoe and leather care
Logic_Trace: Class 3 covers cleaning, polishing, scouring, and abrasive preparations, as well as non-medicated cosmetics. The 'polishing' aspect supports the cobblers' wax. Tailors' wax, used for fabric or thread treatment, aligns with 'preparations' within this general class, distinct from industrial chemicals (Class 1). Sealing wax is Class 16.

Nature: Non-medicated chemical compositions in cream, lotion, gel, or serum form.
Purpose: Moisturizing, protecting, cleansing, and improving the appearance of skin.
Expanded_Name: Non-medicated chemical preparations for skin care, including moisturizing, cleansing, and protection
Logic_Trace: Class 3 explicitly states "Non-medicated cosmetics and toiletry preparations." This confirms that 'Skin care preparations' in this context refers to non-medicated products for cosmetic and hygienic purposes. Medicated ointments and surgical preparations fall under Class 5.

Nature: Chemical or biological preparations (e.g., insecticides, rodenticides, repellents) and impregnated articles (e.g., traps containing such preparations).
Purpose: Destroying, repelling, or controlling pests, vermin, fungi, and unwanted plants.
Expanded_Name: Chemical and biological preparations and articles for pest control, including insecticides, rodenticides, and repellents
Logic_Trace: The Class 5 description explicitly lists "preparations for destroying vermin; fungicides, herbicides." This supports the meaning of chemical or biological compositions for pest control. 'Articles' would refer to items impregnated with these preparations (e.g., bait stations). Devices/traps without such preparations would be in other classes.

Nature: Medical-grade chemical preparations (e.g., disinfectants, antiseptics) and articles impregnated with such preparations.
Purpose: Promoting health and preventing disease through disinfection and sanitation, especially in medical or sanitary contexts.
Expanded_Name: Sanitary and disinfectant preparations and articles for medical and hygienic purposes
Logic_Trace: Class 5 specifies "sanitary preparations for medical purposes" and "disinfectants." This clarifies that 'Hygienic preparations and articles' refers to products with a medical or disinfectant purpose, differentiating them from general non-medicated cleaning or personal hygiene products (Class 3). Articles would be items impregnated with these preparations.

Nature: Medical-grade chemical preparations and materials in various forms (e.g., pastes, waxes, solutions, powders).
Purpose: Filling, restoring, molding, cleaning, or treating teeth and gums in dental procedures.
Expanded_Name: Medical-grade chemical preparations and articles for dental purposes, including fillings, waxes, and impression materials
Logic_Trace: The Class 5 description explicitly mentions "material for stopping teeth, dental wax." This strongly supports the meaning of materials used in dental procedures, which often have a medical or therapeutic component. Non-medicated toothpastes are Class 3, and non-medicated tools like toothbrushes or floss are Class 21.

Nature: Nutritional chemical preparations in solid, liquid, or powdered form.
Purpose: Supplementing diet or providing specific nutritional support for medical or dietary needs for humans and animals.
Expanded_Name: Nutritional chemical preparations for dietary supplementation and dietetic purposes for humans and animals
Logic_Trace: The Class 5 description explicitly lists "dietetic food and substances adapted for medical or veterinary use, food for babies; dietary supplements for human beings and animals." This directly matches the first two meanings, indicating products specifically formulated for health, medical, or supplementary purposes, distinct from ordinary foodstuffs.

Original_Term: Medical and veterinary preparations and articles
Nature: Chemical or biological preparations (e.g., pharmaceuticals, vaccines, serums) and related articles (e.g., medicated dressings, plasters).
Purpose: Diagnosing, treating, curing, mitigating, or preventing diseases in humans and animals.
Expanded_Name: Pharmaceutical, medical, and veterinary preparations and articles for human and animal healthcare
Logic_Trace: The Class 5 description begins with "Pharmaceuticals, medical and veterinary preparations." This confirms the meaning of substances with therapeutic or diagnostic properties. 'Articles' refers to items impregnated with these preparations (e.g., medicated bandages) or specific medical articles (e.g., plasters, materials for dressings). Medical devices/instruments are generally Class 10.

Original_Term: Building and construction materials and elements of metal
Purpose: Forming structural parts or finishing elements in building and construction projects.
Expanded_Name: Fabricated metal materials and elements for building and construction purposes
Logic_Trace: The Class 6 description explicitly states "metal materials for building and construction." This confirms the focus on finished or semi-finished metal components directly used in structures. Raw metallic materials are also covered in Class 6, but the term specifically points to forms for construction. Tools are in Class 8 or 7.

Original_Term: Doors, gates, windows and window coverings of metal
Purpose: Providing access, security, light, and ventilation control for buildings and enclosures.
Expanded_Name: Fabricated metal doors, gates, windows, and window coverings for architectural applications
Logic_Trace: Class 6 covers "metal materials for building and construction." The term specifies "of metal," directly linking these finished building elements to the class. The rejection of non-metal versions is based on this explicit qualifier. Metal parts and fittings would also fall under Class 6 as components or hardware.

Original_Term: Statues and works of art of common metals
Nature: Sculptural or decorative objects crafted from common metals.
Purpose: Displaying artistic expression and enhancing aesthetic appeal.
Expanded_Name: Sculptures and decorative works of art made from common metals
Logic_Trace: Class 6 includes "Common metals and their alloys, ores." The term "Statues and works of art of common metals" aligns with this, covering artistic creations made from non-precious metals. Works of art from precious metals are Class 14. Drawings/paintings are Class 16.

Original_Term: Structures and transportable buildings of metal
Nature: Fabricated metal constructions, either fixed or designed for relocation.
Purpose: Providing enclosed spaces or supporting frameworks for various applications, designed to be movable or constructed on-site.
Expanded_Name: Fabricated metal structures and transportable buildings
Logic_Trace: The Class 6 description explicitly mentions "transportable buildings of metal." This directly covers prefabricated and modular metal buildings. "Structures of metal" also fits within the scope of metal materials for building and construction, encompassing both fixed and transportable types.

Original_Term: Containers, and transportation and packaging articles, of metal
Nature: Fabricated metal vessels, receptacles, and enclosures.
Purpose: Storing, transporting, and packaging goods and materials.
Expanded_Name: Fabricated metal containers and articles for transportation and packaging
Logic_Trace: The Class 6 description explicitly includes "metal containers for storage or transport." This confirms the first meaning. "Packaging articles, of metal" also fits as these are typically made of common metal. The "of metal" qualifier excludes non-metal items.

Original_Term: Agricultural, earthmoving, construction, oil and gas extraction and mining equipment
Purpose: Performing tasks in agriculture, earthmoving, construction, oil and gas extraction, and mining industries.
Expanded_Name: Power-operated machines and implements for agricultural, earthmoving, construction, oil and gas extraction, and mining operations
Logic_Trace: Class 7 covers "Machines, machine tools, power-operated tools" and "agricultural implements, other than hand-operated hand tools." This clearly defines the scope as large, power-operated equipment and non-hand-operated agricultural implements. Hand tools are Class 8, and land vehicles are Class 12.

Purpose: Automatically delivering or distributing predefined quantities of goods or materials.
Expanded_Name: Automatic dispensing machines for industrial and commercial use
Logic_Trace: The Class 7 description explicitly includes "automatic vending machines" and generally covers "Machines." This aligns with industrial and commercial dispensing machines. Medical dispensing devices may fall under Class 5 or 10 if they have a primary medical purpose; however, general dispensing machines are covered here.

Original_Term: Sweeping, cleaning, washing and laundering machines
Purpose: Mechanically cleaning surfaces, objects, or textiles through sweeping, washing, or laundering.
Expanded_Name: Power-operated machines for sweeping, cleaning, washing, and laundering purposes
Logic_Trace: Class 7 covers "Machines, machine tools, power-operated tools." This restricts the term to power-operated devices. Hand-operated tools (Class 8 or 21) and cleaning chemicals (Class 3) are excluded.

Original_Term: Machines and machine tools for treatment of materials and for manufacturing
Purpose: Processing raw materials and fabricating products in manufacturing operations.
Expanded_Name: Power-operated machines and machine tools for industrial material treatment and manufacturing processes
Logic_Trace: Class 7 explicitly includes "Machines, machine tools, power-operated tools." This perfectly aligns with the term, indicating industrial-scale, power-operated equipment for material processing and manufacturing. Hand tools (Class 8) and raw materials (various classes) are excluded.

Purpose: Mechanically lifting, transporting, positioning, and otherwise manipulating objects or materials.
Expanded_Name: Power-operated machines and equipment for moving and handling materials and objects
Logic_Trace: Class 7 covers "Machines, machine tools, power-operated tools." This restricts the term to power-operated machinery for moving and handling tasks. Hand-operated devices (Class 8) and land vehicles (Class 12) are excluded.

Purpose: Moving or compressing liquids or gases, or creating airflows for various applications.
Expanded_Name: Power-operated pumps, compressors, and blowers for fluid and gas handling
Logic_Trace: Class 7 includes "Machines, machine tools, power-operated tools; motors and engines." Pumps, compressors, and blowers are inherently machines or power-operated tools for moving fluids or gas, fitting perfectly within this description. Hand-operated versions (Class 8) are excluded.

Original_Term: Hand-operated hygienic and beauty implements for humans and animals
Purpose: Performing hygienic, grooming, and beauty care tasks for humans and animals.
Expanded_Name: Hand-operated implements for human and animal hygienic and beauty care
Logic_Trace: The Class 8 description explicitly lists "Hand tools and implements, hand-operated; cutlery; side arms, except firearms; razors." This confirms the scope to be non-electric, hand-operated implements. Electric appliances (Class 9 or 11) are excluded.

Original_Term: Food preparation implements, kitchen knives and cutlery
Purpose: Preparing, cutting, and serving food in the kitchen.
Expanded_Name: Hand-operated implements, kitchen knives, and cutlery for food preparation and serving
Logic_Trace: Class 8 specifically mentions "cutlery" and "hand tools and implements, hand-operated." This directly covers non-electric tools for food preparation, including knives. Electric appliances (Class 7 or 11) and cookware (Class 21) are excluded.

Nature: Hand-held implements designed for combat or self-defense.
Purpose: Causing harm or incapacitation through cutting, stabbing, or blunt force.
Expanded_Name: Hand-operated edged and blunt weapons, excluding firearms
Logic_Trace: Class 8 lists "side arms, except firearms." This clearly defines the scope to non-firearm weapons, which include both edged (e.g., swords) and blunt (e.g., clubs) weapons. Firearms are Class 13.

Purpose: Manually lifting, prying, or otherwise moving objects.
Expanded_Name: Hand-operated tools and implements for lifting and prying
Logic_Trace: Class 8 covers "Hand tools and implements, hand-operated." This means 'Lifting tools' in this class must be non-powered, distinguishing them from power-operated lifting equipment (Class 7).

Original_Term: Hand-operated tools and implements for treatment of materials, and for construction, repair and maintenance
Purpose: Shaping, joining, fastening, cutting, or otherwise working with materials in construction, repair, and maintenance tasks.
Expanded_Name: Hand-operated tools and implements for manual treatment of materials, and for construction, repair and maintenance
Logic_Trace: The Class 8 description is "Hand tools and implements, hand-operated." This directly matches the term, emphasizing that these are non-powered tools. Power tools (Class 7) and materials (various classes) are excluded.

Original_Term: Apparatus, instruments and cables for electricity
Nature: Electrical and electronic devices, components, and conductors.
Purpose: Conducting, switching, transforming, accumulating, regulating, or controlling the distribution and use of electricity.
Expanded_Name: Electrical apparatus, instruments, and cables for conducting, distributing, and controlling electricity
Logic_Trace: The Class 9 description explicitly mentions "apparatus and instruments for conducting, switching, transforming, accumulating, regulating or controlling the distribution or use of electricity." This perfectly defines the scope, focusing on items directly involved in electrical power systems. Non-electrical components would be in other classes.

Purpose: Enhancing, correcting, and manipulating light and images
Expanded_Name: Physical optical apparatus and instruments for enhancing and correcting images
Logic_Trace: The Class 9 description explicitly includes "optical apparatus and instruments." This directly supports physical optical devices as the correct meaning. Software for image/video processing could fall under data processing, but "devices, enhancers and correctors" points more strongly to hardware. Eyeglasses, while correctors, are typically specified differently and the general term covers broader optical instruments.

Original_Term: Safety, security, protection and signalling devices
Nature: Electronic and mechanical apparatus and instruments
Purpose: Ensuring safety, providing security, offering protection, and conveying signals
Expanded_Name: Electronic and mechanical apparatus and instruments for safety, security, protection, and signalling
Logic_Trace: Class 9 explicitly lists "signalling, detecting, testing, inspecting, life-saving apparatus and instruments." This aligns perfectly with electronic and mechanical devices for safety, security, protection, and signalling. While some personal protective equipment could be here, the context of "devices" and the specific mentions in the description lean towards apparatus rather than general clothing.

Original_Term: Navigation, guidance, tracking, targeting and map making devices
Nature: Electronic and mechanical apparatus and instruments
Purpose: Facilitating navigation, providing guidance, tracking positions, aiding targeting, and creating maps
Expanded_Name: Electronic and mechanical apparatus and instruments for navigation, guidance, tracking, targeting, and map making
Logic_Trace: Class 9 explicitly includes "navigation, surveying... apparatus and instruments." This directly confirms that physical and electronic devices for navigation, guidance, tracking, targeting, and map making are the correct meaning. Software could be related but "devices" primarily refers to hardware in this context.

Original_Term: Measuring, detecting and monitoring instruments, indicators and controllers
Purpose: Measuring quantities, detecting phenomena, monitoring conditions, indicating states, and controlling processes
Expanded_Name: Physical apparatus and instruments for measuring, detecting, monitoring, indicating, and controlling
Logic_Trace: Class 9 explicitly mentions "weighing, measuring, signalling, detecting, testing, inspecting... apparatus and instruments" and "apparatus and instruments for ... regulating or controlling the distribution or use of electricity." This is a direct match for physical instruments, indicators, and controllers.

Original_Term: Scientific research and laboratory apparatus, educational apparatus and simulators
Nature: Scientific, laboratory, educational apparatus and simulators (both hardware and software)
Purpose: Conducting scientific research, performing laboratory experiments, facilitating education, and simulating environments or processes
Expanded_Name: Scientific research and laboratory apparatus, educational apparatus, and simulators (hardware and software)
Logic_Trace: Class 9 explicitly lists "Scientific, research... and teaching apparatus and instruments." This is a direct match for all components of the term. Simulators, whether hardware or software, fit within this scope.

Purpose: Storing, reproducing, transmitting, and disseminating sound, images, and data
Expanded_Name: Recorded and downloadable content in digital media format
Logic_Trace: Class 9 specifically includes "recorded and downloadable media" and "apparatus and instruments for recording, transmitting, reproducing or processing sound, images or data." This clearly points to digital audio, visual, and data content. Printed materials would typically fall under Class 16.

Purpose: Processing, transmitting, reproducing, and displaying information, sound, and visual media
Expanded_Name: Electronic apparatus and instruments for information technology and audiovisual purposes
Logic_Trace: Class 9 explicitly includes "apparatus and instruments for recording, transmitting, reproducing or processing sound, images or data," and "computers and computer peripheral devices." This comprehensively covers information technology and audiovisual equipment, primarily referring to the physical hardware.

Purpose: Generating magnetic fields, imparting magnetism, and removing magnetism
Expanded_Name: Physical articles and apparatus, namely magnets, magnetizers, and demagnetizers
Logic_Trace: Class 9 includes "Scientific... apparatus and instruments." Magnets are fundamental components in many scientific and electrical apparatus. Magnetizers and demagnetizers are specialized apparatus for scientific or industrial application, fitting within the scope of Class 9. Magnetic toys would be Class 28.

Original_Term: Apparatus and instruments for controlling electricity
Purpose: Conducting, switching, transforming, accumulating, regulating, or controlling the distribution or use of electricity
Expanded_Name: Electrical apparatus and instruments for controlling electricity
Logic_Trace: Class 9 explicitly states "apparatus and instruments for conducting, switching, transforming, accumulating, regulating or controlling the distribution or use of electricity." This is a direct and perfect match for the term.

Purpose: Protecting individuals and property, and ensuring safety
Expanded_Name: Apparatus and equipment for protective and safety purposes
Logic_Trace: Class 9 includes "life-saving apparatus and instruments" and "fire-extinguishing apparatus." It also covers safety devices that are not clothing (like plain vests) or specialized for other classes. Personal protective equipment, if it has a scientific/electrical function or is specified like diving suits, fits here. The general term "equipment" for protective and safety purposes is well-suited to Class 9.

Purpose: Observing, detecting, checking, or recording conditions or data over time
Expanded_Name: Physical apparatus and instruments for monitoring
Logic_Trace: Class 9 explicitly includes "detecting, testing, inspecting... apparatus and instruments." Monitoring instruments, which are used to observe or check the progress or quality of something over time, fit squarely within this description.

Purpose: Assisting with feeding and providing comfort for infants
Expanded_Name: Apparatus, devices, and articles for feeding assistance and as pacifiers for infants
Logic_Trace: Class 10 explicitly includes "apparatus, devices and articles for nursing infants" and "therapeutic and assistive devices adapted for persons with disabilities." Feeding aids for infants or people with disabilities, and pacifiers, perfectly fit this description. Nutritional supplements are Class 5.

Original_Term: Medical and veterinary apparatus and instruments
Purpose: Performing surgical, medical, dental, and veterinary procedures and examinations
Expanded_Name: Physical apparatus and instruments for medical and veterinary purposes
Logic_Trace: Class 10 explicitly lists "Surgical, medical, dental and veterinary apparatus and instruments." This is a direct and perfect match. The term refers to physical devices rather than software.

Original_Term: Flues and installations for conveying exhaust gases
Purpose: Conveying exhaust gases away from heating or combustion sources
Expanded_Name: Installations and apparatus for conveying exhaust gases, including flues
Logic_Trace: Class 11 includes "ventilating... apparatus and installations." Flues and installations for conveying exhaust gases are essential components of heating and ventilating systems, which are covered by Class 11. Smoke detection systems would be Class 9.

Purpose: Providing orthopedic support, correcting deformities, and assisting with mobility for persons with disabilities
Expanded_Name: Orthopaedic articles and assistive devices for mobility
Logic_Trace: Class 10 explicitly lists "orthopaedic articles" and "therapeutic and assistive devices adapted for persons with disabilities." This is a perfect match for both orthopedic aids (like braces) and mobility aids (like wheelchairs).

Nature: Artificial body parts and implantable devices
Purpose: Replacing missing body parts or augmenting existing ones for medical or cosmetic reasons
Expanded_Name: Artificial prosthetics and implants for medical and cosmetic purposes
Logic_Trace: Class 10 explicitly lists "artificial limbs, eyes and teeth." This covers prosthetics. Artificial implants (e.g., pacemakers, dental implants, or other internal prostheses) also fall under the broader category of "medical apparatus and instruments" within Class 10.

Purpose: Providing protection, support, or therapeutic benefit in medical contexts
Expanded_Name: Specialized articles of clothing for medical purposes
Logic_Trace: Class 10 covers "surgical, medical... apparatus and instruments" and "orthopaedic articles" as well as "therapeutic and assistive devices." Medical clothing that serves a therapeutic function, protection in a medical context (e.g., sterility during surgery), or assistive purpose (e.g., compression garments) fits within Class 10. General lab coats without a specific medical function would be outside this class.

Original_Term: Medical furniture and bedding, equipment for moving patients
Purpose: Supporting and accommodating patients during medical examinations, treatment, or transport
Expanded_Name: Specialized furniture, bedding, and equipment for medical use and for moving patients
Logic_Trace: Class 10 covers "Surgical, medical... apparatus and instruments" and implicitly includes furniture and equipment directly used in medical settings for patient care. While general furniture is Class 20, medical-specific furniture like hospital beds, examination tables, and specialized equipment for moving patients (e.g., stretchers, hoists) are considered medical apparatus in this context.

Purpose: Generating steam, heat, or energy via nuclear processes
Expanded_Name: Industrial installations for nuclear energy generation or heat production
Logic_Trace: Class 11 is about "Apparatus and installations for lighting, heating, cooling, steam generating, cooking, drying, ventilating, water supply and sanitary purposes." Nuclear installations primarily for steam generating (e.g., nuclear power plants) or heating fit within this scope. Research reactors would likely be Class 9, but 'installations' implies a larger, functional system related to energy/heat.

Original_Term: Heating, ventilating, and air conditioning and purification equipment (ambient)
Purpose: Regulating ambient temperature, humidity, and air quality through heating, cooling, ventilating, and purification
Expanded_Name: Apparatus and equipment for heating, ventilating, air conditioning, and ambient air purification
Logic_Trace: Class 11 explicitly lists "Apparatus and installations for lighting, heating, cooling, steam generating, cooking, drying, ventilating, water supply and sanitary purposes." This is a direct and complete match for the term.

Purpose: Cooling and freezing substances for preservation or other purposes
Expanded_Name: Apparatus and equipment for refrigerating and freezing
Logic_Trace: Class 11 explicitly lists "cooling... apparatus and installations." Refrigerating and freezing equipment falls directly under this description. Non-electric portable coolers would be Class 21.

Original_Term: Regulating and safety accessories for water and gas installations
Purpose: Regulating flow, pressure, and ensuring safety within water and gas supply and distribution installations
Expanded_Name: Accessories and apparatus for regulating and ensuring safety in water and gas installations
Logic_Trace: Class 11 includes "water supply and sanitary purposes" apparatus and installations. Regulating and safety accessories (like valves, pressure gauges, safety cut-offs) directly contribute to the proper and safe functioning of these installations. Gas detectors would be Class 9.

Purpose: Providing personal heating or drying
Expanded_Name: Electric implements and apparatus for personal heating and drying
Logic_Trace: Class 11 includes "heating, ... drying... apparatus and installations." Personal heating (like electric blankets) and drying (like hair dryers) implements fit perfectly. Heating pads for medical purposes would be Class 10. The term, in this general context, refers to non-medical personal use.

Original_Term: Filters for industrial and household use
Purpose: Removing impurities from air or water for industrial and household applications
Expanded_Name: Filtering apparatus and components for industrial and household use
Logic_Trace: Class 11 includes apparatus for "ventilating, water supply and sanitary purposes." Filters are essential components in these systems for both industrial and household use (e.g., air purifiers, water purifiers). Coffee filters made of paper would typically be Class 16 or 21.

Purpose: Performing industrial processes such as heating, cooling, drying, and water or waste treatment
Expanded_Name: Large-scale industrial installations and apparatus for treatment purposes
Logic_Trace: Class 11 includes "Apparatus and installations for lighting, heating, cooling, steam generating, cooking, drying, ventilating, water supply and sanitary purposes." Industrial treatment installations often involve processes such as drying, heating, cooling, or water treatment, which directly align with Class 11.

Original_Term: Cooking, heating, cooling and preservation equipment, for food and beverages
Purpose: Preparing, warming, cooling, and preserving food and beverages
Expanded_Name: Apparatus and equipment for cooking, heating, cooling, and preserving food and beverages
Logic_Trace: Class 11 explicitly lists "cooking, heating, cooling, ... apparatus and installations." This is a direct and complete match for all aspects of the term relating to food and beverages.

Purpose: Illuminating spaces and reflecting light for illumination purposes
Expanded_Name: Apparatus and components for lighting and lighting reflectors
Logic_Trace: Class 11 explicitly lists "Apparatus and installations for lighting." This perfectly covers lighting equipment. Lighting reflectors are components designed to reflect light for illumination purposes, fitting directly with lighting apparatus. Safety reflectors (e.g., for bicycles), if not integral to a lighting fixture, could be Class 9, but in this context, they are tied to 'lighting'.

Purpose: Facilitating cosmetic sun tanning
Expanded_Name: Electrical appliances for sun tanning
Logic_Trace: Class 11 includes "heating... apparatus and installations." Sun tanning appliances primarily use heat and UV radiation to achieve a tanning effect, which is a form of treatment of the body for cosmetic purposes, fitting within the scope of Class 11's general treatment apparatus. Lotions and sunscreens are in Class 3 or 5.

Original_Term: Sanitary installations, water supply and sanitation equipment
Purpose: Providing water supply, ensuring sanitation, and facilitating waste disposal
Expanded_Name: Installations, apparatus, and equipment for sanitary purposes, water supply, and sanitation
Logic_Trace: Class 11 explicitly lists "water supply and sanitary purposes" apparatus and installations. This is a direct and complete match for the term.

Purpose: Being used as a material for writing, printing, packaging, and various other paper-based applications
Expanded_Name: Raw and semi-processed paper and cardboard materials
Logic_Trace: Class 16 explicitly begins with "Paper and cardboard." This is a direct and perfect match for the term, referring to the raw or semi-processed materials.

Original_Term: Insulation and barrier articles and materials
Nature: Semi-processed materials, such as rubber, gutta-percha, gum, asbestos, mica, plastics, and resins, in various forms.
Purpose: Preventing the transfer of heat, cold, sound, or electricity, and forming protective barriers against liquids or gases.
Expanded_Name: Insulating and barrier articles and materials, including semi-processed rubber, plastics, and other substances, for preventing transfer and forming protective barriers.
Logic_Trace: The Class 17 description explicitly mentions "insulating materials" and "packing, stopping and insulating materials." This directly confirms the meanings related to materials for preventing transfer and forming protective barriers. The meaning of protective clothing is rejected as it falls under Class 9 (protective clothing) or Class 25 (regular clothing).

Nature: Flexible materials or compounds, such as rubber, gutta-percha, or plastics, in the form of gaskets, strips, or viscous substances.
Purpose: Creating airtight, watertight, or dust-proof closures, and filling gaps or cracks to prevent leakage or infiltration.
Expanded_Name: Seals, sealants, and fillers made of materials like rubber or plastics, for creating closures and filling gaps.
Logic_Trace: The Class 17 description includes "packing, stopping and insulating materials," which aligns with the function of seals, sealants, and fillers. This confirms the meanings related to creating closures and filling gaps. The meaning of marine mammals is rejected as irrelevant to Class 17.

Original_Term: Works of art and figurines of paper and cardboard, and architects' models
Nature: Decorative objects, sculptures, figurines, and three-dimensional models, all primarily constructed from paper or cardboard.
Purpose: Artistic expression, decoration, and architectural representation or visualization.
Expanded_Name: Works of art and decorative figurines made of paper and cardboard, and architects' models for design and presentation.
Logic_Trace: Class 16 explicitly covers "paper and cardboard" and "materials for artists," which perfectly matches works of art and figurines made from these materials. The term also directly specifies "architects' models." This confirms both meanings related to paper-based art and architectural representations.

Original_Term: Decoration and art materials and media
Nature: Various physical supplies and substances, including paper, drawing tools, paints, and decorative sheets.
Purpose: Creating artistic works, crafting, and decorative applications.
Expanded_Name: Decoration and art materials and media, including paper, drawing supplies, and decorative components for artistic creation.
Logic_Trace: Class 16 explicitly includes "drawing materials and materials for artists" and mentions "paper and cardboard." This strongly supports the meaning of supplies for creating art and certain physical decorative materials. Digital tools are outside the scope of this class.

Original_Term: Bags and articles for packaging, wrapping and storage, of paper, cardboard or plastics
Nature: Containers, sheets, films, and other articles made of paper, cardboard, or plastics.
Purpose: Packaging, wrapping, and storing various goods and materials.
Expanded_Name: Bags, films, sheets, and other articles of paper, cardboard, or plastics for packaging, wrapping, and storage.
Logic_Trace: The Class 16 description specifically lists "paper and cardboard" and "plastic sheets, films and bags for wrapping and packaging." This directly confirms the meanings related to containers and wrapping materials for packaging, wrapping, and storage. Fashion accessories are rejected as they typically fall under Class 18.

Nature: Assorted physical items, including paper, writing instruments, notebooks, instructional aids, and drawing tools.
Purpose: Facilitating writing, drawing, office functions, and supporting educational instruction and learning processes.
Expanded_Name: Stationery and educational supplies, including writing materials, office requisites, and instructional teaching aids.
Logic_Trace: Class 16 clearly states "stationery and office requisites" and "instructional and teaching materials." This perfectly matches the meanings related to writing/office materials and educational aids. School uniforms are rejected as they fall under Class 25.

Original_Term: Bags and sacks for packaging, storage and transport
Nature: Containers, such as sacks and bags, typically made of textile, synthetic, or natural fibers.
Purpose: Packaging, storing, and transporting materials, often in bulk.
Expanded_Name: Bags and sacks, not of paper, cardboard, rubber or plastics, for packaging, storage, and transport of materials in bulk.
Logic_Trace: Class 22 explicitly includes "sacks for the transport and storage of materials in bulk." This directly corresponds to the meaning of large containers for bulk materials, often made of textile or synthetic fibers (as paper/plastic bags are Class 16). Small shopping bags might fall here if textile, but the emphasis is on larger, bulk capacity. Travel luggage is rejected as it falls under Class 18.

Nature: Physical objects, apparatus, and interactive systems.
Purpose: Providing entertainment, recreation, and amusement through play and interaction.
Expanded_Name: Toys, games, and playthings, including video game apparatus, for recreational purposes and amusement.
Logic_Trace: Class 28 explicitly lists "Games, toys and playthings" and "video game apparatus." This comprehensively covers all aspects of objects and activities designed for entertainment, recreation, and amusement.

Original_Term: Fish, seafood and molluscs, not live
Nature: Non-live animal products originating from aquatic environments, such as fish, crustaceans, and molluscs.
Purpose: Human consumption as food.
Expanded_Name: Non-live fish, seafood, and molluscs, processed or unprocessed, for human consumption.
Logic_Trace: Class 29 specifically includes "meat, fish, poultry and game" and the term explicitly says "not live." This clearly points to edible, non-live aquatic animal products for human consumption. Live marine life (e.g., aquarium fish) is Class 31, and fishing equipment is Class 28.

Nature: Food products derived from milk (animal or plant-based) and their processed forms.
Purpose: Human consumption as food, beverages, or ingredients.
Expanded_Name: Dairy products (e.g., milk, cheese, yogurt) and plant-based dairy substitutes for food purposes.
Logic_Trace: Class 29 lists "milk, cheese, butter, yogurt and other milk products," which directly covers traditional dairy. The inclusion of "dairy substitutes" extends this to plant-based alternatives, which are also food products. Farm equipment is rejected as it falls under Class 7 or 8.

Nature: Unprocessed and processed food items derived from birds' eggs.
Purpose: Human consumption as food or as food ingredients.
Expanded_Name: Birds' eggs and processed egg products for human consumption.
Logic_Trace: Class 29 explicitly lists "eggs." The term specifies "Birds’ eggs and egg products," clearly indicating food items derived from avian sources. Decorative eggs are rejected as they typically fall under Class 28 or Class 20.

Purpose: Engaging in sports, gymnastics, physical training, and other athletic activities.
Expanded_Name: Sporting articles and equipment for gymnastic and athletic activities.
Logic_Trace: Class 28 explicitly includes "gymnastic and sporting articles." This confirms the meaning of gear and apparatus used for sports. Athletic clothing is rejected as it falls under Class 25, and sports facilities are services (e.g., Class 41).

Original_Term: Textile goods, and substitutes for textile goods
Nature: Woven, knitted, felted, or other fabricated materials made from natural or synthetic fibers, and materials designed to mimic them.
Purpose: For use in manufacturing other goods, upholstery, household linen, or general textile applications.
Expanded_Name: Textile goods and materials, including natural and synthetic fabrics, and non-woven substitutes for textiles.
Logic_Trace: Class 24 explicitly states "Textiles and substitutes for textiles." This directly matches the meaning of fabrics made from fibers and materials designed to mimic them. Finished garments are rejected as they typically fall under Class 25.

Original_Term: Agricultural and aquacultural crops, horticulture and forestry products
Nature: Raw, unprocessed biological products, including plant matter (e.g., grains, seeds, fresh produce, trees) and aquacultural products.
Purpose: Cultivation, consumption (as raw produce), or as raw materials for further processing.
Expanded_Name: Raw and unprocessed agricultural, aquacultural, horticultural, and forestry products, including crops, plants, and seeds for cultivation or raw consumption.
Logic_Trace: Class 31 explicitly lists "Raw and unprocessed agricultural, aquacultural, horticultural and forestry products," including "raw and unprocessed grains and seeds," "fresh fruits and vegetables," and "natural plants and flowers." This strongly supports the meaning of raw, unprocessed biological products. Processed food products are rejected as they fall under Class 29 or 30, and tools/machinery under Class 7 or 8.

Nature: Living biological entities, including animals and other organisms capable of reproduction.
Purpose: Keeping as pets, livestock, for breeding purposes, or other live applications such as cultivation.
Expanded_Name: Live animals and living organisms intended for breeding, cultivation, or other live applications.
Logic_Trace: Class 31 explicitly lists "live animals." The phrase "organisms for breeding" further clarifies the purpose for some of these live entities within the class, such as breeding stock or cultivation organisms. Preserved specimens are rejected as they typically fall under Class 20.

Nature: Prepared edible food substances in liquid, semi-solid, or paste form.
Purpose: Decorating, coating, and filling baked goods, pastries, confectionery, and desserts.
Expanded_Name: Edible sweet glazes and fillings for use in confectionery and baked goods.
Logic_Trace: Class 30 covers "bread, pastries and confectionery" and explicitly mentions "sugar, honey, treacle" and other sweet ingredients. While "sweet glazes and fillings" aren't individually listed, they are integral components of confectionery and baked goods in this class, fitting well within its scope. Sweet syrups for beverages are also in Class 30.

Purpose: Human consumption as food, typically as desserts, snacks, or breakfast items.
Expanded_Name: Baked confectionery goods, specifically pastries, cakes, tarts, and biscuits (cookies), for human consumption.
Logic_Trace: Class 30 explicitly includes "bread, pastries and confectionery." The listed items (pastries, cakes, tarts, biscuits/cookies) are specific types of baked sweet goods, fitting perfectly within this category for human consumption. Savory baked goods, while often Class 30, are not the primary focus of these specific sweet items.

Original_Term: Processed grains, starches, and goods made thereof, baking preparations and yeasts
Nature: Edible processed food items derived from grains and starches, and leavening agents.
Purpose: Human consumption as staple foods, ingredients in cooking and baking, or for producing other food products.

Original_Term: Coffee, teas and cocoa and substitutes therefor

Logic_Trace: Class 30 explicitly lists "Coffee, tea, cocoa and substitutes therefor." This directly covers the beverages and their replacement ingredients. Equipment for preparation is rejected as it falls under Class 11 (electric) or Class 21 (non-electric utensils).

Original_Term: Ice, ice creams, frozen yogurts and sorbets

Original_Term: Sugars, natural sweeteners, sweet coatings and fillings, bee products

Logic_Trace: Class 30 explicitly lists "sugar, honey, treacle" and generally covers "sweet coatings and fillings." This clearly matches the meanings of sweetening agents and confectionery components. Bee products are covered here if for food use. Bee products for medicinal use are rejected as they fall under Class 5.

Original_Term: Baked goods, confectionery, chocolate and desserts

Purpose: Enhancing and imparting flavour to food and beverages, and serving as accompaniments to meals.
Expanded_Name: Salts, seasonings, flavourings, and condiments for enhancing the taste of food and beverages.
Logic_Trace: Class 30 explicitly lists "salt, seasonings, spices, preserved herbs; vinegar, sauces and other condiments." This clearly matches the meanings of taste-enhancing substances and prepared accompaniments. While some may have preservative qualities, their primary function in this class is flavouring. Food preservatives as a primary function might be in Class 1 or 5.

Nature: Prepared or semi-prepared edible food products.
