Expanded_Name: Raw or semi-processed materials for brush-making
Logic_Trace: Class 21's description explicitly lists "brush-making materials", providing a direct match.

Purpose: Serving as a base material for manufacturing various rubber products.
Expanded_Name: Raw or semi-processed polymeric materials, namely, rubber
Logic_Trace: Class 17 explicitly lists "Unprocessed and semi-processed rubber", making this a direct match.

Purpose: Used as an insulating material, filler, or pigment in various industrial applications.
Expanded_Name: Natural mineral material, namely, mica
Logic_Trace: Class 17 explicitly lists "mica", providing a direct match for the mineral.

Purpose: Measuring and mapping land features, elevations, and positions for construction, cartography, or land management.
Expanded_Name: Scientific instruments for surveying
Logic_Trace: Class 9 explicitly lists "surveying apparatus and instruments" as part of its broad coverage of scientific and measuring instruments, making this a direct match.

Purpose: Applying ink to a surface for writing or drawing.
Expanded_Name: Stationery writing instruments, namely, pens
Logic_Trace: Class 16 includes "stationery and office requisites". Pens are a fundamental item of stationery, fitting this description.

Original_Term: Cartridges of staples for industrial stapling machines
Purpose: Providing fastening elements for industrial stapling machines.
Expanded_Name: Consumable components for industrial stapling machines, namely, cartridges of staples
Logic_Trace: Class 7 covers "Machines, machine tools, power-operated tools" and their components. Since these cartridges are specifically for 'industrial stapling machines' (which are Class 7), they are considered essential consumable components of those machines.

Purpose: Providing transportation and movement across water surfaces or underwater.
Expanded_Name: Apparatus for locomotion by water, namely, water vehicles
Logic_Trace: Class 12 explicitly covers "Vehicles; apparatus for locomotion by land, air or water", making 'water vehicles' a direct match for 'apparatus for locomotion by water'.

Purpose: Facilitating the soldering process, typically by cleaning surfaces and improving solder flow.
Expanded_Name: Chemical preparations for soldering
Logic_Trace: Class 1 explicitly lists "tempering and soldering preparations" as chemicals for industrial use, providing a direct match.

Purpose: Carrying objects over the shoulder or forming parts of harnesses
Expanded_Name: Shoulder belts [straps] of leather or imitation leather
Logic_Trace: Class 18 explicitly covers "luggage and carrying bags" and "harness and saddlery". It does not cover safety equipment for humans or vehicles. Therefore, meaning 1 is rejected. Meanings 2 and 3 are correct within Class 18.

Purpose: Being worn as the outermost layer of clothing
Expanded_Name: Outer garments
Logic_Trace: Class 25 is simply "Clothing, footwear, headwear". "Outerclothing" most directly refers to garments worn on the outside over other clothes, consistent with the general term "clothing" in this class. Meanings 2 and 3 are specific types, but the general meaning 1 is the most direct and accurate.

Purpose: Serving as raw material for textile manufacturing
Expanded_Name: Unfinished fabrics for textile production
Logic_Trace: Class 24 is "Textiles and substitutes for textiles". The term "Fabrics for textile use" clearly indicates raw or semi-finished materials used to make textiles or textile products, not the finished products themselves. Meaning 1 is the most accurate. Meanings 2 and 3 are too specific or refer to finished goods.

Purpose: Cleaning surfaces using high-pressure steam
Expanded_Name: Power-operated steam cleaning machines
Logic_Trace: Class 7 covers "Machines" and "power-operated tools". Steam cleaning machines, regardless of industrial or household scale, are power-operated machines for cleaning. Meaning 3 refers to the power source itself, not the cleaning apparatus. Both 1 and 2 are types of steam cleaning machines, fitting the general description of "machines".

Original_Term: Protective masks for use by persons working in the dentistry
Purpose: Safeguarding dental professionals from airborne contaminants
Expanded_Name: Protective masks for dental practitioners
Logic_Trace: Class 10 explicitly covers "dental apparatus and instruments". The term specifies "for use by persons working in the dentistry", making meaning 2 perfectly aligned. Meaning 1 is too general. Meaning 3, while dental, is for patients, not "persons working in the dentistry".

Original_Term: Protective mouth masks for dental use
Purpose: Protecting the mouth area during dental procedures
Expanded_Name: Protective mouth masks for dental professionals
Logic_Trace: Class 10 includes "dental apparatus and instruments". The term "mouth masks for dental use" directly indicates masks used in a dental context. "Protective" implies protection for the wearer from the environment, typically a practitioner. Given similar terms in this class, it refers to PPE for practitioners.

Original_Term: Examination couches specially made for dental use
Purpose: Positioning patients for dental examinations and procedures
Expanded_Name: Dental examination couches
Logic_Trace: Class 10 explicitly lists "dental apparatus and instruments". The phrase "specially made for dental use" confirms that these couches are adapted for dental procedures, fitting within this class as dental apparatus. Meaning 1 is too general, and meaning 3 is general furniture, not "apparatus".

Original_Term: Furniture especially made for dental use
Purpose: Supporting dental practices and procedures
Expanded_Name: Specialized furniture for dental offices and procedures
Logic_Trace: Class 10 includes "dental apparatus and instruments". "Furniture especially made for dental use" refers to items integral to the dental practice, not just decorative or general office furniture. This would include dental chairs, cabinets for instruments, etc., which are considered 'apparatus' in this context. Meaning 2 aligns best.

Purpose: Providing seating for dentists during procedures
Expanded_Name: Ergonomic chairs for dentists' use during procedures
Logic_Trace: Class 10 covers "dental apparatus and instruments". While "dentists' armchairs" could ambiguously refer to staff relaxation chairs, the context of Class 10 strongly implies specialized functional equipment. This refers to the ergonomic chairs dentists themselves use for working. Meaning 3 fits.

Original_Term: Armchairs for medical or dental purposes
Purpose: Supporting medical or dental examinations and procedures
Expanded_Name: Armchairs for medical or dental examinations and treatment
Logic_Trace: Class 10 specifically covers "medical, dental apparatus and instruments". The term "for medical or dental purposes" clearly indicates functionality beyond general furniture. This refers to chairs designed for examinations, treatments, or for the medical/dental personnel performing them. Meaning 3 aligns.

Original_Term: Examination chairs specially made for dental use
Purpose: Positioning patients for dental examinations and treatments
Expanded_Name: Specialized examination chairs for dental patients
Logic_Trace: Class 10 includes "dental apparatus and instruments". "Specially made for dental use" makes it clear this is specialized equipment for patient positioning in a dental context. Meaning 2 is the most direct and accurate.

Purpose: Maintaining hygiene and protecting hands during dental procedures
Expanded_Name: Protective gloves for dental procedures
Logic_Trace: Class 10 covers "dental apparatus and instruments". "Gloves for dental use" clearly refers to the protective barrier worn by dental professionals. These are considered instruments/articles in this class. Meaning 2 is correct.

Original_Term: Protective bonnets for the hair for wear by dental practitioners
Purpose: Containing hair and preventing contamination during dental procedures
Expanded_Name: Disposable protective bonnets for dental practitioners
Logic_Trace: Class 10 includes "dental apparatus and instruments". The term specifies "for wear by dental practitioners", making it a specific piece of protective attire for healthcare professionals in dentistry. Meaning 2 is the closest and most appropriate, tailored to dentistry.

Original_Term: Armchairs especially made for dental purposes
Purpose: Facilitating dental examinations and treatments
Expanded_Name: Armchairs specifically designed for dental purposes
Logic_Trace: Class 10 covers "dental apparatus and instruments". The phrase "especially made for dental purposes" indicates specific functionality within the dental field, referring to specialized chairs for patient treatment or practitioner use. Meaning 3 is correct.

Purpose: Shielding the face and eyes during dental procedures
Expanded_Name: Protective visors for dental practitioners
Logic_Trace: Class 10 includes "dental apparatus and instruments". "For dental use" specifies their application within dentistry. This is personal protective equipment for dental professionals. Meaning 3 is correct.

Original_Term: Protective nose masks for dental use
Purpose: Protecting the nasal area during dental procedures
Expanded_Name: Protective nose masks for dental professionals
Logic_Trace: Class 10 includes "dental apparatus and instruments". "Protective nose masks for dental use" implies PPE for professionals in the dental field. This aligns with other protective gear in this class. Meaning 3 is correct.

Original_Term: Protective face masks for dental use
Purpose: Protecting the entire face from contaminants during dental procedures
Expanded_Name: Protective face masks for dental practitioners
Logic_Trace: Class 10 covers "dental apparatus and instruments". "Protective face masks for dental use" clearly refers to PPE for dental professionals. Meaning 3 is correct.

Original_Term: Dental tray covers made of paper
Purpose: Covering dental instrument trays for hygiene and protection
Expanded_Name: Disposable paper covers for dental trays
Logic_Trace: Class 10 includes "dental apparatus and instruments". "Dental tray covers" are consumable articles used to maintain hygiene and protect dental instruments on trays, making them integral to dental procedures. "Made of paper" specifies the material. Meaning 2 is correct.

Purpose: Being consumed as food
Expanded_Name: Prepared anchovy, not live, for food
Logic_Trace: Class 29 explicitly lists "fish". The term "not live" confirms it's for consumption and not live animals. This fits perfectly with meaning 2. Meanings 1 and 3 are rejected by "not live" or the context of food.

Purpose: Being consumed as food
Expanded_Name: Prepared prawns, not live, for food
Logic_Trace: Class 29 includes "fish" (and generally seafood). "Not live" specifies it's for consumption. Meaning 2 is correct.

Purpose: Being consumed as food
Expanded_Name: Prepared tuna, not live, for food
Logic_Trace: Class 29 includes "fish". "Not live" confirms it's for consumption. Meaning 2 is correct.

Purpose: Being consumed as food
Expanded_Name: Prepared salmon, not live, for food
Logic_Trace: Class 29 includes "fish". "Not live" confirms it's for consumption. Meaning 2 is correct.

Purpose: Being consumed as food
Expanded_Name: Prepared sardines, not live, for food
Logic_Trace: Class 29 includes "fish". "Not live" confirms it's for consumption. Meaning 2 is correct.

Original_Term: Caskets made of mother of pearl
Purpose: Storing small items or for decorative display
Expanded_Name: Decorative caskets [small boxes] made of mother-of-pearl
Logic_Trace: Class 20 includes "containers, not of metal, for storage or transport" and specifically lists "mother-of-pearl" as a material. "Caskets" here refers to small boxes, not coffins, given the context of materials like mother-of-pearl and the general scope of Class 20. Meanings 2 and 3 fit.

Purpose: Storing small items or for decorative display
Expanded_Name: Decorative caskets [small boxes] made of whalebone
Logic_Trace: Class 20 includes "containers, not of metal, for storage or transport" and "whalebone". Similar to the previous item, "caskets" here refers to small boxes for storage or decoration. Meaning 2 is correct.

Purpose: Storing small items or for decorative display
Expanded_Name: Decorative caskets [small boxes] made of horn
Logic_Trace: Class 20 includes "containers, not of metal, for storage or transport" and "horn" as a material. "Caskets" refers to small boxes. Meaning 2 is correct.

Purpose: Storing small items or for decorative display
Expanded_Name: Decorative caskets [small boxes] made of meerschaum
Logic_Trace: Class 20 includes "containers, not of metal, for storage or transport" and "meerschaum" as a material. "Caskets" refers to small boxes. Meaning 2 is correct.

Purpose: Storing small items or for decorative display
Expanded_Name: Decorative caskets [small boxes] made of ivory
Logic_Trace: Class 20 includes "containers, not of metal, for storage or transport" and "bone" (ivory falls under bone materials, often treated similarly in classification). "Caskets" refers to small boxes. Meaning 2 is correct.

Purpose: Storing small items or for decorative display
Expanded_Name: Decorative caskets [small boxes] made of amber
Logic_Trace: Class 20 includes "containers, not of metal, for storage or transport" and "yellow amber" as a material. "Caskets" refers to small boxes. Meaning 2 is correct.

Purpose: Storing small items or for decorative display
Expanded_Name: Decorative caskets [small boxes] made of shell
Logic_Trace: Class 20 includes "containers, not of metal, for storage or transport" and "shells" as a material. "Caskets" refers to small boxes. Meaning 2 is correct.

Purpose: Holding human remains for burial or cremation
Expanded_Name: Funerary caskets made of bone
Logic_Trace: Class 20 includes "containers, not of metal, for storage or transport" and mentions "unworked or semi-worked bone". A casket as a container, even if for human remains, aligns with the general container nature of this class when made of bone. The third meaning is the most appropriate general fit for the class.

Original_Term: Provision of access to electronic sites
Purpose: Enabling users to connect to and retrieve information from electronic sites
Expanded_Name: Telecommunications services for providing access to electronic sites
Logic_Trace: Class 38 is 'Telecommunications services'. Providing access to electronic sites is a core telecommunications service, such as internet access or secure network access. Meanings related to physical access or mere authentication are rejected as they fall outside the scope of telecommunications.

Purpose: Providing financial protection against risks related to marine transportation and operations
Expanded_Name: Insurance services for marine transport and risks
Logic_Trace: Class 36 explicitly covers 'insurance services'. Marine insurance is a recognized category of insurance providing financial protection against perils of the sea and marine-related activities, directly matching the class description.

Original_Term: Trays for sorting and counting money
Purpose: Facilitating the manual sorting and counting of banknotes and coins
Expanded_Name: Office requisites, namely, trays for sorting and counting money
Logic_Trace: Class 16 includes 'stationery and office requisites, except furniture'. Trays used for sorting and counting money are typically small, non-mechanical items made of materials like plastic or cardboard, fitting the description of office requisites. Software (Class 9) or industrial machinery (Class 7) are rejected.

Purpose: Providing financial coverage for medical expenses and healthcare services
Expanded_Name: Insurance services, namely, health insurance
Logic_Trace: Class 36 explicitly covers 'insurance services'. Health insurance is a direct and widely understood form of insurance that provides financial coverage for medical treatment and healthcare services, perfectly aligning with the class.

Purpose: Facilitating financial transactions, currency management, and other monetary operations
Expanded_Name: Financial and monetary services
Logic_Trace: Class 36 specifically includes 'Financial, monetary and banking services'. The term 'Monetary services' is a direct match to the class description, referring to services involving money transactions, management, and related financial operations.

Purpose: Recording, summarizing, and maintaining financial transactions for businesses or individuals
Expanded_Name: Business administrative services, namely, book-keeping
Logic_Trace: Class 35 covers 'business management, organization and administration; office functions'. Book-keeping, as a service of recording and maintaining financial records, is a fundamental office function and administrative service, fitting perfectly within this class. Physical books (Class 16) or software (Class 9) are rejected.

Nature: Protective apparatus in the form of clothing
Purpose: Protecting the wearer's body from impact, projectiles, or other physical threats
Expanded_Name: Protective clothing, namely, body armour for personal protection
Logic_Trace: Class 9 includes 'life-saving and teaching apparatus and instruments' and specifically lists 'diving suits'. Body armour, providing protection against physical threats, falls under the category of protective apparatus for personal safety. Regular clothing (Class 25) or device cases are rejected.

Purpose: For breeding, stocking aquariums, or other live animal trade
Expanded_Name: Live fish
Logic_Trace: Class 31 explicitly lists 'live animals'. 'Fish, live' directly matches this description, referring to fish kept alive for purposes such as breeding, stocking, or sale as pets. Dead fish for consumption would be Class 29.

Purpose: Providing nutritional supplementation, dental hygiene, or entertainment for animals
Expanded_Name: Edible animal foodstuffs in the nature of chews
Logic_Trace: Class 31 explicitly lists 'foodstuffs and beverages for animals'. 'Edible chews for animals' are a type of foodstuff designed for animal consumption, fitting perfectly within this class. Non-edible chew toys would be Class 28.

Purpose: Supplementing the protein content of animal feed
Expanded_Name: Wheat proteins being ingredients for animal food
Logic_Trace: Class 31 explicitly lists 'foodstuffs and beverages for animals'. 'Wheat proteins for animal food' are protein ingredients specifically intended for animal consumption, directly matching the class description. Wheat proteins for human food (Class 29/30) or raw grains (Class 31 but not processed protein) are rejected.

Purpose: Providing a treat or supplemental food for dogs
Expanded_Name: Biscuits being foodstuffs for dogs
Logic_Trace: Class 31 explicitly lists 'foodstuffs and beverages for animals'. Dog biscuits are a clear example of prepared foodstuffs intended for animal consumption, fitting perfectly within this class. Biscuits for human consumption (Class 30) are rejected.

Purpose: For breeding, farming, or other live animal trade
Expanded_Name: Live poultry
Logic_Trace: Class 31 explicitly lists 'live animals'. 'Poultry, live' directly matches this description, referring to live birds kept for farming, breeding, or other purposes. Dead poultry for human consumption would be Class 29.

Purpose: Providing nutritional feed for poultry
Expanded_Name: Groats being foodstuffs for poultry
Logic_Trace: Class 31 explicitly lists 'foodstuffs and beverages for animals'. 'Groats for poultry' are hulled and crushed grains specifically intended as feed for birds, directly aligning with this category. Groats for human consumption would be Class 30.

Purpose: Storing and transporting sails
Expanded_Name: Bags for transporting and storing sails
Logic_Trace: Class 22 includes 'sails' and 'sacks for the transport and storage of materials in bulk'. A bag specifically for sails functions as a container for transport and storage of material (sails), often made of textile or synthetic materials, fitting well within this class.

Purpose: Housing and displaying aquatic animals and plants
Expanded_Name: Glass containers, namely, aquariums
Logic_Trace: Class 21 covers 'Household or kitchen utensils and containers' and 'glassware'. An aquarium, being a glass tank used in households for keeping aquatic life, functions as a container and is typically made of glass, fitting the nature of the goods in this class.

Purpose: For planting and cultivation as ornamental plants
Expanded_Name: Live rose bushes
Logic_Trace: Class 31 explicitly lists 'natural plants and flowers' and 'bulbs, seedlings and seeds for planting'. Live rose bushes are natural plants intended for planting and cultivation, directly matching the class description. Artificial plants (Class 26) or dried plants are rejected.

Purpose: For planting and growing flowering plants
Expanded_Name: Plant propagation material, namely, flower bulbs
Logic_Trace: Class 31 explicitly lists 'bulbs, seedlings and seeds for planting'. Flower bulbs are a type of plant propagation material, directly matching the class description. Light bulbs (Class 11) or decorative items (Class 20 or 26) are rejected.

Purpose: For planting, use as animal feed, or further processing into food products
Expanded_Name: Raw and unprocessed cereal grains
Logic_Trace: Class 31 explicitly lists 'raw and unprocessed grains and seeds'. 'Grains [cereals]' directly refers to raw, unprocessed cereal grains, perfectly aligning with the class description. Processed cereals for human consumption (Class 30) are rejected.

Purpose: Providing an absorbent material for animal waste in enclosures
Expanded_Name: Animal care products, namely, litter for animals
Logic_Trace: Class 31 includes 'foodstuffs and beverages for animals' and generally covers products for live animals. Animal litter, often derived from natural or processed plant materials, serves a direct function in the care and welfare of live animals, thus fitting the scope of this class for animal-related raw or minimally processed goods.

Original_Term: Editing or recording of sounds and images
Purpose: Creating, modifying, and preserving audio and visual content for entertainment or cultural purposes
Expanded_Name: Entertainment services, namely, editing or recording of sounds and images
Logic_Trace: Class 41 covers 'entertainment; sporting and cultural activities'. Services related to the production, editing, and recording of sounds and images are integral to the creation of entertainment and cultural content, thus fitting squarely within this class. Software (Class 9) or devices (Class 9) are rejected as they are not services.

Purpose: Creating and producing video content, specifically on videotape
Expanded_Name: Entertainment services, namely, videotape production
Logic_Trace: Class 41 covers 'entertainment; sporting and cultural activities'. Videotape production, as a service, falls under the umbrella of creating entertainment or cultural content. The physical videotapes (Class 9) or recording equipment (Class 9) are rejected as they are not services.

Purpose: Displaying merchandise and organizing retail spaces
Expanded_Name: Non-metallic furniture, namely, shop display fittings
Logic_Trace: Class 20 includes 'Furniture'. Non-metallic shop display fittings such as shelves, racks, or stands function as furniture for arranging and presenting merchandise in a retail environment. The 'non-metallic' distinction is crucial, as metallic equivalents would be in Class 6.

Nature: Covers made of textile or synthetic materials
Purpose: Protecting goods, areas, or structures from weather elements or dust
Expanded_Name: Heavy-duty waterproof protective covers, namely, tarpaulins
Logic_Trace: Class 22 explicitly lists 'tarpaulins'. This is a direct match, indicating heavy-duty protective covers made of textile or synthetic materials.

Purpose: For use as costume accessories or for theatrical performances
Expanded_Name: False hair articles, namely, false beards
Logic_Trace: Class 26 includes 'false hair'. False beards are a specific type of false hair, typically used as costume accessories or for theatrical performances, fitting well within this category of artificial adornments.

Purpose: For decorative display or ornamental purposes
Expanded_Name: Decorative imitation articles, namely, artificial fruit
Logic_Trace: Class 26 includes 'artificial flowers'. Artificial fruit, being decorative imitation items, aligns with artificial flowers as items for ornamentation or decoration, rather than actual food (Class 29/30) or genetically modified produce (Class 31).

Purpose: Aiding in the application of hair color or highlights
Expanded_Name: Hair accessories, namely, caps for hair colouring
Logic_Trace: Class 26 includes 'hair decorations' and general haberdashery. Hair colouring caps are accessories specifically used in conjunction with hair, functioning as a hair accessory during treatment. This aligns with the class's scope for small personal adornment items and accessories for hair, rather than general clothing headwear (Class 25).

Purpose: Adorning clothing or other fashion items
Expanded_Name: Clothing accessories, namely, birds' feathers
Logic_Trace: Class 26 covers 'lace, braid and embroidery, and haberdashery ribbons and bows' which are types of clothing accessories. Birds' feathers, specifically indicated as 'clothing accessories', fit directly into this class as adornments for garments or other fashion items.

Purpose: Weaving and repairing fishing nets
Expanded_Name: Hand tools, namely, shuttles for making fishing nets
Logic_Trace: Class 26 includes 'pins and needles' and items for lace, braid, and embroidery. Shuttles for making nets are small hand tools used in a craft or handicraft activity similar to needlework, fitting the general nature of small tools and haberdashery in this class.

Original_Term: Beads, other than for making jewellery
Purpose: For use in crafts, clothing embellishment, or hair adornment
Expanded_Name: Decorative articles, namely, beads other than for making jewellery
Logic_Trace: Class 26 includes 'buttons, hooks and eyes, pins and needles' and 'hair decorations'. Beads, when not for fine jewelry (which would be Class 14), are commonly used as decorative elements for clothing, hair, or general crafts, fitting the haberdashery and decorative article nature of this class.

Purpose: Covering the head for cosmetic or fashion purposes, or to conceal baldness.
Expanded_Name: Artificial hair coverings for cosmetic purposes
Logic_Trace: The NICE Class 26 description explicitly mentions 'false hair' and 'hair decorations'. 'Artificial hair coverings for the head' directly aligns with this, while other meanings are irrelevant to the class of goods.

Purpose: Securing trouser legs to prevent interference with bicycle chains.
Expanded_Name: Fastening clips for securing trouser legs for cyclists
Logic_Trace: NICE Class 26 covers 'buttons, hooks and eyes, pins and needles' and 'haberdashery ribbons and bows', indicating small functional or decorative articles for clothing. Trouser clips, as functional fasteners for clothing, fit this category.

Purpose: Worn for disguise, theatrical performances, or costume purposes.
Expanded_Name: Artificial facial hair for disguise or costume purposes
Logic_Trace: The NICE Class 26 description clearly states 'false hair'. False moustaches are a type of false hair designed to be worn on the face, making this meaning a direct match.

Purpose: Used as a material for making wigs, hair extensions, or hair decorations.
Expanded_Name: Raw human hair for making wigs and hair extensions
Logic_Trace: The NICE Class 26 description includes 'false hair' and 'hair decorations'. Human hair as a raw material for creating such items (e.g., extensions, wigs, braids) is consistent with the class goods.

Purpose: Used as hair extensions or decorative hair pieces.
Expanded_Name: Braided human hair for use as hair extensions or decorative pieces
Logic_Trace: The NICE Class 26 description includes 'false hair' and 'hair decorations'. Plaited hair, when sold as a product, typically refers to braided extensions or decorative pieces, fitting well within these categories.

Purpose: Marking or personalizing linen articles with letters.
Expanded_Name: Embroidered or sew-on letter appliques for marking linen
Logic_Trace: NICE Class 26 includes 'lace, braid and embroidery' and 'haberdashery'. Letters for marking linen, in the context of haberdashery, refers to embroidered or sew-on letters used for personalization or identification, aligning with the class description. Stencils or paper labels would fall into other classes.

Purpose: Marking or personalizing linen articles with numerals.
Expanded_Name: Embroidered or sew-on numeral appliques for marking linen
Logic_Trace: Similar to 'Letters for marking linen', NICE Class 26 indicates these are haberdashery items. Numerals for marking linen are embroidered or sew-on patches used for identification or personalization, consistent with the class.

Purpose: Storing sewing needles.
Expanded_Name: Cases for storing sewing needles
Logic_Trace: The NICE Class 26 description explicitly lists 'pins and needles', which directly refers to sewing needles. Therefore, needle cases in this context are for storing sewing needles, fitting the haberdashery theme.

Purpose: Covering partial baldness or adding volume to hair.
Expanded_Name: Partial hairpieces for covering baldness
Logic_Trace: The NICE Class 26 description explicitly includes 'false hair'. Toupees are a specific type of false hair, designed to cover partial baldness or add hair volume, making it a direct match.

Purpose: Filling gaps, sealing, or bonding in industrial applications.
Expanded_Name: Oil-based paste filler for industrial sealing and bonding
Logic_Trace: The NICE Class 1 description explicitly includes 'putties and other paste fillers' and 'adhesives for use in industry'. Oil cement, qualified as putty, directly fits as an industrial filler or adhesive.

Purpose: Sealing glass panes into window frames.
Expanded_Name: Pliable sealing compound for setting glass in frames
Logic_Trace: The NICE Class 1 description explicitly lists 'putties and other paste fillers'. Glaziers' putty is a specific type of putty used in construction for sealing glass, fitting this definition perfectly.

Purpose: Installing industrial machinery, equipment, or complete facilities.
Expanded_Name: Installation services for industrial machinery and plant
Logic_Trace: NICE Class 37 covers 'installation and repair services' and 'construction services'. In an industrial context, 'plant' refers to a factory or industrial equipment, making the installation service a direct fit. Planting botanical plants would also be in Class 37 but 'plant' often implies industrial context.

Purpose: Restoring machines to proper working order.
Expanded_Name: Repair services for machines
Logic_Trace: NICE Class 37 explicitly lists 'installation and repair services'. The 'repair of machines' is a direct and unambiguous match for a repair service within this class.

Original_Term: Organization of fashion shows for entertainment purposes
Purpose: Arranging and staging fashion shows to entertain audiences.
Expanded_Name: Organization services for fashion shows provided for entertainment purposes
Logic_Trace: NICE Class 41 includes 'entertainment' and 'cultural activities'. The organization of fashion shows specifically for 'entertainment purposes' is a clear and direct match for an entertainment service.

Purpose: Providing resistance to acid in industrial or scientific applications.
Expanded_Name: Chemical compositions designed for acid resistance in industrial applications
Logic_Trace: NICE Class 1 broadly covers 'Chemicals for use in industry, science and photography'. Acid proof chemical compositions are industrial chemicals designed for a specific resistance, fitting perfectly into this general chemical category.

Purpose: Used as a wax in various applications or as a fuel/illuminant.
Expanded_Name: Paraffin wax and liquid paraffin for industrial or fuel use
Logic_Trace: NICE Class 4 explicitly lists 'wax' and 'fuels and illuminants'. Paraffin, in its various forms (wax, oil), is directly covered by these categories.

Purpose: Used in various manufacturing processes or as a component in industrial products.
Expanded_Name: Waxy substance for industrial applications
Logic_Trace: NICE Class 4 explicitly includes 'Industrial oils and greases, wax'. 'Industrial wax' is a direct and specific match for the goods covered in this class.

Purpose: Used as a base ingredient or component in manufacturing various products.
Expanded_Name: Raw waxy material for industrial and manufacturing purposes
Logic_Trace: NICE Class 4 includes 'wax'. The clarification '[raw material]' confirms that it refers to wax in its fundamental form, suitable for various uses as an ingredient or component, aligning with the class description.

Purpose: Used as a fuel, illuminant, or industrial raw material.
Expanded_Name: Raw or refined petroleum for use as fuel and industrial raw material
Logic_Trace: NICE Class 4 lists 'fuels and illuminants' and 'Industrial oils and greases'. Petroleum, whether raw or refined, is a primary source for these, making it a clear fit for the class.

Purpose: Used as a lubricant, protective coating, or component in industrial applications.
Expanded_Name: Petroleum jelly for industrial lubrication and protective applications
Logic_Trace: NICE Class 4 includes 'Industrial oils and greases, wax; lubricants'. The qualification 'for industrial purposes' directs the interpretation to its use as an industrial lubricant or protective agent, aligning perfectly with the class.

Purpose: Used as a raw material in various industrial and scientific processes, particularly electronics and metallurgy.
Expanded_Name: Chemical element silicon for industrial and scientific applications
Logic_Trace: NICE Class 1 covers 'Chemicals for use in industry, science...'. Silicon, as a chemical element or in its raw forms for industrial applications (e.g., in metallurgy or as a raw material for semiconductors), fits this description. It's distinguished from the polymer 'silicone'.

Purpose: Separating oil from other substances in industrial processes.
Expanded_Name: Chemical compositions for separating oil in industrial applications
Logic_Trace: NICE Class 1 describes 'Chemicals for use in industry, science...'. Oil-separating chemicals are industrial chemicals specifically designed for a separation function, fitting directly into this broad chemical category.

Purpose: Playing computer games.
Expanded_Name: Downloadable computer software applications for playing games
Logic_Trace: The NICE Class 9 description explicitly includes 'recorded and downloadable media, computer software'. Downloadable computer game programs are a direct match as a type of computer software and downloadable media.

Purpose: Playing interactive video games.
Expanded_Name: Computer software programs for playing interactive video games
Logic_Trace: NICE Class 9 covers 'computer software' and 'recorded and downloadable media'. Interactive video game programs are a specific type of computer software, fitting this definition.

Purpose: Playing interactive computer games with multimedia content.
Expanded_Name: Computer software programs for playing interactive multimedia computer games
Logic_Trace: NICE Class 9 includes 'computer software' and 'recorded and downloadable media'. This term describes a specific type of computer game software that incorporates multimedia elements, aligning with the class.

Purpose: Playing video games.
Expanded_Name: Downloadable computer software programs for playing video games
Logic_Trace: NICE Class 9 includes 'recorded and downloadable media, computer software'. Downloadable video game programs are a direct fit as a type of computer software and downloadable media.

Purpose: Used for culinary, aromatic, or medicinal purposes.
Expanded_Name: Freshly harvested garden herbs for culinary use
Logic_Trace: NICE Class 31 explicitly lists 'fresh herbs'. 'Garden herbs, fresh' is a direct and precise match for the goods covered in this class.

Purpose: Used for culinary, aromatic, or medicinal purposes, produced via organic farming methods.
Expanded_Name: Freshly harvested organic herbs for culinary use
Logic_Trace: NICE Class 31 explicitly lists 'fresh herbs'. 'Organic fresh herbs' is a direct match, with 'organic' specifying the cultivation method without changing the fundamental nature as a fresh herb.

Original_Term: Apparatus for drawing up beer under pressure
Purpose: Dispensing beer from a pressurized container.
Expanded_Name: Mechanical apparatus for drawing and dispensing beer under pressure
Logic_Trace: NICE Class 7 covers 'Machines, machine tools, power-operated tools'. An 'apparatus for drawing up beer under pressure' clearly functions as a mechanical device for dispensing, thus fitting the definition of a machine in this class.

Purpose: Transmitting television programs to subscribers via cable networks.
Expanded_Name: Telecommunications service for broadcasting television programs via cable
Logic_Trace: NICE Class 38 is explicitly defined as 'Telecommunications services'. Cable television broadcasting is a direct and clear example of a telecommunications service, fitting this class perfectly.

Purpose: Providing aggregated price data for goods and services to assist purchasing decisions.
Expanded_Name: Information services for comparing commercial prices of goods and services
Logic_Trace: Class 35 is for 'Advertising; business management, organization and administration; office functions'. Meanings 1 and 2 align well with business management and advertising aspects of providing information. Meaning 3 (financial services) would be Class 36. Therefore, the service is about comparing commercial prices for goods or services for consumers or businesses.

Purpose: Facilitating real-time audio and visual communication between multiple parties over electronic networks.
Expanded_Name: Telecommunication services for enabling real-time video and audio conferences
Logic_Trace: Class 38 is 'Telecommunications services'. Meaning 1 perfectly fits this description as it's a communication service. Meanings 2 and 3 would fall under different classes (rental in 39/42, technical support in 42).

Purpose: Used for playing various card games.
Expanded_Name: Physical playing cards for games
Logic_Trace: Class 28 covers 'Games, toys and playthings'. Meaning 1, physical cards for games, fits perfectly. Meaning 2 (software) would be Class 9. Meaning 3 (collectible cards) could potentially fit if they are used in games, but the primary meaning for 'playing cards' is for traditional card games.

Purpose: Masking or eliminating bad breath in animals.
Expanded_Name: Non-medicated breath freshening preparations for animals
Logic_Trace: Class 3 specifically mentions 'Non-medicated cosmetics and toiletry preparations' and 'non-medicated dentifrices'. Meaning 1, non-medicated products for animal breath, aligns perfectly. Meaning 2 would be Class 5 (pharmaceuticals). Meaning 3 would be Class 5 (disinfectants) or Class 3 (air fresheners, but not specifically 'for animals' breath).

Original_Term: Deodorants, other than for human beings or for animals
Purpose: Neutralizing or masking odors in general environments, excluding direct application to humans or animals, often with a sanitizing function.
Expanded_Name: Deodorizing preparations, other than for human beings or for animals, for general sanitary purposes
Logic_Trace: Class 5 includes 'disinfectants' and 'preparations for destroying vermin; fungicides, herbicides'. The 'other than for human beings or for animals' suggests it's not a cosmetic (Class 3) or veterinary product (Class 5, but usually for direct application). Given Class 5's focus on sanitization and pest control, meaning 3, deodorants with a disinfectant or sanitary purpose, or general purpose deodorizers that don't fit into cosmetics, is the best fit. Meaning 1 could be Class 3 if purely cosmetic, but the exclusion makes Class 5 more probable if it has a sanitary function.

Purpose: Deterring animals from specific areas or surfaces.
Expanded_Name: Chemical formulations for repelling animals
Logic_Trace: Class 5 includes 'preparations for destroying vermin; fungicides, herbicides'. Animal repellents, particularly formulations, fit the category of chemical preparations intended to control animal presence, similar to vermin destroyers. Meaning 1, chemical substances, is the direct fit for 'formulations'. Meanings 2 (devices) would be another class (e.g., 9 or 11).

Purpose: Providing essential vitamins to animals for health and well-being.
Expanded_Name: Vitamin dietary supplements for animals
Logic_Trace: Class 5 explicitly lists 'dietary supplements for human beings and animals' and 'veterinary preparations'. Meaning 1 directly matches 'dietary supplements for animals'. Meaning 2 could be Class 31 (animal feed) if it's primarily feed, but 'vitamins for animals' specifically refers to the supplement aspect. Meaning 3, injections, is still a veterinary preparation. The core is the vitamin supplement.

Purpose: Supplying essential minerals to animals for nutritional support and health maintenance.
Expanded_Name: Mineral dietary supplements for animals
Logic_Trace: Class 5 explicitly lists 'dietary supplements for human beings and animals'. Meaning 1, mineral supplements, directly fits this. Meaning 2 could be Class 31 if primarily feed. Meaning 3, water additives, would also be a form of supplement.

Nature: Veterinary medical devices (collars) impregnated with antiparasitic preparations
Purpose: Administering antiparasitic agents to animals to control or eliminate parasites.
Expanded_Name: Collars for animals impregnated with antiparasitic preparations
Logic_Trace: Class 5 includes 'veterinary preparations' and 'preparations for destroying vermin'. Meaning 1, collars with antiparasitic substances, fits perfectly as a veterinary preparation or a form of vermin destruction. Meanings 2 and 3 would be Class 18 (animal collars) and not related to the 'antiparasitic' function.

Purpose: Cleaning and conditioning the fur and skin of pets.
Expanded_Name: Non-medicated shampoos for grooming pets
Logic_Trace: Class 3 clearly states 'Non-medicated cosmetics and toiletry preparations'. The bracketed 'non-medicated grooming preparations' in the term itself further confirms meaning 1. Meaning 2 would be Class 5 (pharmaceuticals). Meaning 3 would be Class 3 (cleaning preparations) but not specifically 'shampoos for pets'.

Original_Term: Advisory services relating to the care of animals
Purpose: Providing expert advice and guidance on the general care, health, and well-being of animals.
Expanded_Name: Professional advisory services relating to animal care
Logic_Trace: Class 44 explicitly lists 'veterinary services' and 'hygienic and beauty care for human beings or animals'. Providing advice on animal care falls directly under veterinary services or general animal welfare and husbandry, which Class 44 covers broadly. Meaning 1 fits perfectly. Meanings 2 (Class 36) and 3 (Class 45) are unrelated to animal care itself.

Original_Term: Advisory services relating to the care of pet animals
Purpose: Providing expert advice and guidance on the care, health, and welfare of pet animals.
Expanded_Name: Professional advisory services relating to the care of pet animals
Logic_Trace: Similar to the previous item, Class 44 includes 'veterinary services' and 'hygienic and beauty care for human beings or animals'. This term is a specific subset of the previous one, focusing on pets. Meaning 1 is the direct fit. Meanings 2 and 3 are business consulting (Class 35) or manufacturing consulting (Class 42) respectively.

Purpose: Providing power and illumination for various applications.
Expanded_Name: Electrical energy for power and illumination
Logic_Trace: Class 4 includes 'fuels and illuminants'. Electrical energy, when supplied, is considered an illuminant or fuel in the context of utilities, even though it's distinct from traditional fossil fuels. This classification often covers energy sources. Meaning 1 fits this interpretation. Meanings 2 (Class 7/11) and 3 (technical term, not a goods/service) are incorrect.

Original_Term: Technological services and design relating thereto
Purpose: Providing expert technological knowledge, research, development, and design solutions.
Expanded_Name: Scientific and technological services, including research and design, for various applications
Logic_Trace: Class 42 explicitly states 'Scientific and technological services and research and design relating thereto'. Meaning 1 is a direct match. Meaning 2 would be Class 37 (installation, maintenance) or 42 (IT consulting). Meaning 3 (sales) is Class 35.

Purpose: Forming walls, roofs, or other structural/covering elements in buildings.
Expanded_Name: Non-metallic building panels for construction
Logic_Trace: Class 19 covers 'Materials, not of metal, for building and construction'. Meaning 1 fits perfectly as building panels are structural or covering components. Meaning 2 could potentially fit if they are integrated into the building structure as a material, but 'building panels' usually implies a more substantial role. Meaning 3 (solar panels) would be Class 9 or 11.

Purpose: Forming the floor surfaces of buildings.
Expanded_Name: Non-metallic flooring materials for building construction
Logic_Trace: Class 19 covers 'Materials, not of metal, for building and construction'. Meaning 1 is a direct fit for building materials. Meaning 2 would be Class 37 (installation). Meaning 3 (specialized systems) might be different, but 'floors, not of metal' refers to the material component.

Purpose: Generating heat using gas as fuel for various applications (e.g., space heating, water heating).
Expanded_Name: Gas-fired heat generating apparatus
Logic_Trace: Class 11 explicitly includes 'heating' apparatus and installations. Meaning 1, appliances for producing heat from gas, fits perfectly. Meaning 2 (electricity generators) would be Class 7. Meaning 3 is unlikely to be called a 'heat generator'.

Nature: Apparatus for optimizing fuel efficiency in heating and power systems
Purpose: Reducing fuel consumption by recovering waste heat or improving combustion efficiency in heating and power installations.
Expanded_Name: Apparatus for economizing fuel in heating and steam generating installations
Logic_Trace: Class 11 includes 'heating' and 'steam generating' apparatus. Fuel economizers are often heat exchangers that recover waste heat from exhaust gases to preheat boiler feed water, thereby improving boiler efficiency. This makes them part of heating/steam generating installations. Meaning 1 is the correct fit. Meaning 2 (software) would be Class 9. Meaning 3 (additives) would be Class 1 (chemicals) or Class 4 (fuels).

Purpose: Providing light and ventilation while forming part of a building's structure.
Expanded_Name: Non-metallic windows for building construction
Logic_Trace: Class 19 covers 'Materials, not of metal, for building and construction'. Non-metallic windows are components of buildings made from non-metallic materials. Meaning 1 is the direct fit. Meaning 2 would be Class 37 (installation). Meaning 3 (coverings) would be Class 20 or 24.

Purpose: Edible food item, typically served as a dessert or condiment.
Expanded_Name: Preserved cranberry compote
Logic_Trace: Class 29 explicitly lists 'jellies, jams, compotes'. Meaning 1 is a direct match for the definition of compote. Meaning 2 would be Class 31 (fresh fruit). Meaning 3 would be Class 32 (beverages).

Original_Term: Agriculture, aquaculture, horticulture and forestry services
Purpose: Providing expertise and labor in the cultivation of plants, rearing of aquatic organisms, and management of forests.
Expanded_Name: Professional services in the fields of agriculture, aquaculture, horticulture, and forestry
Logic_Trace: Class 44 explicitly lists 'agriculture, aquaculture, horticulture and forestry services'. Meaning 1 is a direct and complete match. Meaning 2 (sales) is Class 35. Meaning 3 (manufacturing) is Class 7.

Purpose: Providing aesthetic and hygienic maintenance for animals, such as bathing, clipping, and styling.
Expanded_Name: Professional animal grooming services
Logic_Trace: Class 44 explicitly lists 'hygienic and beauty care for human beings or animals'. Meaning 1, services for grooming animals, fits directly under hygienic and beauty care. Meaning 2 (tools) would be various other classes (e.g., 8, 21). Meaning 3 (veterinary services) is also Class 44 but more specific than general grooming.

Purpose: Used as a corrosion-resistant material in manufacturing and construction.
Expanded_Name: Stainless steel alloy in raw or semi-wrought form
Logic_Trace: Class 6 covers 'Common metals and their alloys, ores'. Stainless steel is a specific alloy. Meaning 1 is the direct interpretation within this class. Meaning 2 (finished products) would fall into various other classes depending on the product (e.g., Class 8 for cutlery, Class 21 for kitchenware). Meaning 3 (scrap) would also be Class 6, but the primary term refers to the material itself.

Purpose: Used as a strong and durable material in manufacturing and construction.
Expanded_Name: Carbon steel alloy in raw or semi-wrought form
Logic_Trace: Class 6 covers 'Common metals and their alloys, ores'. Carbon steel is a specific alloy of iron. Meaning 1 is the direct interpretation within this class. Meaning 2 (finished tools) would be Class 8. Meaning 3 (carbon fibers) would be Class 17 or 22.

Purpose: Transporting golfers and their equipment on a golf course.
Expanded_Name: Motorized golf carts [vehicles]
Logic_Trace: Class 12 is for 'Vehicles; apparatus for locomotion by land, air or water'. The bracketed '[vehicles]' in the term itself confirms meaning 1. Meaning 2 (toys) would be Class 28. Meaning 3 (rental) would be Class 39.

Purpose: Transporting people or goods on water, propelled manually by oars.
Expanded_Name: Manually propelled rowing boats
Logic_Trace: Class 12 is for 'Vehicles; apparatus for locomotion by land, air or water', specifically water vehicles. Meaning 1 is a direct fit. Meaning 2 (toys) would be Class 28. Meaning 3 (services) would be Class 39.

Purpose: Transporting people on water, propelled manually by pedals driving a paddle wheel or directly by paddles.
Expanded_Name: Human-powered paddle boats [water vehicles]
Logic_Trace: Class 12 is for 'Vehicles; apparatus for locomotion by land, air or water', specifically water vehicles. Meaning 1, pedal-powered watercraft, is a common interpretation of 'paddle boats' (pedalos). Meaning 2 (canoes, kayaks) could also fit but 'paddle boats' often refers specifically to pedal boats. Meaning 3 (toys) would be Class 28.

Purpose: Transporting people or goods on water, propelled by wind acting on sails.
Expanded_Name: Sail-powered boats [water vehicles]
Logic_Trace: Class 12 is for 'Vehicles; apparatus for locomotion by land, air or water', specifically water vehicles. Meaning 1 is a direct fit. Meaning 2 (toys) would be Class 28. Meaning 3 (parts) would also be Class 12.

Purpose: Inflating tires and other pneumatic components of vehicles.
Expanded_Name: Air pumps as accessories for vehicles
Logic_Trace: Class 12 covers 'Vehicles; apparatus for locomotion by land, air or water'. The bracketed '[vehicle accessories]' directly links the air pump to vehicles, confirming meaning 1. General air pumps (meaning 2) would be Class 7 (machines) or Class 8 (hand tools). Meaning 3 (AC pumps) would be Class 7 (parts of machines).

Purpose: Transporting people or goods on water, typically propelled by single-bladed paddles.
Expanded_Name: Manually propelled canoes [water vehicles]
Logic_Trace: Class 12 is for 'Vehicles; apparatus for locomotion by land, air or water', specifically water vehicles. Meaning 1 is a direct fit. Meaning 2 (toys) would be Class 28. Meaning 3 (rental) would be Class 39.

Purpose: Securing luggage within or on vehicles
Expanded_Name: Luggage securing nets for vehicles
Logic_Trace: The NICE Class 12 description 'Vehicles; apparatus for locomotion by land, air or water' clearly indicates that the nets are intended for use with vehicles. Therefore, the meaning 'nets specifically designed for securing luggage within or on vehicles' is the correct one.

Purpose: Transporting skis on cars
Expanded_Name: Ski carrying racks for cars
Logic_Trace: The NICE Class 12 description 'Vehicles; apparatus for locomotion by land, air or water' and the explicit mention of 'cars' confirm that this term refers to devices attached to cars for transporting skis. Meaning 3 is correct, while meanings 1 and 2 are irrelevant.

Purpose: Transporting luggage on or in vehicles
Expanded_Name: Luggage carrying racks for vehicles
Logic_Trace: Given NICE Class 12 'Vehicles; apparatus for locomotion by land, air or water', the term refers to apparatus or accessories for vehicles, not people or general bags. Thus, meaning 3 is the correct interpretation.

Purpose: Transporting passengers or goods on water
Expanded_Name: Motor launches [boats]
Logic_Trace: NICE Class 12 'Vehicles; apparatus for locomotion by land, air or water' explicitly pertains to vehicles, including watercraft. This confirms that 'Launches' refers to small motorboats, fitting meaning 2, and rejecting other contexts.

Purpose: Transporting passengers or cargo across water
Expanded_Name: Waterborne ships
Logic_Trace: NICE Class 12 'Vehicles; apparatus for locomotion by land, air or water' clearly indicates that 'Ships' refers to waterborne vehicles. Meaning 1 is the correct interpretation.

Purpose: Emitting audible warnings for bicycles
Expanded_Name: Audible warning systems for bicycles
Logic_Trace: The NICE Class 12 description 'Vehicles; apparatus for locomotion by land, air or water' covers bicycles as land vehicles. The term explicitly specifies 'for bicycles', making meaning 2 the precise and correct interpretation.

Purpose: Providing animal feed or fertilizer
Expanded_Name: Oil cake for animal fodder
Logic_Trace: NICE Class 31 includes 'foodstuffs and beverages for animals'. This context strongly points to 'oil cake' as the by-product of oil extraction used in animal feed (meaning 2), rejecting culinary or industrial interpretations.

Purpose: Being consumed as food
Expanded_Name: Cheese containing herbs [foodstuff]
Logic_Trace: NICE Class 29 explicitly lists 'cheese' among milk and other dairy products. Therefore, 'Cheese containing herbs' refers to a specific variety of cheese (meaning 1) within this food category.

Purpose: Being used as a base for cooking
Expanded_Name: Fish stock for food
Logic_Trace: NICE Class 29 covers 'fish' and 'meat extracts', which includes prepared food items and culinary ingredients. This context confirms 'Fish stock' as a liquid culinary preparation (meaning 2), rejecting financial or live animal interpretations.

Purpose: Being planted and grown
Expanded_Name: Live seedlings for planting
Logic_Trace: NICE Class 31 explicitly lists 'bulbs, seedlings and seeds for planting' under agricultural and horticultural products. This directly confirms meaning 1 as the correct interpretation.

Purpose: Being consumed or processed as food
Expanded_Name: Fresh pulses [legumes]
Logic_Trace: NICE Class 31 covers 'Raw and unprocessed agricultural... products' and 'fresh fruits and vegetables'. Pulses (legumes) fit this description as raw agricultural food products, making meaning 1 correct.

Purpose: Being used in cooking preparations
Expanded_Name: Vegetable juices for culinary purposes
Logic_Trace: NICE Class 29 includes 'preserved, frozen, dried and cooked fruits and vegetables' and other food products. The explicit mention 'for cooking' places this product as a food ingredient, aligning with meaning 1 and Class 29, rather than a beverage (Class 32).

Purpose: Being used as a food ingredient
Expanded_Name: Tomato paste [foodstuff]
Logic_Trace: NICE Class 29 covers 'preserved, frozen, dried and cooked fruits and vegetables'. Tomato paste, being a cooked and concentrated vegetable product for food, fits this description perfectly. Meaning 1 is correct.

Purpose: Providing assistance and guidance to travelers
Expanded_Name: Escorting services for travellers
Logic_Trace: NICE Class 39 is defined as 'Transport; packaging and storage of goods; travel arrangement'. 'Escorting of travellers' directly falls under services related to travel arrangement and assistance during transport. Meaning 1 is the most appropriate.

Purpose: Arranging bookings for travel
Expanded_Name: Travel reservation services
Logic_Trace: NICE Class 39 includes 'travel arrangement'. 'Travel reservation' refers to the service of making bookings for travel, which is a core component of arranging travel. Meaning 1 is correct.

Purpose: Conducting sales of goods or property by auction
Expanded_Name: Auctioneering services
Logic_Trace: NICE Class 35 covers 'business management, organization and administration'. Auctioneering is a business service for facilitating sales, fitting meaning 1 within this classification.

Purpose: Facilitating the procurement of insurance policies
Expanded_Name: Arranging services for insurance
Logic_Trace: NICE Class 36 explicitly lists 'insurance services'. 'Arranging of insurance' directly refers to the service of facilitating insurance procurement, making meaning 1 correct.

Purpose: Reserving seats for transportation during travel
Expanded_Name: Booking services for travel seats
Logic_Trace: NICE Class 39 focuses on 'Transport' and 'travel arrangement'. Booking seats for travel is a direct service related to arranging transportation for travelers. Meaning 1 is the correct interpretation.

Original_Term: Data search in computer files for others
Purpose: Retrieving specific data from computer files for third parties
Expanded_Name: Data search services in computer files for others
Logic_Trace: NICE Class 35 covers 'business management, organization and administration; office functions'. Performing data searches 'for others' is clearly a business or office service provided to clients. Meaning 1 is correct.

Purpose: Expanding functionality or connecting peripherals to personal computers
Expanded_Name: PC expansion cards [computer hardware]
Logic_Trace: NICE Class 9 explicitly mentions 'computers and computer peripheral devices'. PC cards, often referring to expansion cards that add functionality to PCs, fit this description as computer hardware components. Meaning 1 is the correct interpretation.

Purpose: Decoding encoded television signals for viewing
Expanded_Name: Electronic television decoders
Logic_Trace: NICE Class 9 includes 'apparatus and instruments for recording, transmitting, reproducing or processing sound, images or data'. Television decoders are electronic apparatus that process and reproduce television signals, aligning with meaning 1.

Purpose: Cleansing the body during bathing
Expanded_Name: Non-medicated bath soap
Logic_Trace: NICE Class 3 lists 'Non-medicated cosmetics and toiletry preparations' and 'cleaning... preparations'. 'Bath soap' primarily refers to a toiletry preparation for personal hygiene during bathing. Meaning 1 is correct.

Original_Term: Cosmetics for the use on the hair
Purpose: Enhancing the appearance or condition of hair
Expanded_Name: Non-medicated cosmetics for hair
Logic_Trace: NICE Class 3 explicitly covers 'Non-medicated cosmetics and toiletry preparations'. The term 'Cosmetics for the use on the hair' is a specific category within this, directly matching meaning 1.

Purpose: Housing the mainspring in clocks and watches
Expanded_Name: Barrels [parts of clocks and watches]
Logic_Trace: NICE Class 14 focuses on 'horological and chronometric instruments'. The qualifier '[clock- and watchmaking]' directs specifically to meaning 3, which is a component of a timepiece's mechanism.

Purpose: Regulating the movement of clocks
Expanded_Name: Pendulums [parts of clocks]
Logic_Trace: NICE Class 14 covers 'horological and chronometric instruments'. The qualifier '[clock- and watchmaking]' clearly points to the specific component used in clocks for timekeeping, making meaning 1 correct.

Purpose: Forming part of the escapement mechanism in clocks and watches
Expanded_Name: Anchors [parts of clock and watch escapements]
Logic_Trace: NICE Class 14 pertains to 'horological and chronometric instruments'. The qualifier '[clock- and watchmaking]' specifies the context, identifying 'anchors' as a crucial component of a timepiece's escapement mechanism (meaning 2).

Purpose: Displaying time on clocks and watches
Expanded_Name: Dials [parts of clocks and watches]
Logic_Trace: NICE Class 14 is for 'horological and chronometric instruments'. The qualifier '[clock- and watchmaking]' specifically refers to the display faces of these instruments used for showing time, meaning 1.

Purpose: Driving the timekeeping functions of clocks and watches
Expanded_Name: Movements [internal mechanisms] for clocks and watches
Logic_Trace: NICE Class 14 covers 'horological and chronometric instruments'. The term 'Movements' directly refers to the core internal mechanisms of these instruments, making meaning 1 correct.

Purpose: Indicating the time on a watch dial
Expanded_Name: Watch hands [parts of watches]
Logic_Trace: NICE Class 14 relates to 'horological and chronometric instruments'. Watch hands are specific components of watches used for time indication, aligning with meaning 1.

Nature: Electronic or mechanical component for timepieces
Purpose: Generating a stable frequency for accurate timekeeping in timepieces
Expanded_Name: Oscillators for horological timepieces
Logic_Trace: NICE Class 14 is for 'horological and chronometric instruments'. The term explicitly mentions 'for timepieces', confirming that these are components specifically designed to provide the stable frequency required for accurate timekeeping, fitting meaning 1.

Nature: Artificial replicas of fruit, flowers, and vegetables
Purpose: For decorative and ornamental display
Expanded_Name: Artificial decorative fruit, flowers and vegetables
Logic_Trace: Class 26 explicitly includes "artificial flowers" and generally covers "hair decorations" and "false hair", indicating decorative or ornamental items. It does not mention props for theatre or toys (which would be Class 28). Therefore, the correct meaning is decorative items for aesthetic purposes, specifically as ornamental artificial flora and produce.

Original_Term: Hair ornaments, hair rollers, hair fastening articles, and false hair
Purpose: Ornamenting, styling, and securing hair
Expanded_Name: Hair ornaments, rollers, fastening articles, and false hair for styling and decorating
Logic_Trace: Class 26 description includes "hair decorations" and "false hair". Other items in the class like "lace, braid and embroidery" further indicate articles for embellishment and attachment. This confirms the items are for hair styling and decoration. Hair cutting tools or medical devices would fall into other classes (e.g., Class 8 for hand tools, Class 5 or 10 for medical devices).

Original_Term: Probiotic bacterial formulations for medical use
Purpose: Treating or preventing specific medical conditions
Expanded_Name: Probiotic bacterial formulations for medical treatment
Logic_Trace: Class 5 explicitly covers "Pharmaceuticals, medical and veterinary preparations; sanitary preparations for medical purposes; dietetic food and substances adapted for medical or veterinary use, food for babies; dietary supplements for human beings and animals". The term "for medical use" in the original term directly aligns with this class description, confirming its pharmaceutical nature.

Purpose: Immobilizing and supporting body parts post-injury or surgery
Expanded_Name: Surgical splints for immobilizing and supporting body parts
Logic_Trace: Class 10 covers "Surgical, medical, dental and veterinary apparatus and instruments; artificial limbs, eyes and teeth; orthopaedic articles". The term "surgical" directly indicates medical use, and splints are classic orthopedic articles. Carpentry tools would be Class 8, and plant supports Class 21 or 20.

Purpose: Securing or attaching artificial teeth within dentistry
Expanded_Name: Dental pins for securing artificial teeth
Logic_Trace: Class 10 includes "Surgical, medical, dental and veterinary apparatus and instruments; artificial limbs, eyes and teeth". The term explicitly states "artificial teeth", which falls under the scope of dental apparatus and artificial body parts. Fasteners for crafts or general assembly would be in other classes (e.g., Class 6 for metal pins, Class 26 for decorative pins).

Purpose: Polishing, scouring, and grinding surfaces
Expanded_Name: Emery cloth for abrasive polishing and grinding
Logic_Trace: Class 3 includes "cleaning, polishing, scouring and abrasive preparations". Emery cloth is a well-known abrasive material used for these purposes. Fabric for clothing would be Class 24. A general cleaning wipe might be Class 21 or 5 (medicated).

Purpose: Forming the main body and weight of a dart for games
Expanded_Name: Barrels for game darts
Logic_Trace: Class 28 covers "Games, toys and playthings; video game apparatus; gymnastic and sporting articles". Darts are a sporting game, and barrels are a component of the dart itself. Containers for liquids would be Class 20, 21, or 6. Firearm parts would be Class 13.

Purpose: Used in various games and sports activities
Expanded_Name: Game balls for sports and recreational activities
Logic_Trace: Class 28 covers "Games, toys and playthings; video game apparatus; gymnastic and sporting articles". "Balls for games" directly falls under sporting articles and playthings. Decorative spheres would be Class 20, 21, or 26 depending on material and specific nature. Bearings would be Class 7.

Purpose: Used specifically for the recreational activity of juggling
Expanded_Name: Juggling balls for entertainment and recreational activities
Logic_Trace: Class 28 covers "Games, toys and playthings; video game apparatus; gymnastic and sporting articles". Juggling is a form of play or performance, and balls for juggling are considered playthings or sporting articles. Small soft toys are also covered. Stress balls could be Class 28 as well or sometimes 10 (therapeutic). The primary context here is play/performance.

Purpose: For human food, either as a primary ingredient or prepared dish
Expanded_Name: Processed cereals for human consumption
Logic_Trace: Class 30 explicitly lists "flour and preparations made from cereals" and "rice, pasta and noodles". This confirms the context is cereals processed for human consumption (e.g., breakfast cereals, flour grains). Field crops would be Class 31, and animal feed Class 31.

Nature: Unworked or semi-worked flat panels of glass
Purpose: For further manufacturing or use in household/kitchen articles, excluding building applications
Expanded_Name: Unworked or semi-worked glass sheets (excluding building glass)
Logic_Trace: Class 21 includes "unworked or semi-worked glass, except building glass; glassware, porcelain and earthenware". This indicates that glass sheets, in this class, are raw or semi-processed material for various household or kitchen uses, but explicitly not for building. Window panes would be Class 19.

Purpose: Enabling locomotion and landing for aircraft
Expanded_Name: Aircraft tyres for locomotion and landing systems

Expanded_Name: Prepared squid for human consumption
