Live squid is not a prepared food product, and squid ink is a different product (possibly Class 30 if a food colorant, or Class 16 if for writing).

Purpose: For direct human consumption as food
Expanded_Name: Snails prepared for human consumption
Logic_Trace: Class 29 includes "Meat, fish, poultry and game" and "preserved, frozen, dried and cooked fruits and vegetables". This term is very explicit, directly stating "prepared for human consumption", which is precisely the scope of food products in Class 29. Medicinal snails (Class 5) or live pets (Class 31) are incorrect.

Purpose: For human consumption as a dried seafood product
Expanded_Name: Dried squid for human consumption
Logic_Trace: Class 29 includes "Meat, fish, poultry and game" and "preserved, frozen, dried and cooked fruits and vegetables". "Dried squid" is a common preserved seafood item for human consumption, fitting perfectly into the "dried" food category of this class. Decorative items (Class 20) or animal feed (Class 31) are outside the scope of food for human consumption.

Purpose: For use as an industrial solvent, fuel, or illuminant
Expanded_Name: Petroleum ether [industrial solvent]
Logic_Trace: Class 4 includes "Industrial oils and greases, wax; lubricants; fuels and illuminants". Petroleum ether, being a petroleum fraction, is used as an industrial solvent and can also function as a fuel, thus aligning with the broad categories of industrial chemicals and fuels in Class 4. Its solvent properties are a primary aspect.

Nature: Edible baked goods and sugar-based sweet treats
Purpose: For human consumption as desserts or sweets
Expanded_Name: Pastry and confectionery for human consumption
Logic_Trace: Class 30 explicitly lists "bread, pastries and confectionery". This term directly matches the class description for edible baked goods and sweets. Raw ingredients (e.g., flour Class 30, sugar Class 30, but not the final products) or equipment (Class 7 or 21) are rejected.

Purpose: For food preparation and processing in kitchens
Expanded_Name: Electric kitchen machines for food preparation
Logic_Trace: Class 7 includes "Machines, machine tools, power-operated tools". The terms "electric" and "machines" strongly point to Class 7 for powered devices. "Kitchen machines" defines their specific application, fitting perfectly. Non-electric utensils (Class 8 or 21) and large commercial installations (often encompassing multiple classes) are rejected.

Purpose: For lifting and moving people or goods between different levels
Expanded_Name: Lifts [elevators] for vertical transportation of people and goods
Logic_Trace: Class 7 includes "Machines, machine tools, power-operated tools". Lifts (elevators) are complex machines for vertical transportation. The explicit exclusion "other than ski-lifts" clarifies the scope, making it a direct fit for the "machines" category in Class 7. Weightlifting equipment is Class 28 or 9.

Purpose: Providing personal hygiene and beauty maintenance for human beings
Expanded_Name: Hygienic care services for human beings
Logic_Trace: Class 44 is for "Medical services; veterinary services; hygienic and beauty care for human beings or animals". The term explicitly refers to "care for human beings" and "hygienic", which is a direct match for the service class description. Products (e.g., Class 3, 5) and medical treatments (while related, hygienic care often implies non-curative maintenance) are distinct.

Purpose: For reducing the viscosity of lacquers to facilitate application
Expanded_Name: Chemical thinners for lacquers
Logic_Trace: Class 2 includes "Paints, varnishes, lacquers" and "colorants, dyes". Thinners are chemical preparations used with lacquers to adjust their consistency, and logically belong to the same class as the substances they modify. Equipment (Class 7 or 8) or pre-thinned lacquers (which are the final product, not the thinner itself) are rejected.

Nature: Chemical solvent derived from tree resins
Purpose: For thinning paints and varnishes
Expanded_Name: Turpentine as a thinner for paints
Logic_Trace: Class 2 includes "Paints, varnishes, lacquers". The explicit descriptor "[thinner for paints]" makes this a direct fit for the class, as it's a substance used in conjunction with paints to adjust their consistency. Wood (Class 19) or medical balm (Class 5) are rejected.

Purpose: For reducing the viscosity of paints to facilitate application
Expanded_Name: Chemical thinners for paints
Logic_Trace: Class 2 includes "Paints, varnishes, lacquers". This general term for substances that thin paints is a direct fit for the class, as it's a preparatory or additive product for paints. Paint removing agents (often also Class 2 but with a different primary purpose) and paint brushes (Class 16 or 21) are rejected.

Purpose: For creating hollows or holes when attached to hand-operated tools
Expanded_Name: Hollowing bits, being parts of hand-operated tools
Logic_Trace: Class 8 includes "Hand tools and implements, hand-operated". The phrase "[parts of hand tools]" is explicit, confirming that these bits are specific components for hand tools (e.g., carving tools or drills) to create hollows or holes. Power tool accessories and drilling machines (Class 7) are rejected.

Purpose: For cutting materials when attached to hand-operated saws
Expanded_Name: Saw blades, being parts of hand-operated saws
Logic_Trace: Class 8 includes "Hand tools and implements, hand-operated". The phrase "[parts of hand tools]" is explicit, indicating these are cutting edges specifically for hand saws. Blades for power saws (Class 7) and general knives (while Class 8, 'saw blades' is more specific) are distinct.

Purpose: For holding saw blades and forming the body of hand-operated saws
Expanded_Name: Frames for hand-operated saws
Logic_Trace: Class 8 includes "Hand tools and implements, hand-operated". A frame for a handsaw is an integral structural component of the hand tool itself, designed to hold the saw blade. This is a direct fit. Picture frames (Class 20) and frames for power saws (Class 7) are rejected.

Nature: Accessory or component for hand saws
Purpose: For holding a saw blade or an entire hand saw during use or assembly
Expanded_Name: Holders for hand-operated saws or saw blades
Logic_Trace: Class 8 includes "Hand tools and implements, hand-operated". In the context of Class 8, "saw holders" refers to components or accessories that facilitate the use or assembly of hand saws, such as vices or clamps to hold workpieces for sawing, or components for the hand saw itself. Racks for storage (Class 20) and holders for power saws (Class 7) are rejected.

Original_Term: Extension pieces for braces for screw taps
Purpose: Extending the reach or functionality of hand-operated screw tap braces
Expanded_Name: Hand tool extension pieces for screw tap braces
Logic_Trace: Class 8 is 'Hand tools and implements, hand-operated'. This context explicitly confirms that the term refers to extension pieces for hand-operated braces used with screw taps, rejecting the dental or construction meanings.

Purpose: Performing various operations (e.g., drilling, driving screws) when attached to hand tools
Expanded_Name: Interchangeable bits for hand-operated tools
Logic_Trace: Class 8 is 'Hand tools and implements, hand-operated'. The bracketed clarification '[parts of hand tools]' explicitly guides the meaning to interchangeable components for hand tools, confirming the first meaning and rejecting others.

Purpose: Cutting materials when integrated into hand-operated shears
Expanded_Name: Cutting blades for hand-operated shears
Logic_Trace: Class 8 is 'Hand tools and implements, hand-operated'. This classification restricts 'shear blades' to those used in hand-operated shearing devices, rejecting blades for industrial machines (Class 7) or animal clippers that are often electric (Class 7).

Purpose: Scoring and cutting glass by hand
Expanded_Name: Diamond-tipped components for hand-operated glaziers' tools
Logic_Trace: Class 8 is 'Hand tools and implements, hand-operated'. The phrase '[parts of hand tools]' confirms the first meaning, identifying these as diamond-tipped components for hand tools used in glazing, and rejecting the jewelry or general construction tools without specific hand-tool context.

Purpose: Shaving wood surfaces when fitted into hand planes
Expanded_Name: Cutting blades for hand-operated woodworking planes
Logic_Trace: Class 8 is 'Hand tools and implements, hand-operated'. This classification specifically indicates that 'plane irons' are the cutting blades used in hand-operated woodworking planes, rejecting any association with aircraft or generic flattening tools.

Purpose: Being consumed as a snack or accompaniment to food
Expanded_Name: Baked savory crackers made from cereals
Logic_Trace: Class 30 covers 'Flour and preparations made from cereals; bread, pastries and confectionery'. This context clearly indicates that 'crackers' refers to the baked food product, rejecting the explosive devices or informal term for hackers.

Purpose: Imparting knowledge and skills in various aspects of management
Expanded_Name: Educational and training services in the field of management
Logic_Trace: Class 41 is 'Education; providing of training'. This clearly aligns with the provision of instruction and training in management, selecting the first meaning and rejecting general consulting (Class 35).

Purpose: Enabling user interaction and performing specific functions on a computer
Expanded_Name: Interactive computer software programs
Logic_Trace: Class 9 covers 'recorded and downloadable media, computer software'. This broad definition for software confirms that 'interactive computer software' refers to any digital program designed for user engagement, fitting all brainstormed examples as specific types of interactive software.

Purpose: Facilitating learning, teaching, and educational activities
Expanded_Name: Downloadable educational computer software
Logic_Trace: Class 9 covers 'recorded and downloadable media, computer software'. This classification confirms that 'education software' refers to computer programs used to facilitate learning and teaching, encompassing various applications within the educational domain.

Purpose: Providing expert guidance and recommendations to improve business management, organization, and administration
Expanded_Name: Professional advisory and consulting services in the field of business management
Logic_Trace: Class 35 covers 'Business management, organization and administration'. This context specifically refers to professional guidance and recommendations for business operations, matching the first meaning and distinguishing it from training services (Class 41) or legal advice (Class 45).

Purpose: Conveying water for garden irrigation and watering purposes
Expanded_Name: Flexible non-metallic hoses for garden watering
Logic_Trace: Class 17 covers 'Flexible pipes, tubes and hoses, not of metal'. This clearly identifies the product as a flexible, non-metallic conduit used for watering in a garden context, rejecting industrial applications (which might also fit, but 'garden' specifies) or ornamental items.

Purpose: Supplementing the diet with colostrum for health and wellness
Expanded_Name: Dietary supplements containing colostrum for human consumption
Logic_Trace: Class 5 covers 'Dietary supplements for human beings and animals'. This directly matches the concept of colostrum in a supplementary form for health purposes, distinguishing it from pure pharmaceutical preparations or food ingredients.

Purpose: Dissolving or loosening adhesive residues from surfaces
Expanded_Name: Chemical preparations for removing adhesives
Logic_Trace: Class 3 covers 'Cleaning, polishing, scouring and abrasive preparations'. This context indicates that 'adhesive removers' are chemical cleaning solutions, not hand tools (Class 8) or services.

Purpose: Providing fresh razor blades for manual shaving
Expanded_Name: Replaceable cartridges containing razor blades for manual shavers
Logic_Trace: Class 8 covers 'Razors'. This classification confirms that the cartridges are for shaving razors, specifically designed to hold and dispense razor blades for personal grooming, rejecting ammunition or ink cartridges.

Purpose: Releasing fumigants to destroy vermin and pests
Expanded_Name: Fumigating sticks for destroying vermin
Logic_Trace: Class 5 covers 'Preparations for destroying vermin; fungicides, herbicides'. This directly matches the primary purpose of 'fumigating sticks' as pest control agents, distinguishing them from incense or air fresheners (Class 3).

Purpose: Securing prostheses, such as dentures or artificial body parts, to the body
Expanded_Name: Medical adhesive preparations for affixing prostheses
Logic_Trace: Class 5 covers 'Pharmaceuticals, medical and veterinary preparations; sanitary preparations for medical purposes... material for stopping teeth, dental wax'. This context clearly indicates that these adhesives are for medical or dental applications, specifically for securing prostheses, rather than general-purpose adhesives (Class 1) or tapes (also Class 5 but different form).

Nature: Small bags containing fragrant solid material
Purpose: Imparting fragrance or deodorizing confined spaces like drawers or closets
Expanded_Name: Scented sachets for perfuming and deodorizing
Logic_Trace: Class 3 covers 'Perfumery, essential oils'. This confirms that 'scented sachets' are fragrance-emitting products for ambient scenting or deodorizing, distinguishing them from tea bags or spice packets (Class 30).

Purpose: Trimming or shaving hair when fitted into electric razors
Expanded_Name: Replaceable cutting blades for electric razors
Logic_Trace: Class 8 covers 'Razors'. While electric razors themselves may fall into Class 9, their replaceable cutting blades are traditionally classified alongside manual razors and their parts in Class 8, confirming their function as shaving components.

Purpose: Storing and safely dispensing new razor blades and often collecting used ones
Expanded_Name: Non-mechanical dispensers for razor blades
Logic_Trace: Class 8 covers 'Razors'. As an accessory directly related to razors and their blades, a non-mechanical dispenser for them falls under this class, intended for safe handling and storage for personal shaving use. This rejects industrial mechanisms (Class 7) or retail display units (Class 35).

Purpose: Fine shaping, filing, and finishing small components and intricate designs
Expanded_Name: Hand-operated needle files for fine finishing work
Logic_Trace: Class 8 covers 'Hand tools and implements, hand-operated'. This classification directly identifies 'needle files' as specific types of small, hand-operated abrasive tools for precision work, distinguishing them from digital files or sharpening tools for sewing needles.

Nature: Chemical cleaning preparations in solid or liquid form
Purpose: Cleaning various surfaces, fabrics, or skin within a household environment
Expanded_Name: Chemical soap preparations for general household cleaning
Logic_Trace: Class 3 covers 'Cleaning, polishing, scouring and abrasive preparations' and 'Non-medicated cosmetics and toiletry preparations'. This broad scope confirms that 'soaps for household use' include both cleaning agents for surfaces and fabrics, as well as personal hygiene soaps, for domestic application.

Purpose: Physically removing nails from various surfaces or materials
Expanded_Name: Hand-operated tools for extracting nails
Logic_Trace: Class 8 covers 'Hand tools and implements, hand-operated'. The explicit 'hand-operated' qualifier confirms that this term refers to manual tools for removing nails from construction materials, rejecting medical tools (Class 10) or industrial machines (Class 7).

Purpose: Sharpening or refining the edge of straight razors
Expanded_Name: Abrasive strops for sharpening and honing straight razors
Logic_Trace: Class 8 covers 'Razors'. A 'razor strop' is a well-known accessory for maintaining the edge of straight razors, fitting directly into this class as a hand-operated implement for razor maintenance, rejecting other strap meanings.

Purpose: Organizing and transporting personal toiletry items
Expanded_Name: Empty carrying bags for toiletries
Logic_Trace: Class 18 covers 'Luggage and carrying bags'. The crucial phrase 'sold empty' ensures that the item is the bag itself, not a kit with contents, fitting perfectly into the carrying bags category. This rejects kits (multi-class) or tool bags (potentially Class 8 or 20).

Purpose: Supplementing the diet with prebiotics to support digestive health
Expanded_Name: Dietary supplements containing prebiotics for human consumption
Logic_Trace: Class 5 covers 'Dietary supplements for human beings and animals'. This directly matches the concept of prebiotics in a supplementary form for health purposes, distinguishing it from pure pharmaceutical preparations or food ingredients.

Purpose: Monitoring, detecting, and recording activities for security and safety purposes
Expanded_Name: Electronic apparatus and instruments for security surveillance
Logic_Trace: Class 9 covers 'Apparatus and instruments for recording, transmitting, reproducing or processing sound, images or data; detecting, testing, inspecting'. This broad description fully encompasses electronic devices and systems used for security monitoring, confirming the first meaning and rejecting services (Class 45) or physical barriers.

Purpose: Cleansing the skin by dissolving and removing cosmetic make-up
Expanded_Name: Non-medicated cosmetic preparations for removing make-up
Logic_Trace: Class 3 covers 'Non-medicated cosmetics and toiletry preparations'. This context clearly indicates that 'make-up removing preparations' are cosmetic chemical solutions for skin cleansing, distinguishing them from tools (Class 21) or services (Class 44).

Purpose: Filtering ground coffee during the electric brewing process
Expanded_Name: Electric filtering components for coffee makers
Logic_Trace: Class 11 covers 'Apparatus and installations for lighting, heating, cooling, steam generating, cooking'. As a component of electric coffee-making apparatus (which are heating/cooking devices), 'electric coffee filters' refer to integral electrically-operated filtering parts within such machines, not non-electric filters (Class 21) or power filters (Class 9).

Purpose: Heating and boiling water using electricity
Expanded_Name: Electric household appliances for boiling water
Logic_Trace: Class 11 covers 'Apparatus and installations for heating'. This classification directly refers to electrically powered appliances designed for boiling water for household use, rejecting non-electric versions (Class 21) or industrial equipment (Class 7).

Purpose: Preparing food through various cooking methods using electricity
Expanded_Name: Electric household utensils and appliances for cooking
Logic_Trace: Class 11 covers 'Apparatus and installations for cooking'. This classification explicitly identifies 'electric cooking utensils' as electrically powered devices used for food preparation in a household context, differentiating them from non-electric utensils (Class 21) or industrial machinery (Class 7).

Purpose: Roasting coffee beans
Expanded_Name: Coffee bean roasting machines
Logic_Trace: Class 11 covers "Apparatus and installations for lighting, heating, cooling, steam generating, cooking, drying...". Machines for roasting coffee beans fall directly under 'heating' and 'drying' apparatus, confirming the product is a machine. The other brainstormed meanings refer to persons or services, not apparatus.

Purpose: Holding and serving coffee
Expanded_Name: Drinking mugs for coffee
Logic_Trace: Class 21 explicitly includes "Household or kitchen utensils and containers; cookware and tableware; glassware, porcelain and earthenware". Coffee mugs are drinking vessels that fit perfectly into the 'tableware' and 'glassware, porcelain and earthenware' categories.

Original_Term: Water purifying chemicals for swimming pools
Purpose: Purifying water in swimming pools
Expanded_Name: Chemical preparations for purifying water in swimming pools
Logic_Trace: Class 1 covers "Chemicals for use in industry, science and photography...". The term clearly identifies these as 'chemicals' with the specific purpose of 'purifying water' in 'swimming pools', fitting the class description precisely. Physical systems would be Class 11, and surface cleaning solutions typically Class 3.

Original_Term: Chemical preparations for use in water softening
Purpose: Softening water by chemical means
Expanded_Name: Chemical preparations for softening water
Logic_Trace: Class 1 is for "Chemicals for use in industry, science...". The term explicitly states 'Chemical preparations' for 'water softening', which is a chemical process, making it a perfect fit. Mechanical apparatus would be Class 11.

Purpose: Sanitizing swimming pool water
Expanded_Name: Chlorine chemical for sanitizing swimming pool water
Logic_Trace: Class 1 pertains to "Chemicals for use in industry, science...". Chlorine is a well-known chemical substance, and its use for swimming pools falls under this classification for industrial/scientific use in water treatment.

Original_Term: Chemicals for the purification of water used in swimming pools
Purpose: Purifying water used in swimming pools
Expanded_Name: Chemical preparations for purifying water used in swimming pools
Logic_Trace: Class 1 is for "Chemicals for use in industry, science...". The term specifies 'Chemicals' for 'purification of water' in 'swimming pools', directly aligning with the scope of Class 1. Mechanical filters are Class 11, and surface cleaning chemicals are Class 3.

Original_Term: Chemicals for use in swimming pools
Purpose: Treating and maintaining the water quality of swimming pools
Expanded_Name: Chemical preparations for treating swimming pool water
Logic_Trace: Class 1 covers "Chemicals for use in industry, science...". Given the general nature and the class, this refers to chemical preparations that directly affect the water's properties and quality for use in swimming pools, such as purification, pH balance, or algae prevention. Surface cleaners would be Class 3, and paints/coatings Class 2.

Purpose: Maintaining and repairing plumbing systems
Expanded_Name: Maintenance and repair services for plumbing
Logic_Trace: Class 37 explicitly includes "Installation and repair services". 'Maintenance' directly falls under repair and upkeep services for plumbing systems, making it a perfect match. Tools (Class 8) and fixtures (Class 11) are goods, not services.

Original_Term: Application of coatings to swimming pools
Purpose: Applying protective and decorative coatings to swimming pools
Expanded_Name: Services for the application of coatings to swimming pools
Logic_Trace: Class 37 covers "Construction services; installation and repair services". The 'application of coatings' is a specialized construction or finishing service, fitting within this class. The coatings (Class 2) and tools (Class 8) are goods, not services.

Original_Term: Chemical preparations for testing swimming pool water
Purpose: Testing the chemical composition and quality of swimming pool water
Expanded_Name: Chemical reagents for testing swimming pool water
Logic_Trace: Class 1 is for "Chemicals for use in industry, science...". The term specifies 'Chemical preparations' for 'testing swimming pool water', which are chemical reagents used in analytical science, fitting Class 1 precisely. Testing kits could involve other classes (e.g., Class 9 for apparatus), and testing services are Class 42.

Original_Term: Water treatment chemicals for use in swimming spas
Purpose: Treating water in swimming spas for purification and balance
Expanded_Name: Chemical preparations for water treatment in swimming spas
Logic_Trace: Class 1 pertains to "Chemicals for use in industry, science...". The term 'water treatment chemicals' for 'swimming spas' clearly indicates chemical preparations intended for altering or maintaining the water quality, which falls within Class 1. Filtration equipment is Class 11, and surface cleaners are Class 3.

Purpose: Consumption as a snack or appetizer
Expanded_Name: Prepared snack foods consisting primarily of fish crackers
Logic_Trace: Class 29 includes "Fish... for food". Fish crackers are prepared food products where fish is a key ingredient, directly falling under this category. Crackers as a general food item are Class 30, and pet food is Class 31.

Original_Term: Steamed cakes of smashed fish and yam (hampen)
Purpose: Consumption as a savory dish or component
Expanded_Name: Steamed cakes consisting primarily of smashed fish and yam (hampen)
Logic_Trace: Class 29 covers "Meat, fish, poultry and game; meat extracts; preserved, frozen, dried and cooked fruits and vegetables...". As a 'steamed cake' consisting of 'smashed fish and yam', this is a cooked, processed food item where fish is a principal ingredient, fitting squarely within Class 29.

Original_Term: Steamed or toasted cakes of fish paste (kamaboko)
Purpose: Consumption as a savory food item
Expanded_Name: Processed food items consisting of steamed or toasted cakes of fish paste (kamaboko)
Logic_Trace: Class 29 includes "Meat, fish...". Kamaboko is a well-known prepared food item made from processed fish paste, often steamed or toasted, which is consumed as a savory food. This is a direct fit for Class 29.

Purpose: Nourishing animals
Expanded_Name: Prepared meal for feeding animals
Logic_Trace: Class 31 explicitly lists "foodstuffs and beverages for animals". 'Meal for animals' directly refers to prepared animal feed, making it a perfect match. Ground grains are raw products, and a 'mealworm' is a live animal, both covered by Class 31 but not the primary meaning of 'meal'.

Purpose: Transmitting and receiving wireless signals or data
Expanded_Name: Electronic apparatus for wireless transmission and reception of signals
Logic_Trace: Class 9 covers "Apparatus and instruments for recording, transmitting, reproducing or processing sound, images or data". Wireless transmitters and receivers are electronic apparatus designed specifically for 'transmitting' and 'receiving' signals or data, fitting this description precisely. Software (also Class 9) is distinct from hardware, and antennas can be Class 9 or 7 depending on their function.

Purpose: Flavoring food
Expanded_Name: Blends of spices and herbs for flavoring food (seasoning mixes)
Logic_Trace: Class 30 includes "salt, seasonings, spices, preserved herbs; vinegar, sauces and other condiments". 'Seasoning mixes' are clearly blends of these items intended for flavoring food, making them a direct fit for Class 30.

Purpose: Providing illumination for bicycles
Expanded_Name: Lighting apparatus for bicycles
Logic_Trace: Class 11 covers "Apparatus and installations for lighting, heating...". Bicycle lights are apparatus whose primary function is lighting, regardless of the vehicle they are attached to, hence fitting Class 11 perfectly.

Purpose: Human consumption as a food source
Expanded_Name: Processed edible insects, not live, for human consumption
Logic_Trace: Class 29 includes "Meat, fish, poultry and game; meat extracts; preserved, frozen, dried and cooked fruits and vegetables... ". While insects are not explicitly listed, the term 'edible' and 'not live' implies they are processed for human food, similar to other protein sources in this class. Live insects for animal feed or bait are Class 31.

Purpose: Use as raw material in metalworking and manufacturing
Expanded_Name: Semi-finished billets of common metal
Logic_Trace: Class 6 covers "Common metals and their alloys, ores; metal materials for building and construction...". Metal billets are semi-finished forms of common metals, used as raw material in manufacturing, directly fitting the 'metal materials' aspect of Class 6.

Purpose: Delivering food to customers
Expanded_Name: Transport services for delivering food to consumers
Logic_Trace: Class 39 explicitly includes "Transport; packaging and storage of goods". Food delivery is a transport service for goods (foodstuffs), fitting precisely into this class. Food preparation is Class 43, and retail sales are Class 35.

Original_Term: Flavourings made from vegetables [other than essential oils]
Purpose: Imparting flavor to food
Expanded_Name: Vegetable-based food flavorings [other than essential oils]
Logic_Trace: Class 30 covers "salt, seasonings, spices, preserved herbs; vinegar, sauces and other condiments". Flavorings, particularly those derived from vegetables (and explicitly excluding essential oils which might fall into Class 3 or 5), are substances added to food for taste, fitting within the broader category of condiments and seasonings.

Original_Term: Flavourings for snack foods [other than essential oils]
Purpose: Imparting flavor to snack foods
Expanded_Name: Food flavorings for snack foods [other than essential oils]
Logic_Trace: Class 30 includes "salt, seasonings, spices, preserved herbs; vinegar, sauces and other condiments". Flavorings for snack foods, excluding essential oils, are substances added to impart taste, aligning with the scope of seasonings and condiments in Class 30.

Purpose: Serving as an interior lining for footwear
Expanded_Name: Textile fabrics for lining footwear
Logic_Trace: Class 24 is for "Textiles and substitutes for textiles; household linen...". Regardless of its end-use in footwear, the core nature of the product is 'fabric' (a textile material), which is clearly covered by Class 24. Finished shoe insoles might be Class 25, but the term specifies 'fabric'.

Purpose: Transporting and supplying foodstuffs
Expanded_Name: Logistics and transport services for the distribution of foodstuffs
Logic_Trace: Class 39 covers "Transport; packaging and storage of goods". The 'distribution of foodstuffs' encompasses the transport, packaging, and storage aspects of moving food products through the supply chain, fitting perfectly into this service class. Retail sales are Class 35, and manufacturing is not a service.

Original_Term: Cooked meals consisting principally of fish
Purpose: Human consumption as a main course
Expanded_Name: Prepared cooked meals consisting principally of fish
Logic_Trace: Class 29 includes "Meat, fish, poultry and game; meat extracts; preserved, frozen, dried and cooked fruits and vegetables...". 'Cooked meals' principally of fish are prepared food items where fish forms the major part, directly matching the description for food in Class 29. Raw fish is also Class 29, but this specifies 'cooked meals'.

Purpose: Smoothing and polishing surfaces
Expanded_Name: Polishing and abrasive buffing compounds
Logic_Trace: Class 3 includes "Cleaning, polishing, scouring and abrasive preparations". 'Buffing compounds' are directly identified as preparations used for polishing and abrasive finishing, fitting perfectly into this category. Mechanical machines are Class 7, and cosmetic tools are typically Class 21 or 8.

Purpose: Absorbing shocks and vibrations in vehicle suspension systems
Expanded_Name: Suspension shock absorbers for vehicles
Logic_Trace: Class 12 covers "Vehicles; apparatus for locomotion by land, air or water". Suspension shock absorbers are critical parts of vehicles, contributing to their function and safety during locomotion. Shock absorbers for other machinery are Class 7, and tools are Class 8.

Purpose: Connecting railway carriages and locomotives
Expanded_Name: Mechanical couplings for railway vehicles
Logic_Trace: Class 12 covers "Vehicles; apparatus for locomotion by land, air or water". Railway couplings are essential mechanical components that physically connect railway vehicles, enabling them to form a train and thus facilitating locomotion. Couplings for other uses (e.g., pipes) are in other classes (e.g., Class 6 for metal).

Original_Term: Crankcases for land vehicle components, other than for engines
Purpose: Enclosing and protecting mechanical components within land vehicles (excluding engines)
Expanded_Name: Crankcases for non-engine mechanical components of land vehicles
Logic_Trace: Class 12 covers "Vehicles; apparatus for locomotion by land, air or water". Crankcases are typically protective housings for moving parts. If they are for 'land vehicle components', even if not the engine itself, they are still parts of vehicles and contribute to their structure or function. Engine crankcases are also Class 12. Crankcases for non-vehicle machinery are Class 7.

Purpose: Blocking mud and water splashes when riding bicycles.
Expanded_Name: Protective shields for blocking mud and water splashes when riding bicycles
Logic_Trace: The term 'Bicycle mudguards' combined with Class 12 ('Vehicles; apparatus for locomotion by land, air or water') clearly points to parts or accessories for bicycles that protect from mud. 'Decorative accessories' is too broad, and a 'specific brand' is not the nature of the product itself.

Purpose: Connecting the spokes to the axle and allowing the wheel to rotate on a bicycle.
Expanded_Name: Central mechanical components for connecting spokes to the axle and allowing the wheel to rotate on a bicycle
Logic_Trace: Class 12 confirms we are dealing with physical parts of vehicles. 'Central components of bicycle wheels' fits perfectly. 'Connection points' is a function but not the primary nature. 'Information centers' is completely out of context.

Purpose: Propelling bicycles by transferring power from the rider's feet.
Expanded_Name: Foot-operated levers for propelling bicycles by transferring power from the rider's feet
Logic_Trace: Class 12 points to vehicle parts. 'Foot-operated levers for propelling a bicycle' is the direct and correct meaning. The other options are clearly out of context.

Purpose: Protecting occupants in automobiles during a collision.
Expanded_Name: Inflatable safety cushions for protecting occupants in automobiles during a collision
Logic_Trace: The explicit description '[safety devices for automobiles]' combined with Class 12 makes it clear. 'Inflatable safety cushions for vehicles' is the only correct interpretation. The other options are irrelevant.

Purpose: Generating and transmitting power to move land vehicles.
Expanded_Name: Mechanical or electrical systems for generating and transmitting power to move land vehicles
Logic_Trace: Class 12 ('Vehicles; apparatus for locomotion...') directly relates to the movement of vehicles. 'Systems that generate motion for land vehicles' is the accurate interpretation. 'Engines or motors' is a type of mechanism, but 'systems' is broader and more encompassing as per the term. 'Marketing strategies' is entirely unrelated.

Purpose: Engaging and disengaging the engine from the transmission in land vehicles.
Expanded_Name: Mechanical coupling devices for engaging and disengaging the engine from the transmission in land vehicles

Logic_Trace: Class 5 is for 'pharmaceuticals, medical and veterinary preparations'. The term 'medicated wipes' directly implies a medical purpose due to the medication, fitting perfectly within the class. Other types of wipes are not relevant.

Purpose: Applying pharmaceutical lotions for medical or therapeutic purposes, often to delicate skin areas
Expanded_Name: Disposable paper tissues pre-impregnated with pharmaceutical lotions for medical or therapeutic use
Logic_Trace: Class 5 covers 'pharmaceuticals, medical and veterinary preparations'. Tissues 'impregnated with pharmaceutical lotions' clearly fall under medical preparations, aligning with the class. Non-medicated tissues are excluded.

Purpose: Forming a seam between the upper and sole of footwear, adding durability and shape
Expanded_Name: Strips of material, known as welts, used as components in the manufacture of footwear
Logic_Trace: Class 25 is for 'Clothing, footwear, headwear'. 'Welts for footwear' explicitly defines them as components for shoes, fitting perfectly within the class description. Other meanings of 'welts' are irrelevant.

Purpose: Increasing traction and preventing slipping on various surfaces when applied to footwear
Expanded_Name: Detachable or integrated devices designed to provide non-slip properties for footwear
Logic_Trace: Class 25 is for 'Clothing, footwear, headwear'. 'Non-slipping devices for footwear' are accessories specifically designed for shoes, aligning with the class. Non-slip mats or general adhesives are outside this specific context.

Purpose: Providing members or guests with entertainment, educational, sporting, or cultural activities within a club setting
Expanded_Name: Services provided by clubs relating to entertainment or education for members and guests
Logic_Trace: Class 41 explicitly covers 'entertainment' and 'education'. The term 'Club services [entertainment or education]' directly specifies these activities, making it a perfect match for the class. Physical premises or administrative membership are distinct concepts.

Nature: Strips of leather with a fastening mechanism
Purpose: Controlling and guiding animals, particularly dogs, during walks or training
Expanded_Name: Leashes made of leather for leading or restraining animals
Logic_Trace: Class 18 explicitly lists 'leather and imitations of leather' and 'collars, leashes and clothing for animals'. 'Leather leashes' perfectly combines both the material and the specific animal accessory, making it a direct match. Leashes of other materials or for humans are excluded.

Purpose: Recording, transmitting, reproducing, or processing sound and images
Expanded_Name: Electronic apparatus for recording, transmitting, reproducing, or processing sound and images
Logic_Trace: The Class 9 description explicitly mentions 'audiovisual apparatus and instruments' and 'apparatus and instruments for recording, transmitting, reproducing or processing sound, images or data'. This aligns perfectly with all brainstormed meanings, indicating it refers to a broad category of electronic equipment for visual and auditory media.

Purpose: Playing computer and video games
Expanded_Name: Physical hardware device for playing computer and video games
Logic_Trace: The Class 28 description lists 'Games, toys and playthings; video game apparatus'. This clearly indicates that 'Computer game apparatus' refers to the physical hardware used for playing games, not the software itself. 'Software' would fall under Class 9. Arcade machines are also a form of apparatus.

Nature: Printed sheets of paper or cardboard
Purpose: Advertising or conveying information
Expanded_Name: Printed advertising leaflets made of paper or cardboard
Logic_Trace: The Class 16 description includes 'printed matter' and 'paper and cardboard'. This strongly indicates that 'Flyers' refers to printed advertising materials. The other meanings (people, animals, machines) are irrelevant to this class.

Purpose: Storing and transmitting electronic data
Expanded_Name: Physical media or devices for storing and transmitting electronic data
Logic_Trace: The Class 9 description includes 'recorded and downloadable media, computer software, blank digital or analogue recording and storage media' and 'apparatus and instruments for recording, transmitting, reproducing or processing sound, images or data'. This confirms that 'Electronic data carriers' refers to physical media for storing and carrying data. Network infrastructure or protocols are services or software, not physical carriers within this context.

Purpose: For use in agriculture, horticulture, or food production (e.g., malting)
Expanded_Name: Raw and unprocessed grains, including their germ, for agricultural or food production purposes
Logic_Trace: The Class 31 description specifies 'Raw and unprocessed agricultural, aquacultural, horticultural and forestry products; raw and unprocessed grains and seeds; malt'. This confirms that 'Germ grains' refers to grains in their raw or unprocessed state, specifically relating to their germinating or seed properties, such as malt. Contaminated grains or just the embryo part are too specific or out of context.

Purpose: Manufacturing or use as fabric
Expanded_Name: Textile fabric made from esparto grass
Logic_Trace: The Class 24 description is 'Textiles and substitutes for textiles; household linen; curtains of textile or plastic'. This clearly indicates that 'Esparto fabric' refers to a textile material, aligning with 'fabric made from esparto grass'. Paper products would be Class 16.

Original_Term: Shower curtains of textile or plastic
Purpose: Enclosing showers and preventing water splash, made of textile or plastic
Expanded_Name: Curtains of textile or plastic for use in showers
Logic_Trace: The Class 24 description explicitly mentions 'curtains of textile or plastic' and 'household linen'. This term is already very specific and clearly falls within the class as a type of household textile or plastic item used for showers. The term itself is very descriptive.

Purpose: Serving and drinking beer
Expanded_Name: Drinking glasses specifically designed for beer
Logic_Trace: The Class 21 description includes 'Household or kitchen utensils and containers; cookware and tableware... glassware, porcelain and earthenware'. This indicates that 'Beer glasses' are drinking vessels for household or commercial use. Eyeglasses would be Class 9, and brewing equipment components would be Class 7 or 11.

Purpose: Human consumption as a seafood dish
Expanded_Name: Prepared food product consisting primarily of crab meat, shaped into cakes
Logic_Trace: The Class 29 description lists 'Meat, fish, poultry and game; meat extracts; preserved, frozen, dried and cooked fruits and vegetables'. 'Crab cakes' clearly refers to a cooked food product primarily made from fish/seafood (crab meat). Decorative cakes would be Class 30, and feed for crabs would be Class 31.

Purpose: Conducting, switching, transforming, accumulating, regulating, or controlling electrical current in apparatus
Expanded_Name: Electrical components forming circuits for conducting, switching, transforming, accumulating, regulating, or controlling electricity in apparatus
Logic_Trace: The Class 9 description includes 'apparatus and instruments for conducting, switching, transforming, accumulating, regulating or controlling the distribution or use of electricity'. Electronic circuits are fundamental components that perform these functions within electronic devices. Design schematics are services (Class 42) and a complete path for current is too general.

Purpose: Processing and storing data or performing logical operations within electronic apparatus
Expanded_Name: Integrated circuit (IC) chips for use in electronic apparatus
Logic_Trace: The Class 9 description covers electronic apparatus and components. The square brackets '[integrated circuits]' explicitly define the meaning of 'Chips' in this context, aligning it with microchips used in electronic devices. Snack food (Class 29 or 30), wood chips (Class 31 or 4 if fuel), or poker chips (Class 28) are irrelevant.

Purpose: Processing and storing data or performing logical operations within electronic apparatus
Expanded_Name: Integrated circuit (IC) chips made of semiconductor materials
Logic_Trace: The Class 9 description includes 'apparatus and instruments for conducting, switching, transforming, accumulating, regulating or controlling the distribution or use of electricity'. Semiconductor chips are essential components for performing these functions in modern electronics. Raw semiconductor material (Class 1) or manufacturing tools (Class 7) would fall into different classes.

Purpose: Human consumption as a food product
Expanded_Name: Cooked and preserved beans in cans for human consumption
Logic_Trace: The Class 29 description includes 'preserved, frozen, dried and cooked fruits and vegetables'. 'Canned beans' clearly falls into the category of preserved vegetables for human consumption. Beans for planting (Class 31) or animal feed (Class 31) are incorrect contexts.

Purpose: Human consumption as a food product
Expanded_Name: Cooked and preserved pulses in cans for human consumption
Logic_Trace: The Class 29 description includes 'preserved, frozen, dried and cooked fruits and vegetables'. Pulses are a type of legume (vegetable), and 'canned' indicates preservation. This aligns perfectly with foodstuffs for human consumption. Planting pulses (Class 31) or mechanical pulses (various classes depending on context) are not applicable.

Purpose: Human consumption as a food item
Expanded_Name: Processed and preserved dried fruit products for human consumption
Logic_Trace: The Class 29 description includes 'preserved, frozen, dried and cooked fruits and vegetables'. 'Dried fruit products' clearly refers to fruits that have undergone a drying process for preservation and consumption as food. Cosmetic products (Class 3) or decorative items (various classes) are out of scope.

Purpose: Providing schedules or information about events, performances, or broadcasts
Expanded_Name: Printed programmes, brochures, or booklets containing schedules or information
Logic_Trace: The Class 16 description includes 'printed matter'. This clearly indicates that 'Printed programmes' refers to physical printed documents, such as event schedules or leaflets. Computer programs (software) would be Class 9, and TV/radio schedules are information, not a physical product for this class.

Purpose: Conducting scientific and technological research specifically related to data processing methods and systems
Expanded_Name: Scientific and technological research services relating to data processing
Logic_Trace: The Class 42 description mentions 'Scientific and technological services and research and design relating thereto; industrial analysis, industrial research... design and development of computer hardware and software'. This confirms that the term refers to the *service* of conducting research, rather than physical equipment or published materials (which would be Class 9 or 16, respectively).

Purpose: Disseminating real-time or predictive data regarding road conditions and traffic flow to assist with transport and travel
Expanded_Name: Information services providing data on road conditions and traffic for travel and transport purposes
Logic_Trace: The Class 39 description includes 'Transport; packaging and storage of goods; travel arrangement'. While not explicitly listing 'information', providing road and traffic information is directly related to facilitating transport and travel arrangements. This is clearly a service, not a physical product like signs (Class 6 or 9) or software (Class 9).

Purpose: Attesting to facts, achievements, or ownership
Expanded_Name: Printed paper certificates attesting to facts, achievements, or ownership
Logic_Trace: The Class 16 description explicitly includes 'printed matter' and 'paper and cardboard'. This confirms that 'Printed certificates' refers to physical documents printed on paper or similar material. Digital certificates (Class 9) or financial instruments (Class 36) are incorrect meanings for this class.

Purpose: Remotely controlling the functions of other electronic devices
Expanded_Name: Handheld electronic remote control apparatus for operating other devices
Logic_Trace: The Class 9 description includes 'apparatus and instruments for conducting, switching, transforming, accumulating, regulating or controlling the distribution or use of electricity'. Remote controls are electronic apparatus designed specifically for controlling other electronic devices. This aligns perfectly. Systems or mechanisms for remote operation are too broad; the term specifically points to the physical controller.

Purpose: Recording, storing, and reproducing audio, video, or data
Expanded_Name: Magnetic tape cassettes for recording, storing, and reproducing audio, video, or data
Logic_Trace: The Class 9 description includes 'apparatus and instruments for recording, transmitting, reproducing or processing sound, images or data; recorded and downloadable media, computer software, blank digital or analogue recording and storage media'. Tape cassettes, particularly for audio/video, fall directly under 'recorded and downloadable media' or 'blank digital or analogue recording and storage media'. Other meanings are not relevant.

Purpose: Protecting individuals in medical environments from contamination or injury
Expanded_Name: Protective clothing specifically designed for medical purposes
Logic_Trace: The Class 10 description includes 'Surgical, medical, dental and veterinary apparatus and instruments'. Protective clothing used in a medical context, such as gowns or masks, serves a direct medical or hygienic purpose. Fashion clothing (Class 25) or therapeutic garments (also Class 10, but distinct purpose) are different.

Purpose: Providing medical, healing, or assistive therapy to the wearer
Expanded_Name: Garments specifically designed for therapeutic purposes for human beings
Logic_Trace: The Class 10 description includes 'Surgical, medical, dental and veterinary apparatus and instruments; therapeutic and assistive devices adapted for persons with disabilities'. Therapeutic garments, such as compression socks or post-operative wear, directly fall under therapeutic devices. Stylish clothing (Class 25) or clothing for therapists (Class 25) is not applicable.

Purpose: Assisting in the care and feeding of infants
Expanded_Name: Apparatus and devices for nursing infants
Logic_Trace: The Class 10 description explicitly lists 'apparatus, devices and articles for nursing infants'. This confirms the meaning relates to infant care. Household appliances (Class 11) or general medical devices for nurses (broadly Class 10, but more specific definition needed) are not the primary meaning here.

Purpose: Applying therapeutic pressure to the body for medical or assistive purposes
Expanded_Name: Compression garments for therapeutic and medical purposes
Logic_Trace: The Class 10 description includes 'Surgical, medical, dental and veterinary apparatus and instruments; therapeutic and assistive devices adapted for persons with disabilities'. Compression garments, often used for medical conditions like lymphatic swelling or post-surgery recovery, are clearly therapeutic devices. Athletic wear (Class 25) or fashion apparel (Class 25) are distinct.

Purpose: Protecting human skin from harmful sun exposure
Expanded_Name: Non-medicated cosmetic creams for sun protection
Logic_Trace: The Class 3 description includes 'Non-medicated cosmetics and toiletry preparations'. The term explicitly states '[cosmetics]', clarifying its nature as a non-medicated personal care product for skin protection. Medicinal creams (Class 5) or industrial coatings (Class 2) are outside this scope.

Purpose: Providing advisory, management, and transactional support for investments in real estate
Expanded_Name: Financial services relating to investment in real estate
Logic_Trace: The Class 36 description includes 'Financial, monetary and banking services; insurance services; real estate services'. This confirms that 'Real estate investment' refers to the financial services involved in property investments, not the physical properties (which are goods or assets, not services in this class) or the act of purchase itself (which is part of the service).

Purpose: Providing care for personal hygiene and aesthetic improvement for human beings or animals
Expanded_Name: Hygienic and beauty care services for human beings or animals
Logic_Trace: The Class 44 description includes 'Medical services; veterinary services; hygienic and beauty care for human beings or animals'. This clearly indicates that the term refers to *services* provided, not products (which would be Class 3 or 5) or educational programs (Class 41).

Original_Term: Hotels, hostels and boarding houses, holiday and tourist accommodation
Purpose: Providing temporary lodging and accommodation for travelers, tourists, or residents
Expanded_Name: Hospitality services for providing temporary accommodation including hotels, hostels, boarding houses, holiday, and tourist accommodation
Logic_Trace: The Class 43 description is 'Services for providing food and drink; temporary accommodation'. This explicitly covers the provision of temporary lodging, aligning perfectly with all parts of the term. The physical buildings (not a service class) or travel agency services (Class 39) are distinct.

Purpose: Providing a flat surface for various activities, holding objects, or dining
Expanded_Name: Furniture tables
Logic_Trace: The Class 20 description explicitly mentions "Furniture". Therefore, "Furniture items for holding objects or dining" is the correct meaning. Data tables and multiplication tables are unrelated to furniture.

Purpose: Displaying flowers and potted plants
Expanded_Name: Furniture stands for displaying flowers
Logic_Trace: The term itself includes "[furniture]" and Class 20 is "Furniture". Thus, "Furniture specifically designed to hold flowers/plants" is the correct meaning. The other meanings are outside the scope of furniture.

Purpose: Serving as an unprocessed artificial resin or plastic for industrial and scientific applications, often for its elastic properties
Expanded_Name: Unprocessed chemical silicone liquid elastomers for industrial and scientific use
Logic_Trace: Class 1 is for "Chemicals for use in industry, science... unprocessed artificial resins, unprocessed plastics". Silicone liquid elastomers fit perfectly as chemical compounds or unprocessed plastics for industrial use. Medical implants and cosmetic products are finished goods, not raw chemicals.

Purpose: Serving as a raw material in industrial and scientific processes, for manufacturing and other applications
Expanded_Name: Unprocessed chemical silicone resins for industrial use
Logic_Trace: Class 1 explicitly mentions "unprocessed artificial resins". Therefore, "Unprocessed chemical resins for industrial use" is the correct interpretation. Finished products or sealants (if ready for end-use) would typically be in other classes.

Purpose: Serving as a chemical component or raw material for various industrial, scientific, and manufacturing processes
Expanded_Name: Chemical silicone fluids for industrial and scientific use
Logic_Trace: Class 1 covers "Chemicals for use in industry, science". Silicone fluids are fundamental chemical compounds with diverse industrial applications. Lubricants are a specific use, but the general nature of the fluid itself as a chemical raw material fits Class 1. Cosmetic ingredients, if finished, would not be Class 1.

Purpose: Serving as raw materials in various industrial, scientific, and manufacturing applications, including as unprocessed artificial resins or plastics
Expanded_Name: Unprocessed chemical silicones for industrial and scientific use
Logic_Trace: Class 1 is for "Chemicals for use in industry, science and photography, as well as in agriculture, horticulture and forestry; unprocessed artificial resins, unprocessed plastics". In this context, "Silicones" refers to the raw chemical material or polymer, not finished products or medical devices.

Purpose: Providing a flame to ignite tobacco products for smokers
Expanded_Name: Lighters being smokers' articles
Logic_Trace: Class 34 explicitly lists "smokers' articles". "Lighters for smokers" perfectly aligns with this description. General ignition tools or pyrotechnic devices are not the specific focus of this class, which is about smoking accessories.

Purpose: Being used as a component for rolling cigarettes for smokers
Expanded_Name: Paper for cigarettes, being smokers' articles
Logic_Trace: Class 34 includes "smokers' articles". Cigarette paper is a quintessential smoker's article used for rolling tobacco. Generic paper or paper for industrial manufacturing is not the primary intent for this classification.

Purpose: Filtering smoke in cigarettes for smokers
Expanded_Name: Filters for cigarettes, being smokers' articles
Logic_Trace: Class 34 includes "smokers' articles". Cigarette filters are direct components of cigarettes or accessories for rolling them, clearly falling under smokers' articles. Air or water filters are unrelated.

Purpose: Supplying data, reports, and insights pertinent to business operations, management, and strategic decision-making
Expanded_Name: Business information provision services
Logic_Trace: Class 35 covers "Advertising; business management, organization and administration; office functions". The provision of business information is a core service related to business management and administration, which involves gathering, processing, and distributing information crucial for business operations. Selling specific reports or general consulting are related but the core service here is the *provision* of the information.

Purpose: Protecting medical professionals or patients during medical, surgical, or healthcare procedures
Expanded_Name: Gowns for medical purposes
Logic_Trace: Class 10 includes "Surgical, medical, dental and veterinary apparatus and instruments" and generally covers articles used in the medical field. Medical gowns, being protective or patient-care garments, directly fall under medical articles. Fashion gowns are unrelated.

Purpose: Securing saddles on horses as part of harness and saddlery
Expanded_Name: Leather girths for saddlery
Logic_Trace: Class 18 explicitly lists "harness and saddlery". Girths are essential components of saddlery, used to secure a saddle on a horse. The material (leather) is also covered. Belts or general straps are too broad; the specific context of saddlery is key.

Purpose: Forming the upper part of boots, enclosing the foot and ankle, and contributing to the footwear's structure
Expanded_Name: Uppers for boots [footwear parts]
Logic_Trace: Class 25 is for "Clothing, footwear, headwear". Boot uppers are components of footwear. The other meanings are unrelated to clothing or footwear.

Purpose: Sealing documents, envelopes, or other office-related materials within an office environment
Expanded_Name: Machines for sealing, for office use
Logic_Trace: Class 16 includes "stationery and office requisites, except furniture". Sealing machines used in an office context fit this description perfectly. Industrial packaging or food sealing machines are not typical office requisites.

Purpose: Being used as a material for bookbinding to secure pages or covers
Expanded_Name: Cords being bookbinding material
Logic_Trace: Class 16 explicitly covers "bookbinding material". Cords specifically made for bookbinding are a direct fit. General utility or electrical cords are unrelated to bookbinding materials.

Purpose: Being used as a covering or reinforcing material for bookbinding
Expanded_Name: Cloth being bookbinding material
Logic_Trace: Class 16 explicitly covers "bookbinding material". Cloth specifically designated for bookbinding is a direct fit. General purpose cloth or cleaning cloths are unrelated.

Purpose: Binding books and documents within an office environment
Expanded_Name: Bookbinding apparatus and machines for office use
Logic_Trace: Class 16 includes "stationery and office requisites, except furniture" and "bookbinding material". The term itself specifies "[office equipment]". This confirms the meaning as machines and tools for binding books in an office. Industrial equipment would be different, and craft tools are too general.

Purpose: Being used as a covering or reinforcing material for bookbinding
Expanded_Name: Fabrics being bookbinding material
Logic_Trace: Class 16 explicitly covers "bookbinding material". Fabrics specifically intended for bookbinding are a direct match. Other types of fabrics are outside this context.

Purpose: Transmitting electrical power to or between apparatus and instruments
Expanded_Name: Electrical power cables for conducting electricity
Logic_Trace: Class 9 includes "apparatus and instruments for conducting, switching, transforming, accumulating, regulating or controlling the distribution or use of electricity". Power cables are fundamental for conducting electricity, thus fitting this description. Data cables are a specific type, and anchor cables are entirely unrelated.

Purpose: Being consumed as a foodstuff
Expanded_Name: Prepared escargots for food consumption
Logic_Trace: Class 29 lists "Meat, fish, poultry and game" and "preserved, frozen, dried and cooked fruits and vegetables". Escargots are typically prepared snails served as a delicacy, fitting the "meat" or "preserved/cooked" category of food. Live animals would be Class 31.

Original_Term: Publication of online reviews in the field of entertainment
Purpose: Providing entertainment-related information and critical assessments to an online audience
Expanded_Name: Online publication services for entertainment reviews
Logic_Trace: Class 41 covers "Education; providing of training; entertainment; sporting and cultural activities". The publication of reviews, especially in the field of entertainment, is an activity directly related to entertainment, providing information and opinions on entertainment. It's a service. Creating the content is part of the service, and hosting is a separate service (Class 42).

Purpose: Being consumed as a fresh food item or used as a raw ingredient
Expanded_Name: Fresh citrus fruit [unprocessed]
Logic_Trace: Class 31 explicitly lists "fresh fruits and vegetables". "Citrus fruit, fresh" is a direct and perfect match for this category. Processed products would be in Class 29 or 32.

Purpose: Being consumed as a fresh food item or used as a raw ingredient
Expanded_Name: Fresh berries [unprocessed]
Logic_Trace: Class 31 explicitly lists "fresh fruits and vegetables". "Berries, fresh" is a direct and perfect match. Frozen berries would be Class 29, and candies Class 30.

Purpose: Being used as animal feed, fertilizer, or for further extraction, remaining in its raw or unprocessed state
Expanded_Name: Unprocessed fruit residue [marc], being an agricultural product
Logic_Trace: Class 31 includes "Raw and unprocessed agricultural... products". Fruit residue, or marc, is an unprocessed byproduct of fruit processing, often used for animal feed, fertilizer, or distillation, thus retaining its 'raw' agricultural product nature. Compost (Class 1) or finished fruit products (Class 29) are distinct.

Purpose: Being consumed as a fresh food item or used as a raw ingredient
Expanded_Name: Fresh oranges [unprocessed]
Logic_Trace: Class 31 explicitly lists "fresh fruits and vegetables". "Oranges, fresh" is a direct and perfect match for this category. Orange juice (Class 32) or artificial flavorings (Class 30 or 1) are processed or artificial.

Purpose: Being used as a food ingredient or consumed as a semi-finished food item
Expanded_Name: Processed fruit pulp for food
Logic_Trace: Class 29 includes "preserved, frozen, dried and cooked fruits and vegetables". Fruit pulp, while not necessarily "cooked" or "preserved" in the traditional sense, implies a degree of processing beyond "fresh" (Class 31) and is generally intended as a food ingredient or semi-finished food product. It's not raw agricultural produce.

Original_Term: Instruments for use in the preparation of artificial teeth
Purpose: Being used by dental professionals for the creation and preparation of artificial teeth
Expanded_Name: Dental instruments for the preparation of artificial teeth
Logic_Trace: Class 10 explicitly lists "dental apparatus and instruments" and "artificial... teeth". Instruments used in the *preparation* of artificial teeth clearly fall under dental instruments. Crafting tools or general lab instruments are too broad.

Original_Term: High frequency cutting instruments for dental purposes
Purpose: Cutting dental tissues or materials using high-frequency energy during dental procedures
Expanded_Name: High frequency cutting instruments for dental use
Logic_Trace: Class 10 explicitly lists "dental apparatus and instruments". Instruments using high frequency for cutting, specifically for dental purposes, are a direct fit. Industrial tools are unrelated, and general surgical tools (while covered by Class 10) are not as specific as "dental purposes".

Nature: Handheld dental instruments or parts thereof
Purpose: Facilitating various dental procedures by holding and operating rotating or vibrating tools
Expanded_Name: Hand pieces for dental instruments
Logic_Trace: Class 10 explicitly lists "dental apparatus and instruments". Dental hand pieces are fundamental, handheld components of dental equipment used by dentists. General hand tools or industrial machinery parts are not relevant.

Original_Term: Ultrasonic diagnostic instruments for dental use
Purpose: Diagnosing dental conditions and pathologies using ultrasonic technology
Expanded_Name: Ultrasonic diagnostic instruments for dental examination
Logic_Trace: Class 10 explicitly lists "dental apparatus and instruments". Ultrasonic diagnostic instruments, specifically for dental use, are a direct and precise fit. Cleaning devices or industrial sensors are not diagnostic medical instruments.

Original_Term: Electrical utensils for use by dentists in the care of the teeth
Purpose: Caring for teeth by dentists
Expanded_Name: Electrical dental instruments for professional teeth care
Logic_Trace: The context 'for use by dentists in the care of the teeth' and Nice Class 10 'dental apparatus and instruments' clearly point to specialized electrical instruments for dental purposes. The other meanings are irrelevant.

Purpose: Isolating teeth and surrounding tissues during dental procedures
Expanded_Name: Protective dental dams for isolating teeth during dental procedures
Logic_Trace: The prefix 'Dental' and Nice Class 10 'dental apparatus and instruments' confirm the meaning of a protective sheet used specifically in dentistry. The other meanings are incorrect given the specific term and class.

Original_Term: Apparatus for the application of laser radiation for dental purposes
Purpose: Administering laser radiation for dental treatments
Expanded_Name: Dental apparatus for the application of laser radiation
Logic_Trace: The phrase 'for dental purposes' and Nice Class 10 'dental apparatus and instruments' explicitly indicate apparatus for laser application in dentistry. General industrial or non-dental medical uses are excluded.

Original_Term: Tooth polishing appliances for use by dentists
Purpose: Polishing teeth professionally
Expanded_Name: Professional tooth polishing appliances for dental use
Logic_Trace: The phrase 'for use by dentists' and Nice Class 10 'dental apparatus and instruments' clearly specify professional dental appliances for tooth polishing. Personal or industrial uses are not applicable.

Purpose: Simulating jaw and tooth movements for dental analysis and prosthetic fabrication
Expanded_Name: Mechanical dental articulators for simulating jaw movement
Logic_Trace: The prefix 'Dental' and Nice Class 10 'dental apparatus and instruments' specify a mechanical device used in dentistry to simulate jaw movements for prosthetic work or diagnosis. The other meanings are irrelevant.

Purpose: Reflecting and illuminating the oral cavity for examination by dentists
Expanded_Name: Hand-held dental mirrors for oral examination
Logic_Trace: The phrase 'for dentists' and Nice Class 10 'dental apparatus and instruments' explicitly indicate specialized hand-held mirrors for professional oral examination. General household or decorative mirrors are excluded.

Purpose: Fabricating dental prosthetics and restorations by milling materials
Expanded_Name: Dental apparatus for milling dental prosthetics and restorations
Logic_Trace: The term 'Dental apparatus' and Nice Class 10 'dental apparatus and instruments' clearly refer to apparatus used for milling within the dental field. Industrial or food production milling are not relevant.

Original_Term: Devices [oral irrigators] for use by dentists in washing the gums
Purpose: Washing and cleansing gums and oral cavities during dental procedures
Expanded_Name: Oral irrigators for professional use by dentists in washing gums
Logic_Trace: The explicit mention of '[oral irrigators]' and 'for use by dentists in washing the gums' along with Nice Class 10 'dental apparatus and instruments' confirms the professional dental use of these devices.

Original_Term: Electrical oral irrigators for use by dentists
Purpose: Professionally irrigating oral cavities and gums
Expanded_Name: Electrical oral irrigators for professional use by dentists
Logic_Trace: The term 'Electrical oral irrigators' combined with 'for use by dentists' and Nice Class 10 'dental apparatus and instruments' specifically points to professional electrical devices for oral irrigation, not personal or general cleaning devices.

Original_Term: Electrical utensils for use by dentists in the care of the mouth
Purpose: Providing professional oral care by dentists
Expanded_Name: Electrical dental instruments for professional oral care
Logic_Trace: The context 'for use by dentists in the care of the mouth' and Nice Class 10 'dental apparatus and instruments' clearly define these as specialized electrical instruments for dental oral care. Generic kitchen or medical tools are inappropriate.

Purpose: Providing foundational support for dental prosthetics or during dental procedures
Expanded_Name: Dental support structures for prosthetics and procedures
Logic_Trace: The term 'Dental' and Nice Class 10 'dental apparatus and instruments; artificial limbs, eyes and teeth' confirm that these are supportive structures used in a dental context. Construction or cosmetic applications are irrelevant.

Purpose: Administering injections or performing procedures in dentistry
Expanded_Name: Surgical needles for dental purposes
Logic_Trace: The phrase 'for dental purposes' and Nice Class 10 'dental apparatus and instruments' specifically identify these as needles used in dentistry. General medical or non-medical needles are not the correct context.

Purpose: Performing various dental treatments and procedures
Expanded_Name: Laser apparatus for dental treatments
Logic_Trace: The phrase 'for dental purposes' and Nice Class 10 'dental apparatus and instruments' clearly indicates lasers intended for dental applications. Industrial or general medical lasers are not in context.

Purpose: Administering substances or aspirating fluids during dental procedures
Expanded_Name: Medical syringes for dental purposes
Logic_Trace: The phrase 'for dental purposes' and Nice Class 10 'dental apparatus and instruments' confirms that these are syringes tailored for dental use. General medical or industrial syringes are outside this specific context.

Original_Term: Instruments for use in the fitting of artificial teeth
Purpose: Facilitating the fitting and adjustment of artificial teeth
Expanded_Name: Dental instruments for fitting artificial teeth
Logic_Trace: The phrase 'for use in the fitting of artificial teeth' and Nice Class 10 'dental apparatus and instruments; artificial limbs, eyes and teeth' clearly points to specialized dental instruments. General or household tools are not relevant.

Original_Term: Apparatus for use in the repair of teeth
Purpose: Performing restorative procedures for damaged teeth
Expanded_Name: Dental apparatus for tooth repair
Logic_Trace: The phrase 'for use in the repair of teeth' and Nice Class 10 'dental apparatus and instruments' confirms apparatus specifically for dental tooth repair. General repair tools are not applicable.

Purpose: Curing and hardening dental materials through polymerization
Expanded_Name: Polymerization apparatus for curing dental materials
Logic_Trace: The phrase 'for dental purposes' and Nice Class 10 'dental apparatus and instruments' specifies polymerization apparatus used in dentistry for curing dental materials. Industrial or general laboratory equipment is not in context.

Original_Term: Apparatus for use in identifying periodontal disease
Purpose: Detecting and diagnosing periodontal disease
Expanded_Name: Dental diagnostic apparatus for identifying periodontal disease
Logic_Trace: The phrase 'for use in identifying periodontal disease' and Nice Class 10 'dental apparatus and instruments' clearly points to specialized dental diagnostic apparatus for this specific condition. General medical or research apparatus are too broad.

Purpose: Removing fluids, saliva, and debris from the oral cavity during dental procedures
Expanded_Name: Dental suction apparatus for oral fluid and debris removal

Logic_Trace: The term 'Dental curing lights' and Nice Class 10 'dental apparatus and instruments' explicitly refer to lights used to cure dental materials. General illumination or photographic lights are not applicable.

Expanded_Name: Precision dental drill bits for preparing teeth

Purpose: Providing loans secured by real estate
Expanded_Name: Financial lending services secured by real estate
Logic_Trace: The term 'Real estate lending services' and Nice Class 36 'Financial, monetary and banking services; real estate services' directly confirm financial services involving loans secured by real estate. Other types of lending are not specified.

Purpose: Determining financial value or risk specifically for insurance underwriting or claims
Expanded_Name: Financial evaluation services for insurance risk and value assessment
Logic_Trace: The phrase 'Financial evaluation for insurance purposes' and Nice Class 36 'Financial, monetary and banking services; insurance services' explicitly indicates financial assessments related to insurance. Other types of financial evaluation are not the correct context.
