Expanded_Name: Financial evaluation services provided by banks
Logic_Trace: The term 'Financial evaluations [banking]' and Nice Class 36 'Financial, monetary and banking services' clearly specify financial assessment services conducted by banks. Academic research or personal planning are not the core activity described.

Purpose: Managing financial, monetary, and real estate assets on behalf of others
Expanded_Name: Financial and real estate asset management services
Logic_Trace: Class 36 specifies 'Financial, monetary and banking services; insurance services; real estate services'. The meaning of managing financial investments and portfolios, or managing real estate assets, directly aligns with the class description. Software (Class 9) is rejected.

Purpose: Creating architectural and structural designs for buildings
Expanded_Name: Architectural building design services
Logic_Trace: Class 42 includes 'Scientific and technological services and research and design relating thereto; industrial design services'. Providing services for creating architectural designs and plans for buildings fits perfectly within this description. Interior design (often also Class 42) is more specific, and software (Class 9) is rejected.

Purpose: Providing expert advice and guidance in the field of architecture
Expanded_Name: Architectural advisory and consulting services
Logic_Trace: Class 42 covers 'Scientific and technological services and research and design relating thereto'. Architectural consultancy, which involves providing expert advice in the architectural field, falls under the realm of specialized design and technical services. Software (Class 9) is rejected.

Original_Term: Technological research for the building construction industry
Purpose: Conducting scientific and technological investigations and analysis for advancements in the building construction industry
Expanded_Name: Scientific and technological research services for the building construction industry
Logic_Trace: Class 42 explicitly includes 'Scientific and technological services and research and design relating thereto' and 'industrial research services'. This term is a direct and precise match for the services described in this class. Reports (Class 16) or internal departments are not the service itself.

Purpose: Developing plans and designs for civil engineering projects and infrastructure
Expanded_Name: Professional civil engineering planning and design services
Logic_Trace: Class 42 covers 'Scientific and technological services and research and design relating thereto'. Professional civil engineering planning and design services are a core component of these technical and scientific services. Software (Class 9) or educational courses (Class 41) are rejected.

Original_Term: Latex to be used in the manufacture of inflatable playthings
Purpose: Serving as a raw material in the manufacture of various products, specifically inflatable playthings
Expanded_Name: Semi-processed natural latex for manufacturing inflatable playthings
Logic_Trace: Class 17 lists 'Unprocessed and semi-processed rubber, gutta-percha, gum, asbestos, mica and substitutes for all these materials'. Latex, especially specified as a material 'to be used in the manufacture', perfectly fits this description as a semi-processed raw material. Finished playthings (Class 28) are rejected.

Purpose: Imparting color to materials or surfaces, used as colorants, dyes, pigments, or inks
Expanded_Name: Industrial and artistic dyes, colorants, pigments, and inks
Logic_Trace: Class 2 explicitly lists 'colorants, dyes; inks for printing, marking and engraving'. This term is a direct and comprehensive match for the items in this class. Cosmetic dyes (Class 3) or specific art supplies (which might combine items from multiple classes) are distinct.

Purpose: Serving as a nutritious food source for human consumption
Expanded_Name: Prepared edible seeds for human consumption
Logic_Trace: Class 29 includes 'preserved, frozen, dried and cooked fruits and vegetables; oils and fats for food'. Edible seeds, often dried or prepared for consumption, fall under the general category of food products. Seeds for planting (Class 31) are rejected, and seed oils are a different product form.

Purpose: Used as a food ingredient or dietary supplement
Expanded_Name: Edible linseed oil for food purposes
Logic_Trace: Class 29 explicitly lists 'oils and fats for food'. The term 'Linseed oil for food' is a direct and perfect match. Linseed oil for industrial use (Class 2 or 3) is rejected, and flax seeds are a different product form.

Purpose: Conducting systematic investigations to acquire new technical knowledge or to develop new technologies
Expanded_Name: Professional technical research services
Logic_Trace: Class 42 covers 'Scientific and technological services and research and design relating thereto; industrial analysis, industrial research services'. 'Technical research' directly aligns with these descriptions of professional services. Reports (Class 16) or funding (Class 36) are rejected.

Purpose: Conducting systematic investigations to acquire new scientific knowledge or to understand fundamental principles
Expanded_Name: Professional scientific research services
Logic_Trace: Class 42 explicitly covers 'Scientific and technological services and research and design relating thereto'. 'Scientific research' is a direct and fundamental service described within this class. Publications (Class 16) or laboratory operation (Class 42, but distinct from the research service itself) are rejected.

Purpose: Equipping and decorating residential or commercial interior spaces
Expanded_Name: Movable furniture and related household furnishings (not including textiles or lighting)
Logic_Trace: Class 20 explicitly lists 'Furniture, mirrors, picture frames'. While 'furnishings' can be broad, in this context, it refers to items typically associated with furniture or general household articles not classified elsewhere (e.g., decorative objects of wood, plastic, etc.). Textile furnishings (Class 24), lamps (Class 11), or carpets (Class 27) are distinct categories. Therefore, this refers to the furniture itself and closely related non-textile, non-electric decorative articles for interior spaces.

Purpose: Regulating, managing, or controlling the distribution and use of electricity
Expanded_Name: Electronic apparatus for controlling electricity
Logic_Trace: Class 9 includes 'apparatus and instruments for conducting, switching, transforming, accumulating, regulating or controlling the distribution or use of electricity'. 'Electric control apparatus' is a direct match, covering physical devices that regulate or control electricity. Remote controls and software are also in Class 9 but are more specific forms.

Purpose: Transmitting, receiving, or processing signals for telecommunication purposes
Expanded_Name: Electronic telecommunications apparatus for transmitting and receiving signals
Logic_Trace: Class 9 includes 'apparatus and instruments for recording, transmitting, reproducing or processing sound, images or data'. 'Electric telecommunications apparatus' clearly falls within the scope of devices used for transmitting and receiving data. Infrastructure components can be various classes (e.g., cables Class 9, masts Class 6), and services (Class 38) are rejected.

Purpose: Carrying, transporting, and protecting laptop computers
Expanded_Name: Protective carrying bags specially adapted for laptop computers
Logic_Trace: Class 9 includes 'computers and computer peripheral devices'. Bags 'adapted for' laptops are considered accessories for these electronic devices, and Class 9 often covers protective cases for specific electronic items (e.g., mobile phones, tablets). General bags (Class 18) are rejected as these are specifically adapted. Laptops (Class 9) are the device itself.

Purpose: Holding and protecting passports and other travel documents
Expanded_Name: Passport holders made of paper, plastic or non-leather materials
Logic_Trace: Class 16 includes 'stationery and office requisites'. Passport holders, when made of materials like paper, cardboard, or plastic, fit into this class as a type of document holder. Leather passport holders (Class 18) and digital apps (Class 9) are rejected.

Purpose: Holding and protecting cheque books
Expanded_Name: Cheque book holders made of paper, plastic or non-leather materials
Logic_Trace: Class 16 includes 'stationery and office requisites'. Holders for cheque books, typically made of paper, cardboard, or plastic, fit this category as a type of document holder. Leather cheque book holders (Class 18) and digital apps (Class 9) are rejected.

Purpose: Serving as a textile or textile substitute for various applications
Expanded_Name: Fibreglass fabrics specifically intended for textile manufacturing and use
Logic_Trace: Class 24 explicitly lists 'Textiles and substitutes for textiles'. Fibreglass fabrics, when designated 'for textile use', fall directly into this category as a specialized textile or textile substitute. Finished products would be classified by their nature (e.g., Class 12 for boat hulls, Class 17 for insulation), and raw materials (Class 17) are rejected.

Purpose: Manufacturing and assembling goods according to specific client designs or requirements
Expanded_Name: Industrial custom manufacturing and assembly services
Logic_Trace: Class 40 is titled 'Treatment of materials'. Custom manufacturing and assembly involve the transformation or treatment of raw materials or components into a finished product for others, fitting this class. The manufactured goods would be classified by their nature, and design services (Class 42) are rejected.

Original_Term: Printing, and photographic and cinematographic development
Purpose: Reproducing images and text through printing, and processing photographic and cinematographic film
Expanded_Name: Printing services and photographic and cinematographic film development services
Logic_Trace: Class 40 explicitly includes 'printing services' and broadly covers 'treatment of materials'. Photographic and cinematographic development involves the chemical treatment of film, which is a 'treatment of materials'. This term is a direct and comprehensive match. Equipment (Class 7 or 9) and final products (Class 16 or 9) are rejected.

Purpose: Processing, preserving, or preparing food and beverages for consumption or storage
Expanded_Name: Industrial food and beverage treatment and preservation services
Logic_Trace: Class 40 explicitly includes 'food and drink preservation' and broadly covers 'treatment of materials'. 'Food and beverage treatment' encompasses various processes that modify or preserve food items, directly fitting the class. Food/beverages (Class 29, 30, 32) and equipment (Class 7) are rejected.

Original_Term: Duplication of audio and video recordings
Purpose: Copying and reproducing audio and video content onto various recording media
Expanded_Name: Duplication services for audio and video recordings
Logic_Trace: Class 40 covers 'treatment of materials' and includes services related to reproduction and processing of media. Duplication of recordings involves treating blank media or existing digital files to create copies, fitting this class. The duplicated recordings (Class 9) and equipment (Class 9) are rejected.

Original_Term: Air and water conditioning and purification
Purpose: Improving the quality of air and water through conditioning, filtering, and purification processes
Expanded_Name: Air and water conditioning and purification services
Logic_Trace: Class 40 explicitly includes 'air purification and treatment of water'. The term is a direct and perfect match for the services described within this class. Apparatus (Class 11) and chemicals (Class 1) are rejected.

Original_Term: Research and development in the field of biotechnology
Purpose: Conducting scientific and technological investigations to innovate and develop new applications in the field of biotechnology
Expanded_Name: Scientific and technological research and development services in the field of biotechnology
Logic_Trace: Class 42 explicitly covers 'Scientific and technological services and research and design relating thereto' and 'industrial research services'. This term is a direct and precise match for professional services in biotechnology R&D. Products (various classes) and facilities are not the service itself.

Purpose: Conducting systematic analysis and research for industrial applications and development
Expanded_Name: Professional industrial analysis and research services
Logic_Trace: Class 42 explicitly includes 'industrial analysis, industrial research and industrial design services'. This term is a direct and perfect match for the professional services described within this class. Reports (Class 16) and general business consulting (Class 35) are rejected.

Original_Term: Advertising services relating to the sale of motor vehicles
Purpose: Promoting the sale of motor vehicles through various advertising media and campaigns
Expanded_Name: Advertising services specifically for the sale of motor vehicles
Logic_Trace: Class 35 explicitly lists 'Advertising'. The specific subject matter ('relating to the sale of motor vehicles') clarifies the target of the advertising but does not change the fundamental nature of the service, which is advertising. Motor vehicles (Class 12) and sales services (also Class 35 or 37 depending on scope) are distinct from the advertising service itself.

Purpose: Propelling watercraft through manual force
Expanded_Name: Oars for propelling water vehicles
Logic_Trace: Class 12 includes 'Vehicles; apparatus for locomotion by land, air or water'. Oars are fundamental apparatus for manual propulsion of watercraft, directly fitting the description of apparatus for locomotion by water. Decorative oars (Class 20) and exercise equipment components (Class 28) are rejected.

Purpose: Disconnecting or decoupling specific mechanisms or systems on boats
Expanded_Name: Mechanical disengaging gear for marine vessels
Logic_Trace: Class 12 covers 'Vehicles; apparatus for locomotion by land, air or water'. Disengaging gear for boats is a specialized mechanical component of boats, which are vehicles for locomotion by water. Thus, it fits within this class. General boat parts might be in other classes, and safety equipment depends on its nature.

Purpose: Venting exhaust gases from a ship's engine or other systems
Expanded_Name: Exhaust funnels for ships and marine vessels
Logic_Trace: Class 12 includes 'Vehicles; apparatus for locomotion by land, air or water'. Funnels for ships, serving as essential structural and functional parts of a ship (a water vehicle), clearly belong to this class. General-purpose funnels (Class 21) and navigational instruments (Class 9) are rejected.

Purpose: Holding oars in position for rowing on a boat
Expanded_Name: Rowlocks for holding oars on boats
Logic_Trace: Class 12 covers 'Vehicles; apparatus for locomotion by land, air or water'. Rowlocks are specific mechanical fittings used in conjunction with oars for the propulsion of boats, making them a component part of water vehicles. Exercise machine parts (Class 28) and security locks (Class 6) are rejected.

Purpose: Forming the internal support structure for ships
Expanded_Name: Structural wooden frames for ships
Logic_Trace: The NICE Class 12 description 'Vehicles; apparatus for locomotion by land, air or water' clearly indicates items related to water vehicles. The term 'Timbers [frames] for ships' directly specifies a component for ships, making the interpretation of structural wooden components for shipbuilding the correct meaning. General wood for construction or forested areas are irrelevant in this context.

Purpose: Launching and hauling boats
Expanded_Name: Inclined slipways for launching and hauling boats
Logic_Trace: The NICE Class 12 description 'Vehicles; apparatus for locomotion by land, air or water' points to equipment for water vehicles. 'Inclined ways for boats' refers to physical structures aiding boat movement to and from water. This aligns perfectly with slipways for launching and hauling. General non-level paths or metaphorical meanings are incorrect.

Purpose: Supporting sails or rigging on boats
Expanded_Name: Vertical support poles for sails or rigging on boats
Logic_Trace: The NICE Class 12 description 'Vehicles; apparatus for locomotion by land, air or water' makes the nautical interpretation clear. 'Masts for boats' are unequivocally the vertical supports for sails on watercraft. Other types of poles or botanical meanings are not relevant to vehicles.

Purpose: Forming the watertight shell and primary structure of ships
Expanded_Name: Main structural bodies of ships
Logic_Trace: The NICE Class 12 description 'Vehicles; apparatus for locomotion by land, air or water' clearly relates to watercraft. 'Ship hulls' refers to the primary structural component of a ship. Botanical coverings or verb forms are not applicable in this context.

Purpose: Propelling canoes through water
Expanded_Name: Hand-operated paddles for propelling canoes
Logic_Trace: The NICE Class 12 description 'Vehicles; apparatus for locomotion by land, air or water' indicates items for water vehicles. 'Paddles for canoes' are explicitly defined as propulsion tools for canoes. Other uses of paddles or the verb form are irrelevant here.

Purpose: Preventing damage to ships when docking or alongside other vessels
Expanded_Name: Protective cushioning devices for preventing damage to ships
Logic_Trace: The NICE Class 12 description 'Vehicles; apparatus for locomotion by land, air or water' specifies water vehicles. 'Fenders for ships' are well-known protective devices for marine vessels. Car fenders or fireplace screens are not relevant to ships.

Purpose: Raising and lowering boats on or from a larger vessel
Expanded_Name: Small cranes for raising and lowering boats on vessels
Logic_Trace: The NICE Class 12 description 'Vehicles; apparatus for locomotion by land, air or water' implies components for vessels. 'Davits for boats' refers specifically to the hoisting equipment on a larger vessel used to deploy or retrieve smaller boats. A surname or a general tool is incorrect.

Purpose: Controlling the direction and movement of ships
Expanded_Name: Mechanical steering systems for controlling the direction of ships
Logic_Trace: The NICE Class 12 description 'Vehicles; apparatus for locomotion by land, air or water' clarifies the context of water vehicles. 'Steering gears for ships' precisely defines the machinery responsible for maneuvering a ship. Land vehicle steering or general directional equipment are not specific enough.

Purpose: Securing ropes and lines on boats
Expanded_Name: Nautical fittings for securing ropes and lines on boats
Logic_Trace: The NICE Class 12 description 'Vehicles; apparatus for locomotion by land, air or water' combined with the explicit '[nautical]' qualifier in the term leaves no doubt that it refers to marine fittings for ropes. Shoe cleats or general reinforcing strips are inappropriate.

Purpose: Supporting sails, rigging, or other ship structures
Expanded_Name: Long, sturdy poles or beams for supporting sails or rigging on ships
Logic_Trace: The NICE Class 12 description 'Vehicles; apparatus for locomotion by land, air or water' points to ship components. 'Spars for ships' clearly denotes the structural poles on sailing vessels. The verb meanings are entirely unrelated.

Purpose: Propelling boats through water by a single rower using two oars
Expanded_Name: Rowing oars for propelling boats by a single rower
Logic_Trace: The NICE Class 12 description 'Vehicles; apparatus for locomotion by land, air or water' indicates components for water vehicles. 'Sculls' specifically refers to the type of oars used in competitive rowing. The verb form and anatomical meaning are incorrect in this context.

Purpose: Facilitating the sale of vehicles through auction
Expanded_Name: Auctioneering services for vehicles
Logic_Trace: The NICE Class 35 description 'Advertising; business management, organization and administration; office functions' covers services, especially commercial transactions and management. 'Auctioning of vehicles' is a service facilitating sales. The vehicles are goods (Class 12), and bidding is an action within the service.

Purpose: Directing the course of watercraft
Expanded_Name: Steering devices for directing the course of watercraft
Logic_Trace: The NICE Class 12 description 'Vehicles; apparatus for locomotion by land, air or water' focuses on marine and other transport vehicles. While rudders exist on aircraft, the broader context within Class 12 (and other nautical terms in this list) strongly suggests marine rudders as the primary interpretation. Therefore, steering devices for watercraft is the most fitting meaning.

Purpose: Being spread on food items like bread or toast for consumption
Expanded_Name: Edible chocolate-based spreads for food
Logic_Trace: The NICE Class 30 description lists 'chocolate' and various foodstuffs. 'Chocolate spreads' clearly refers to an edible product designed for consumption. Non-edible coatings or techniques are unrelated to this classification.

Purpose: Being spread on food items like bread or toast for consumption
Expanded_Name: Edible sweet spreads, including honey, for food
Logic_Trace: The NICE Class 30 description includes 'honey' and general foodstuffs. 'Sweet spreads [honey]' explicitly denotes an edible product for consumption. Cosmetics or distribution methods are not food products.

Purpose: For planting in gardens or landscapes
Expanded_Name: Live perennial woody plants for horticultural purposes
Logic_Trace: The NICE Class 31 description covers 'natural plants and flowers' and 'bulbs, seedlings and seeds for planting'. Live shrubs fit this botanical classification perfectly. The beverage or verb meanings are not relevant to live plants.

Original_Term: Retail services in relation to pet products
Purpose: Selling pet products to the general public
Expanded_Name: Retail store services featuring pet products
Logic_Trace: The NICE Class 35 description 'Advertising; business management, organization and administration; office functions' includes retail services. The term explicitly specifies 'Retail services', confirming it is a commercial service. The pet products are goods (various classes, e.g., 31 for pet food, 20 for pet beds), and shopping is a consumer activity, not the service itself.

Purpose: Cleaning teeth when attached to an electric toothbrush handle
Expanded_Name: Detachable brush heads for electric toothbrushes
Logic_Trace: The NICE Class 21 description includes 'brushes, except paintbrushes' and 'articles for cleaning purposes'. The term 'Heads for electric toothbrushes' refers to the replaceable brushing components for oral hygiene. The entire toothbrush (often Class 21 as an article for cleaning) or metaphorical meanings are incorrect.

Purpose: Guiding individuals in the preparation of food using recipes
Expanded_Name: Advisory services concerning cooking recipes for food preparation
Logic_Trace: The NICE Class 43 description 'Services for providing food and drink; temporary accommodation' relates to food provision. While not directly serving food, providing 'Advice concerning cooking recipes' is a service that facilitates food preparation, aligning with the broader scope of food and drink services. It's not education (Class 41) but more akin to culinary guidance impacting food provision.

Purpose: Visually presenting numerical or symbolic data
Expanded_Name: Electronic digital display indicators for data presentation
Logic_Trace: The NICE Class 9 description includes 'measuring, signalling... apparatus and instruments' and 'apparatus and instruments for recording, transmitting, reproducing or processing sound, images or data'. 'Digital indicators' are physical electronic devices for display, fitting perfectly. Software or specific data types are not the apparatus itself.

Purpose: Measuring and displaying time intervals or controlling events based on time
Expanded_Name: Electronic apparatus for timing and measuring purposes
Logic_Trace: The NICE Class 9 description covers 'measuring... apparatus and instruments'. 'Electronic timing apparatus' clearly refers to physical electronic devices used for time measurement and control, which is central to Class 9. Software or purely mechanical devices are excluded by 'apparatus' and 'electronic'.

Purpose: Capturing, storing, and reproducing various forms of data or signals
Expanded_Name: Electrical apparatus for recording and storing data
Logic_Trace: The NICE Class 9 description includes 'apparatus and instruments for recording, transmitting, reproducing or processing sound, images or data'. 'Electrical recorders' are physical devices for data capture, fitting this perfectly. Human recorders or resistance components are unrelated.

Original_Term: Electronic temperature recorders, other than for medical use
Purpose: Measuring and logging temperature data for non-medical applications
Expanded_Name: Electronic temperature recording instruments, not for medical use
Logic_Trace: The NICE Class 9 description covers 'scientific, research... measuring, signalling... apparatus and instruments' and 'apparatus and instruments for recording... data'. The term explicitly states 'other than for medical use', ensuring it falls within Class 9 for non-medical scientific/industrial recording. Medical devices are Class 10, and software is not the apparatus.

Purpose: Regulating or controlling events and processes based on time
Expanded_Name: Electronic instruments for timing and control purposes
Logic_Trace: The NICE Class 9 description includes 'measuring... apparatus and instruments' and 'apparatus and instruments for... regulating or controlling the distribution or use of electricity'. 'Electronic timing control instruments' perfectly describes devices for time-based regulation and control, fitting Class 9. Simple display devices or manual tools are not covered.

Purpose: Recording and displaying scores for games, competitions, or other activities
Expanded_Name: Electronic devices for recording and displaying scores
Logic_Trace: The NICE Class 9 description includes 'apparatus and instruments for recording... data'. The term 'Score recorders [electronic]' specifies electronic devices for recording numerical data (scores), which is a clear fit for Class 9. Manual devices or musical recording equipment (unless it's electronic for data) are not within scope.

Purpose: Logging and documenting periods of time for various applications
Expanded_Name: Apparatus for recording and tracking time
Logic_Trace: The NICE Class 9 description includes 'measuring... apparatus and instruments' and 'apparatus and instruments for recording... data'. 'Time recording apparatus' explicitly refers to physical devices that capture and log time data, fitting perfectly. Simple time display or software solutions are not the physical 'apparatus'.

Purpose: Recording employee start and end times for work
Expanded_Name: Time clocks for recording employee work hours
Logic_Trace: The NICE Class 9 description includes 'measuring... apparatus and instruments' and 'apparatus and instruments for recording... data'. The parenthetical '[time recording devices]' clarifies the function, directly identifying them as instruments for logging time, typically for employee attendance. General clocks or specific sports timers are less precise than the core 'time recording' function.

Original_Term: Electronic heart rate recorders [other than for medical use]
Purpose: Tracking and logging heart rate data for non-medical purposes, such as fitness or athletic performance
Expanded_Name: Electronic heart rate recorders for non-medical use
Logic_Trace: The NICE Class 9 description covers 'scientific, research... measuring... apparatus and instruments' and 'apparatus and instruments for recording... data'. The term specifies 'Electronic heart rate recorders' and explicitly excludes 'medical use', directing it to non-medical applications like fitness or athletic performance, which fall squarely into Class 9. Medical devices are Class 10, and software is not the apparatus.

Original_Term: Instruments for recording heart activity [for scientific purposes]
Purpose: Monitoring and recording cardiac activity for research and scientific study
Expanded_Name: Scientific instruments for recording heart activity for research purposes
Logic_Trace: The NICE Class 9 description explicitly includes 'Scientific, research... apparatus and instruments' and 'apparatus and instruments for recording... data'. The term specifies '[for scientific purposes]', confirming that these are research instruments, distinct from medical diagnostic devices (Class 10) or consumer fitness trackers (also Class 9 but with a different primary purpose).

Purpose: Measuring and logging pressure data over time
Expanded_Name: Instruments for measuring and recording pressure data
Logic_Trace: The NICE Class 9 description covers 'measuring... apparatus and instruments' and 'apparatus and instruments for recording... data'. 'Pressure recorders' clearly fit this description as devices that measure and record pressure. Devices for applying pressure or specialized audio recorders are not implied by the general term 'pressure recorders' in this context.

Purpose: Measuring and recording speed data
Expanded_Name: Electronic apparatus for measuring and recording speed
Logic_Trace: The class description mentions "measuring, signalling, detecting, testing, inspecting apparatus and instruments" and "apparatus and instruments for recording... data". This strongly supports meaning #1, which describes a physical electronic device for measuring and recording speed. Meanings #2 and #3 are less aligned with the typical understanding of a 'recorder' in this class context.

Purpose: Measuring and recording distances in kilometres
Expanded_Name: Apparatus for measuring and recording distances in kilometres
Logic_Trace: The class description includes "measuring, signalling, detecting, testing, inspecting apparatus and instruments" and "apparatus and instruments for recording... data". This aligns with meaning #1, a device for measuring and recording distance. Meaning #2 (software) is a less direct interpretation of 'recorders' without 'software' being specified. Meaning #3 is encompassed by meaning #1.

Purpose: Measuring speed
Expanded_Name: Apparatus for measuring speed
Logic_Trace: The class description explicitly lists "measuring, signalling, detecting, testing, inspecting apparatus and instruments". This directly confirms meaning #1, referring to physical devices that measure speed. Meaning #2 (software) is less likely for the term 'meters', which implies hardware. Meaning #3 is a specific application falling under meaning #1.

Purpose: Measuring speed
Expanded_Name: Instruments for measuring speed
Logic_Trace: The class description explicitly includes "measuring apparatus and instruments". This directly matches meaning #1, which describes physical devices for measuring speed. Meaning #2 (methodologies) is too abstract for goods. Meaning #3 (software) is a possibility but 'instruments' typically refers to hardware.

Purpose: Measuring temperature
Expanded_Name: Instruments for measuring temperature
Logic_Trace: The class description explicitly includes "measuring apparatus and instruments". This directly aligns with meaning #1, referring to physical devices for measuring temperature. Meaning #2 (software) is less direct for the term 'instruments'. Meaning #3 (methods) is not a good.

Original_Term: Time measuring instruments (not including clocks and watches)

Expanded_Name: Instruments for measuring time, other than clocks and watches
Logic_Trace: The class description explicitly lists "measuring apparatus and instruments" and the specific exclusion of clocks and watches. This confirms meaning #1, focusing on specialized instruments for measuring time that fall outside of general timekeeping devices. Meaning #2 (software) is less likely for 'instruments'. Meaning #3 is too niche and not an active instrument.

Purpose: Recording data over time
Expanded_Name: Electronic apparatus for logging data
Logic_Trace: The class description includes "apparatus and instruments for recording, transmitting, reproducing or processing sound, images or data". This directly matches meaning #1, referring to electronic devices designed to log data. Meaning #2 (software) could be a component but 'data loggers' typically refers to the integrated hardware. Meaning #3 (people) is irrelevant for goods.

Original_Term: Shipping agency services for arranging the transportation of goods
Purpose: Arranging the transportation of goods
Expanded_Name: Shipping agency services for arranging transportation of goods

Meaning #2 describes the action itself, not the arranging service. Meaning #3 is a broader consultancy service that might fall into other classes.

Meaning #2 would be a goods class (e.g., Class 6 or 20). Meaning #3 is very similar to #1, but #1 is more direct for the given term.

Purpose: Loading cargo onto ships
Expanded_Name: Services for loading goods onto ships

Loading goods onto a ship is an essential part of their transportation and handling, confirming meaning #1. Meaning #2 (equipment) would be a goods class. Meaning #3 (management) is a broader service.

Purpose: Transporting passengers or cargo by air

Logic_Trace: The class description explicitly lists "Transport". Air transport refers to the service of moving goods or people by air, directly matching meaning #1. Meaning #2 (aircraft) would be a goods class (Class 12). Meaning #3 (industry) is too broad.

Purpose: Transporting passengers or cargo by water
Expanded_Name: Water transportation services
Logic_Trace: The class description explicitly lists "Transport". Water transport services refer to the service of moving goods or people by water, directly matching meaning #1. Meaning #2 (vessels) would be a goods class (Class 12). Meaning #3 could be related but the term specifically indicates the transport service itself.

Purpose: Loading commercial goods for transport
Expanded_Name: Services for loading freight
Logic_Trace: The class description includes "Transport; packaging and storage of goods". Loading freight is a service directly related to preparing goods for transport or storage, confirming meaning #1. Meaning #2 (equipment) would be a goods class. Meaning #3 (freight) refers to the goods themselves, not the service.

Purpose: Loading goods for transport

Logic_Trace: The class description includes "Transport; packaging and storage of goods". Loading cargo is a service directly related to preparing goods for transport or storage, confirming meaning #1. Meaning #2 (cargo) refers to the goods themselves, not the service. Meaning #3 (systems) would be a goods class.

Logic_Trace: The class description explicitly lists "Tobacco and tobacco substitutes". Chewing tobacco is a direct type of tobacco product, confirming meaning #1. Meaning #2 (act) is not a good. Meaning #3 (plants) would be Class 31.

The term 'Non-medicated lip care preparations' perfectly aligns with meaning #1, as it refers to cosmetic products for lip care, explicitly excluding medicated versions. While they can be used for drawing, their primary classification is as a writing instrument. Industrial tools would be in a different goods class.

Expanded_Name: Services for the production of live performances

Producing live performances falls squarely within providing entertainment services, confirming meaning #1. Meaning #2 is the result of the service. Purpose: Providing entertainment and storytelling through sequential art
Expanded_Name: Printed comic books
Logic_Trace: The class description explicitly lists "printed matter". Comic books are a type of printed matter, confirming meaning #1. Meaning #2 (digital) would be Class 9. Meaning #3 (art style/genre) is not a good.

Purpose: Conducting scientific research and analysis
Expanded_Name: Scientific research and analysis services
Logic_Trace: The class description explicitly lists "Scientific and technological services and research and design relating thereto; industrial analysis, industrial research". This directly confirms meaning #1, referring to professional services in scientific fields. Meaning #2 (instruments) would be Class 9. Meaning #3 (education) would be Class 41.

Purpose: Providing technological research, design, and development
Expanded_Name: Technological research, design and development services
Logic_Trace: The class description explicitly lists "Scientific and technological services and research and design relating thereto; design and development of computer hardware and software". This directly confirms meaning #1, referring to professional services in technological fields. Meaning #2 (products) would be various goods classes. Meaning #3 (maintenance/repair) would be Class 37.

Purpose: Consumption as an alcoholic drink
Expanded_Name: Anise-flavored alcoholic liqueur
Logic_Trace: The class description explicitly lists "Alcoholic beverages, except beers". The term includes '[liqueur]', which directly confirms it's an alcoholic beverage, aligning with meaning #1. Meaning #2 (non-alcoholic) would be Class 32. Meaning #3 (seeds) would be Class 30 or 31.

Purpose: Consumption as a food ingredient or additive
Expanded_Name: Powdered dairy cream for food
Logic_Trace: The class description explicitly lists "milk, cheese, butter, yogurt and other milk products". Powdered cream, being a derivative of cream (a milk product), fits perfectly into meaning #1, assuming it's dairy-based and intended for food consumption, which is consistent with Class 29. Meaning #2 would be Class 30. Meaning #3 would be Class 3.

Purpose: Storing and displaying jewellery
Expanded_Name: Boxes for storing jewellery
Logic_Trace: The class description includes "jewellery". While generic boxes are usually Class 6 or 20, 'jewellery boxes' in Class 14 implies a specific connection to jewellery, often being decorative and valuable themselves, or sold alongside jewellery items, supporting meaning #1. Meanings #2 and #3 would typically fall into other classes.

Purpose: Stamping dates onto documents
Expanded_Name: Handheld date stamps [office requisites]
Logic_Trace: The class description explicitly lists "stationery and office requisites". Date stamps, as physical devices used in offices for marking documents, directly fit meaning #1. Meaning #2 (postage stamps) are printed matter, also Class 16, but 'date stamps' refers to the device. Meaning #3 (digital) would be software (Class 9).

Purpose: Providing definitions and information about words
Expanded_Name: Printed dictionaries
Logic_Trace: The class description explicitly lists "printed matter" and "instructional and teaching materials". Printed dictionaries directly fit meaning #1. Meaning #2 (digital) would be Class 9 or 42 (services). Meaning #3 (field of study) is not a good.

Purpose: Scheduling and recording appointments
Expanded_Name: Printed appointment books
Logic_Trace: The class description explicitly lists "paper and cardboard" and "stationery and office requisites". Printed appointment books directly fit meaning #1 as a type of stationery. Meaning #2 (digital apps) would be Class 9. Meaning #3 (services) would be a different service class.

Purpose: Conveying personal or business contact information
Expanded_Name: Printed visiting cards [business cards]
Logic_Trace: The class description explicitly lists "paper and cardboard", "printed matter", and "stationery and office requisites". Printed visiting cards (business cards) directly fit meaning #1. Meaning #2 (digital) would be software (Class 9). Meaning #3 (playing cards) would be Class 28.

Purpose: Stimulating hair growth for medical purposes
Expanded_Name: Medicinal preparations for stimulating hair growth
Logic_Trace: The class description explicitly lists "Pharmaceuticals, medical and veterinary preparations". The term 'Medicinal' directly indicates a medical purpose, confirming meaning #1 and distinguishing it from non-medicated cosmetic products (Class 3). Meaning #3 (herbal remedies) would fall under this class if they are indeed medicinal.

Purpose: Providing warmth and covering as an outer garment
Expanded_Name: Overcoats [clothing]
Logic_Trace: The class description explicitly lists "Clothing, footwear, headwear". Overcoats as an item of clothing perfectly match meaning #1. Meaning #2 (protective coatings) would be Class 1 or 2. Meaning #3 (paint/finish) would be Class 2.

Purpose: Writing, recording notes, or keeping diaries
Expanded_Name: Blank journals for writing
Logic_Trace: Class 16 description ("Paper and cardboard; printed matter; stationery and office requisites") supports both periodical publications and blank books for writing. Financial ledgers also fit as stationery. Given 'Periodicals' is a separate term in the input for Class 16, 'Journals' in this context is more likely referring to blank books for writing, distinguishing it from general 'periodicals'.

Purpose: Prepaying postage for mail services
Expanded_Name: Printed postage stamps
Logic_Trace: Class 16 description ("Printed matter; stationery") directly supports the meaning of adhesive labels for mail (meaning 1). Rubber stamps (meaning 2) typically fall under Class 8 (hand tools) or Class 16 (for stamp pads, but not the stamp itself). Collectible items (meaning 3) describe a use, but the item itself is the stamp.

Purpose: Transporting personal effects
Expanded_Name: Handbags for gentlemen
Logic_Trace: Class 18 description ("Luggage and carrying bags") directly aligns with meaning 1 (bags carried by men for personal items). Meanings 2 and 3 describe specific styles or retail contexts, but the core product is a carrying bag.

Purpose: Disseminating information on various subjects at regular intervals
Expanded_Name: Printed periodical publications
Logic_Trace: Class 16 description ("Printed matter") supports all meanings. Given 'Journals' was interpreted as blank books, 'Periodicals' here refers to regularly published printed materials like magazines, newsletters, and general academic journals.

Purpose: Wearing during sports activities or as team identification
Expanded_Name: Sports jerseys [clothing]
Logic_Trace: Class 25 description ("Clothing, footwear, headwear") directly supports meaning 1 (garments worn by athletes) under 'Clothing'. Meaning 2 (fabric) would be Class 24. Meaning 3 (souvenir apparel) describes a purpose, but the item itself is clothing.

Purpose: Reporting current events and information daily or weekly
Expanded_Name: Printed newspapers
Logic_Trace: Class 16 description ("Printed matter") directly supports meaning 1 (daily or weekly printed publications). Meaning 2 (digital platforms) would be Class 38 or 42. Meaning 3 (newsprint paper) would be a raw material.

Purpose: Containing and protecting documents for mailing or storage
Expanded_Name: Paper envelopes for stationery purposes
Logic_Trace: Class 16 description ("Paper and cardboard; stationery"). The explicit term "[stationery]" confirms meaning 1 (paper pouches for mailing) as the correct interpretation, falling directly under 'stationery'. Meanings 2 and 3 are irrelevant.

Purpose: Transporting documents and personal items, typically for business
Expanded_Name: Attaché cases [carrying bags]
Logic_Trace: Class 18 description ("Luggage and carrying bags") directly supports meaning 1 (rigid rectangular carrying cases for documents), which is the precise definition of an attaché case and falls under 'carrying bags'.

Purpose: Wearing as swimwear or underwear
Expanded_Name: Trunks [clothing]
Logic_Trace: Class 25 description ("Clothing, footwear, headwear") immediately rejects meanings 1 (which would be Class 18 or 20) and 3. Meaning 2 (swim shorts or underwear) fits perfectly under 'Clothing'.

Purpose: Linking data processing equipment for signal transmission
Expanded_Name: Couplers for data processing equipment
Logic_Trace: Class 9 description ("Apparatus and instruments for recording, transmitting, reproducing or processing sound, images or data; computers and computer peripheral devices"). The explicit term "[data processing equipment]" confirms meaning 1 (devices for connecting electrical or data lines) for data processing purposes. Meanings 2 and 3 are mechanical and not related to data processing.

Purpose: Providing furniture for temporary use
Expanded_Name: Rental services for furniture
Logic_Trace: Class 43 description ("Services for providing food and drink; temporary accommodation"). While furniture itself is Class 20, the rental of furniture is a service related to temporary arrangements, events, or accommodation, fitting the scope of Class 43.

Purpose: Providing chairs for temporary interior use
Expanded_Name: Hiring services for interior chairs
Logic_Trace: Class 43 description ("Services for providing food and drink; temporary accommodation"). Similar to 'Rental of furniture', the hire of chairs is a service for temporary provision, aligning with Class 43's focus on temporary setups for events or accommodation.

Original_Term: Rental of furniture, linens and table settings
Purpose: Providing furniture, linens, and table settings for temporary use
Expanded_Name: Rental services for furniture, linens, and table settings
Logic_Trace: Class 43 description ("Services for providing food and drink; temporary accommodation"). This service involves the temporary provision of items for events or accommodation, fitting directly within the scope of Class 43.

Purpose: Providing chairs and tables for temporary use
Expanded_Name: Rental services for chairs and tables
Logic_Trace: Class 43 description ("Services for providing food and drink; temporary accommodation"). This is another service for the temporary provision of items, fitting directly into the context of temporary setups for events or accommodation under Class 43.

Purpose: Serving as commemorative items or non-monetary currency substitutes with aesthetic or collectible value
Expanded_Name: Copper tokens [collectible or decorative]
Logic_Trace: Class 14 description ("Precious metals and their alloys; jewellery, precious and semi-precious stones..."). Copper is not a precious metal. However, other entries in Class 14 like 'Monetary coin sets for collecting purposes' and 'Metal tokens used for mass transit' indicate that tokens, if intended for collection, commemoration, or as items of decorative value (even if not strictly precious metal), can be classified here. Thus, these copper tokens are interpreted as collectible or decorative articles, aligning them with the broader scope of Class 14 beyond just precious metals.

Original_Term: Monetary coin sets for collecting purposes
Purpose: Collecting and display as valuable articles
Expanded_Name: Monetary coin sets for collecting purposes
Logic_Trace: Class 14 description ("Precious metals and their alloys; jewellery..."). Monetary coin sets, especially for collecting, often have intrinsic value due to their material (e.g., precious metals) or rarity, aligning with the 'precious' and 'jewellery' aspects of the class or as valued collectible items within this category.

Original_Term: Metal tokens used for mass transit
Purpose: Serving as payment or access for mass transit, specifically interpreted as collectible or commemorative articles within Class 14
Expanded_Name: Metal tokens for mass transit [collectible or commemorative]
Logic_Trace: Class 14 description ("Precious metals and their alloys; jewellery..."). While utilitarian transit tokens are typically Class 6 or 9, their presence in Class 14, similar to 'Copper tokens' and 'Monetary coin sets', implies they are categorized here for their collectible, commemorative, or decorative value, aligning them with items having a 'precious' or 'jewellery-like' context.

Purpose: Providing temporary parking spaces for vehicles
Expanded_Name: Rental services for parking places
Logic_Trace: Class 39 description ("Transport; packaging and storage of goods; travel arrangement"). Providing parking is a direct service related to 'transport' and the temporary 'storage' of vehicles.

Original_Term: Provision of access to an electronic on-line network for information retrieval
Purpose: Enabling connection to an online network for retrieving information
Expanded_Name: Provision of access to an electronic online network for information retrieval [telecommunications services]
Logic_Trace: Class 38 description ("Telecommunications services"). Providing access to any online network for information retrieval is a fundamental telecommunications service.

Purpose: Enabling remote users to access and retrieve information from databases
Expanded_Name: Providing telecommunications access to databases
Logic_Trace: Class 38 description ("Telecommunications services"). Providing remote access to databases involves the transmission and reception of data over a network, which is a core telecommunications service. Selling software (meaning 2) would be Class 9, and development (meaning 3) Class 42.

Purpose: Filtering and purifying water
Expanded_Name: Water purifying apparatus
Logic_Trace: Class 11 description ("Apparatus and installations for lighting, heating, cooling, steam generating, cooking, drying, ventilating, water supply and sanitary purposes"). 'Water purifying apparatus' directly fits the 'water supply and sanitary purposes' aspect of Class 11. Meaning 2 (chemicals) would be Class 1 or 5. Meaning 3 (services) would be Class 37, 40, or 42.

Purpose: Use for medical, sanitary, or therapeutic purposes (e.g., balneotherapy)
Expanded_Name: Thermal water for medical or therapeutic purposes
Logic_Trace: Class 5 description ("Pharmaceuticals, medical and veterinary preparations; sanitary preparations for medical purposes..."). 'Thermal water' in Class 5 refers to water with therapeutic or medicinal properties, often used in spas or for health purposes, aligning with 'sanitary preparations for medical purposes' or general well-being within the class.

Original_Term: Key fobs in precious metals or coated therewith
Purpose: Holding keys and serving as personal accessories
Expanded_Name: Key fobs made of precious metals or coated therewith
Logic_Trace: Class 14 description ("Precious metals and their alloys; jewellery, precious and semi-precious stones..."). The term explicitly states 'in precious metals or coated therewith', placing it directly in Class 14 as jewellery or articles made of/coated with precious metals.

Purpose: Holding keys and serving as personal accessories
Expanded_Name: Silver key fobs
Logic_Trace: Class 14 description ("Precious metals and their alloys; jewellery..."). Silver is a precious metal, so key fobs made of silver fall directly into Class 14 as articles of precious metal.

Original_Term: Key fobs made of leather or leather imitations
Nature: Decorative articles of leather or imitation leather, considered imitation jewellery or incorporating precious metal components
Purpose: Holding keys and serving as personal accessories with decorative or aesthetic value
Expanded_Name: Key fobs made of leather or leather imitations [imitation jewellery]
Logic_Trace: Class 14 description ("Precious metals and their alloys; jewellery, precious and semi-precious stones..."). Key fobs made solely of leather or imitations typically belong to Class 18. For these to be in Class 14, they must be interpreted as key fobs that *incorporate* or *are coated with* precious metals, or are designed as *imitation jewellery* falling under the scope of Class 14 despite their primary material. This interpretation is necessary to align with the assigned class.

Purpose: Wearing for physical exercise and athletic activities
Expanded_Name: Gym suits [clothing]
Logic_Trace: Class 25 description ("Clothing, footwear, headwear") directly supports meaning 1 (athletic clothing). Meanings 2 and 3 are irrelevant or misinterpretations of the term in this context.

Purpose: Managing and facilitating import operations on behalf of others
